3_10_13.NRSVCE: you shall eat it in a holy place, because it is your due and your sons’ due, from the offerings by fire to the LORD; for so I am commanded.

3_10_14.NKJV: The breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering you shall eat in a clean place, you, your sons, and your daughters with you; for they are your due and your sons’ due, which are given from the sacrifices of peace offerings of the children of Israel.
3_10_14.KJV: And the wave breast and heave shoulder shall ye eat in a clean place; thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee: for they be thy due, and thy sons' due, which are given out of the sacrifices of peace offerings of the children of Israel.
3_10_14.ESV: But the breast that is waved and the thigh that is contributed you shall eat in a clean place, you and your sons and your daughters with you, for they are given as your due and your sons' due from the sacrifices of the peace offerings of the people of Israel.
3_10_14.NASB: 'The breast of the wave offering, however, and the thigh of the offering you may eat in a clean place, you and your sons and your daughters with you; for they have been given as your due and your sons' due out of the sacrifices of the peace offerings of the sons of Israel.
3_10_14.NLT: But the breast and thigh that were lifted up as a special offering may be eaten in any place that is ceremonially clean. These parts have been given to you and your descendants as your portion of the peace offerings presented by the people of Israel.
3_10_14.WEB: The waved breast and the heaved thigh you shall eat in a clean place, you, and your sons, and your daughters with you: for they are given as your portion, and your sons’ portion, out of the sacrifices of the peace offerings of the children of Israel.
3_10_14.CSB17: But you and your sons and your daughters may eat the breast of the presentation offering and the thigh of the contribution in any ceremonially clean place, because these portions have been assigned to you and your children from the Israelites’ fellowship sacrifices.
3_10_14.AMP: But the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the heave offering you may eat in a clean place, you and your sons and daughters with you; for the breast and the thigh are your portion and your sons' portion, given out of the sacrifices of the peace offerings of the Israelites.
3_10_14.MSG: Also, you and your sons and daughters are to eat the breast of the Wave-Offering and the thigh of the Contribution-Offering in a clean place. They are provided as your portion and the portion of your children from the Peace-Offerings presented by the People of Israel.
3_10_14.NIV: But you and your sons and your daughters may eat the breast that was waved and the thigh that was presented. Eat them in a ceremonially clean place; they have been given to you and your children as your share of the Israelites’ fellowship offerings.
3_10_14.NRSVCE: But the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is raised, you and your sons and daughters as well may eat in any clean place; for they have been assigned to you and your children from the sacrifices of the offerings of well-being of the people of Israel.

3_10_15.NKJV: The thigh of the heave offering and the breast of the wave offering they shall bring with the offerings of fat made by fire, to offer as a wave offering before the LORD. And it shall be yours and your sons’ with you, by a statute forever, as the LORD has commanded.”
3_10_15.KJV: The heave shoulder and the wave breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave offering before the LORD; and it shall be thine, and thy sons' with thee, by a statute for ever; as the LORD hath commanded.
3_10_15.ESV: The thigh that is contributed and the breast that is waved they shall bring with the food offerings of the fat pieces to wave for a wave offering before the LORD, and it shall be yours and your sons' with you as a due forever, as the LORD has commanded.”
3_10_15.NASB: 'The thigh offered by lifting up and the breast offered by waving they shall bring along with the offerings by fire of the portions of fat, to present as a wave offering before the LORD; so it shall be a thing perpetually due you and your sons with you, just as the LORD has commanded.'
3_10_15.NLT: You must lift up the thigh and breast as a special offering to the LORD, along with the fat of the special gifts. These parts will belong to you and your descendants as your permanent right, just as the LORD has commanded.”
3_10_15.WEB: The heaved thigh and the waved breast they shall bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave offering before Yahweh: and it shall be yours, and your sons’ with you, as a portion forever; as Yahweh has commanded.”
3_10_15.CSB17: They are to bring the thigh of the contribution and the breast of the presentation offering, together with the offerings of fat portions made by fire, to present as a presentation offering before the LORD. It will belong permanently to you and your children, as the LORD commanded.”
3_10_15.AMP: They shall bring the thigh presented by lifting up and the breast presented by waving, along with the offerings by fire of the fat, to present as a wave offering before the LORD. This shall be yours and your sons' with you, as your perpetual portion, just as the LORD has commanded."
3_10_15.MSG: Bring the thigh of the Contribution-Offering and the breast of the Wave-Offering and the fat pieces of the Fire-Gifts and lift them up as a Wave-Offering. This will be the regular share for you and your children as ordered by God."
3_10_15.NIV: The thigh that was presented and the breast that was waved must be brought with the fat portions of the offerings made by fire, to be waved before the Lord as a wave offering. This will be the regular share for you and your children, as the Lord has commanded.”
3_10_15.NRSVCE: The thigh that is raised and the breast that is elevated they shall bring, together with the offerings by fire of the fat, to raise for an elevation offering before the LORD; they are to be your due and that of your children forever, as the LORD has commanded.

3_10_17.NKJV: “Why have you not eaten the sin offering in a holy place, since it is most holy, and God has given it to you to bear the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?
3_10_17.KJV: Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin offering in the holy place, seeing it is most holy, and God hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?
3_10_17.ESV: “Why have you not eaten the sin offering in the place of the sanctuary, since it is a thing most holy and has been given to you that you may bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD?
3_10_17.NASB: 'Why did you not eat the sin offering at the holy place? For it is most holy, and He gave it to you to bear away the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD.
3_10_17.NLT: “Why didn’t you eat the sin offering in the sacred area?” he demanded. “It is a holy offering! The LORD has given it to you to remove the guilt of the community and to purify the people, making them right with the LORD.
3_10_17.WEB: “Why haven’t you eaten the sin offering in the place of the sanctuary, since it is most holy, and he has given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before Yahweh?
3_10_17.CSB17: “Why didn’t you eat the sin offering in the sanctuary area? For it is especially holy, and he has assigned it to you to take away the guilt of the community and make atonement for them before the LORD.
3_10_17.AMP: "Why did you not eat the sin offering in the holy place? For it is most holy; and God gave it to you to remove the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the LORD.
3_10_17.MSG: "Why didn't you eat the Absolution-Offering in the Holy Place since it is most holy? The offering was given to you for taking away the guilt of the community by making atonement for them before God.
3_10_17.NIV: “Why didn’t you eat the sin offering in the sanctuary area? It is most holy; it was given to you to take away the guilt of the community by making atonement for them before the Lord.
3_10_17.NRSVCE: “Why did you not eat the sin offering in the sacred area? For it is most holy, and God has given it to you that you may remove the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement on their behalf before the LORD.

3_10_18.NKJV: See! Its blood was not brought inside the holy place; indeed you should have eaten it in a holy place, as I commanded.”

3_10_18.NRSVCE: Its blood was not brought into the inner part of the sanctuary. You should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as I commanded.”

3_10_19.NKJV: And Aaron said to Moses, “Look, this day they have offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, and such things have befallen me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been accepted in the sight of the LORD?”
3_10_19.KJV: And Aaron said unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD; and such things have befallen me: and if I had eaten the sin offering to day, should it have been accepted in the sight of the LORD?
3_10_19.ESV: And Aaron said to Moses, “Behold, today they have offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, and yet such things as these have happened to me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would the LORD have approved?”
3_10_19.NASB: But Aaron spoke to Moses, 'Behold, this very day they presented their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD. When things like these happened to me, if I had eaten a sin offering today, would it have been good in the sight of the LORD?'
3_10_19.NLT: Then Aaron answered Moses, “Today my sons presented both their sin offering and their burnt offering to the LORD. And yet this tragedy has happened to me. If I had eaten the people’s sin offering on such a tragic day as this, would the LORD have been pleased?”
3_10_19.WEB: Aaron spoke to Moses, “Behold, today they have offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before Yahweh; and such things as these have happened to me. If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been pleasing in Yahweh’s sight?”
3_10_19.CSB17: But Aaron replied to Moses, “See, today they presented their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD. Since these things have happened to me, if I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been acceptable in the LORD’s sight?”
3_10_19.AMP: Then Aaron said to Moses, "This very day they have [obediently] presented their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD, but [such terrible things] as these have happened to me [and to them]; if I [and my sons] had eaten a sin offering today would it have been acceptable and pleasing in the sight of the LORD?"
3_10_19.MSG: Aaron replied to Moses, "Look. They sacrificed their Absolution-Offering and Whole-Burnt-Offering before God today, and you see what has happened to me—I've lost two sons. Do you think God would have been pleased if I had gone ahead and eaten the Absolution-Offering today?"
3_10_19.NIV: Aaron replied to Moses, “Today they sacrificed their sin offering and their burnt offering before the Lord, but such things as this have happened to me. Would the Lord have been pleased if I had eaten the sin offering today?”
3_10_19.NRSVCE: And Aaron spoke to Moses, “See, today they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the LORD; and yet such things as these have befallen me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been agreeable to the LORD?”

3_11_8.NRSVCE: Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch; they are unclean for you.

3_11_9.NKJV: ‘These you may eat of all that are in the water: whatever in the water has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the rivers—that you may eat.
3_11_9.KJV: These shall ye eat of all that are in the waters: whatsoever hath fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, them shall ye eat.
3_11_9.ESV: “These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the rivers, you may eat.
3_11_9.NASB: 'These you may eat, whatever is in the water: all that have fins and scales, those in the water, in the seas or in the rivers, you may eat.
3_11_9.NLT: “Of all the marine animals, these are ones you may use for food. You may eat anything from the water if it has both fins and scales, whether taken from salt water or from streams.
3_11_9.WEB: “‘These you may eat of all that are in the waters: whatever has fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, that you may eat.
3_11_9.CSB17: “This is what you may eat from all that is in the water: You may eat everything in the water that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or streams.
3_11_9.AMP: 'These you may eat, whatever is in the water: whatever has fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, these you may eat;
3_11_9.MSG: "Among the creatures that live in the water of the seas and streams, you may eat any that have fins and scales.
3_11_9.NIV: “‘Of all the creatures living in the water of the seas and the streams, you may eat any that have fins and scales.
3_11_9.NRSVCE: These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the streams — such you may eat.

3_11_10.NKJV: But all in the seas or in the rivers that do not have fins and scales, all that move in the water or any living thing which is in the water, they are an abomination to you.
3_11_10.KJV: And all that have not fins and scales in the seas, and in the rivers, of all that move in the waters, and of any living thing which is in the waters, they shall be an abomination unto you:
3_11_10.ESV: But anything in the seas or the rivers that does not have fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and of the living creatures that are in the waters, is detestable to you.
3_11_10.NASB: 'But whatever is in the seas and in the rivers that does not have fins and scales among all the teeming life of the water, and among all the living creatures that are in the water, they are detestable things to you,
3_11_10.NLT: But you must never eat animals from the sea or from rivers that do not have both fins and scales. They are detestable to you. This applies both to little creatures that live in shallow water and to all creatures that live in deep water.
3_11_10.WEB: All that don’t have fins and scales in the seas, and in the rivers, of all that move in the waters, and of all the living creatures that are in the waters, they are an abomination to you,
3_11_10.CSB17: But these are to be abhorrent to you: everything in the seas or streams that does not have fins and scales among all the swarming things and other living creatures in the water.
3_11_10.AMP: but whatever does not have fins and scales in the seas and in the rivers, of all the teeming life in the waters, and of all the living creatures that are in the waters, they are [to be considered] detestable to you.
3_11_10.MSG: But anything that doesn't have fins and scales, whether in seas or streams, whether small creatures in the shallows or huge creatures in the deeps, you are to detest.
3_11_10.NIV: But all creatures in the seas or streams that do not have fins and scales—whether among all the swarming things or among all the other living creatures in the water—you are to detest.
3_11_10.NRSVCE: But anything in the seas or the streams that does not have fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and among all the other living creatures that are in the waters — they are detestable to you

3_11_11.NKJV: They shall be an abomination to you; you shall not eat their flesh, but you shall regard their carcasses as an abomination.
3_11_11.KJV: They shall be even an abomination unto you; ye shall not eat of their flesh, but ye shall have their carcases in abomination.
3_11_11.ESV: You shall regard them as detestable; you shall not eat any of their flesh, and you shall detest their carcasses.
3_11_11.NASB: and they shall be abhorrent to you; you may not eat of their flesh, and their carcasses you shall detest.
3_11_11.NLT: They will always be detestable to you. You must never eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies.
3_11_11.WEB: and you detest them. You shall not eat of their meat, and you shall detest their carcasses.
3_11_11.CSB17: They are to remain abhorrent to you; you must not eat any of their meat, and you must abhor their carcasses.
3_11_11.AMP: They shall be hated things to you. You may not eat their meat; you shall detest their carcasses.
3_11_11.MSG: Yes, detest them. Don't eat their meat; detest their carcasses.
3_11_11.NIV: And since you are to detest them, you must not eat their meat and you must detest their carcasses.
3_11_11.NRSVCE: and detestable they shall remain. Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall regard as detestable.

3_11_12.NKJV: Whatever in the water does not have fins or scales—that shall be an abomination to you.
3_11_12.KJV: Whatsoever hath no fins nor scales in the waters, that shall be an abomination unto you.
3_11_12.ESV: Everything in the waters that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
3_11_12.NASB: 'Whatever in the water does not have fins and scales is abhorrent to you.
3_11_12.NLT: Any marine animal that does not have both fins and scales is detestable to you.
3_11_12.WEB: Whatever has no fins nor scales in the waters, that is an abomination to you.
3_11_12.CSB17: Everything in the water that does not have fins and scales will be abhorrent to you.
3_11_12.AMP: Everything in the water that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
3_11_12.MSG: Anything living in the water that doesn't have fins and scales is detestable to you.
3_11_12.NIV: Anything living in the water that does not have fins and scales is to be detestable to you.
3_11_12.NRSVCE: Everything in the waters that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.

3_11_13.NKJV: ‘And these you shall regard as an abomination among the birds; they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: the eagle, the vulture, the buzzard,
3_11_13.KJV: And these are they which ye shall have in abomination among the fowls; they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the ospray,
3_11_13.ESV: “And these you shall detest among the birds; they shall not be eaten; they are detestable: the eagle, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
3_11_13.NASB: 'These, moreover, you shall detest among the birds; they are abhorrent, not to be eaten: the eagle and the vulture and the buzzard,
3_11_13.NLT: “These are the birds that are detestable to you. You must never eat them: the griffon vulture, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
3_11_13.WEB: “‘These you shall detest among the birds; they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: the eagle, and the vulture, and the black vulture,
3_11_13.CSB17: “You are to abhor these birds. They must not be eaten because they are abhorrent: eagles, bearded vultures, Egyptian vultures,
3_11_13.AMP: 'These you shall detest among the birds; they are not to be eaten, for they are hated things: the eagle and the vulture and the buzzard,
3_11_13.MSG: "These are the birds you are to detest. Don't eat them. They are detestable: eagle, vulture, osprey,
3_11_13.NIV: “‘These are the birds you are to detest and not eat because they are detestable: the eagle, the vulture, the black vulture,
3_11_13.NRSVCE: These you shall regard as detestable among the birds. They shall not be eaten; they are an abomination: the eagle, the vulture, the osprey,

3_11_14.NKJV: the kite, and the falcon after its kind;
3_11_14.KJV: And the vulture, and the kite after his kind;
3_11_14.ESV: the kite, the falcon of any kind,
3_11_14.NASB: and the kite and the falcon in its kind,
3_11_14.NLT: the kite, falcons of all kinds,
3_11_14.WEB: and the red kite, any kind of black kite,
3_11_14.AMP: the kite, every kind of falcon,
3_11_14.NIV: the red kite, any kind of black kite,
3_11_14.NRSVCE: the buzzard, the kite of any kind;

3_11_16.NKJV: the ostrich, the short-eared owl, the sea gull, and the hawk after its kind;
3_11_16.KJV: And the owl , and the night hawk, and the cuckow, and the hawk after his kind,
3_11_16.ESV: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind,
3_11_16.NASB: and the ostrich and the owl and the sea gull and the hawk in its kind,
3_11_16.NLT: the eagle owl, the short-eared owl, the seagull, hawks of all kinds,
3_11_16.WEB: the horned owl, the screech owl, and the gull, any kind of hawk,
3_11_16.CSB17: ostriches, short-eared owls, gulls, any kind of hawk,

3_11_16.NIV: the horned owl, the screech owl, the gull, any kind of hawk,
3_11_16.NRSVCE: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;

3_11_17.NKJV: the little owl, the fisher owl, and the screech owl;
3_11_17.KJV: And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl,
3_11_17.ESV: the little owl, the cormorant, the short-eared owl,
3_11_17.NASB: and the little owl and the cormorant and the great owl,
3_11_17.NLT: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
3_11_17.WEB: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
3_11_17.AMP: the little owl and the cormorant and the great owl,
3_11_17.NIV: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
3_11_17.NRSVCE: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,

3_11_18.NKJV: the white owl, the jackdaw, and the carrion vulture;
3_11_18.KJV: And the swan, and the pelican, and the gier eagle,
3_11_18.ESV: the barn owl, the tawny owl, the carrion vulture,
3_11_18.NASB: and the white owl and the pelican and the carrion vulture,
3_11_18.NLT: the barn owl, the desert owl, the Egyptian vulture,
3_11_18.WEB: the white owl, the desert owl, the osprey,
3_11_18.AMP: the white owl, the pelican, the carrion vulture,
3_11_18.NIV: the white owl, the desert owl, the osprey,
3_11_18.NRSVCE: the water hen, the desert owl, the carrion vulture,

3_11_19.NKJV: the stork, the heron after its kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.KJV: And the stork, the heron after her kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.
3_11_19.ESV: the stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.NASB: and the stork, the heron in its kinds, and the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.NLT: the stork, herons of all kinds, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.WEB: the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.CSB17: storks, any kind of heron, hoopoes, and bats.
3_11_19.AMP: the stork, all kinds of heron, the hoopoe, and the bat.
3_11_19.NIV: the stork, any kind of heron, the hoopoe and the bat.
3_11_19.NRSVCE: the stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.

3_11_20.NKJV: ‘All flying insects that creep on all fours shall be an abomination to you.
3_11_20.KJV: All fowls that creep, going upon all four, shall be an abomination unto you.
3_11_20.ESV: “All winged insects that go on all fours are detestable to you.
3_11_20.NASB: 'All the winged insects that walk on [all] fours are detestable to you.
3_11_20.NLT: “You must not eat winged insects that walk along the ground; they are detestable to you.
3_11_20.WEB: “‘All flying insects that walk on all fours are an abomination to you.
3_11_20.CSB17: “All winged insects that walk on all fours are to be abhorrent to you.
3_11_20.AMP: 'All winged insects that walk on all fours are detestable to you;
3_11_20.MSG: "All flying insects that walk on all fours are detestable to you.
3_11_20.NIV: “‘All flying insects that walk on all fours are to be detestable to you.
3_11_20.NRSVCE: All winged insects that walk upon all fours are detestable to you.

3_11_21.NKJV: Yet these you may eat of every flying insect that creeps on all fours: those which have jointed legs above their feet with which to leap on the earth.
3_11_21.KJV: Yet these may ye eat of every flying creeping thing that goeth upon all four, which have legs above their feet, to leap withal upon the earth;
3_11_21.ESV: Yet among the winged insects that go on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to hop on the ground.
3_11_21.NASB: 'Yet these you may eat among all the winged insects which walk on [all] fours: those which have above their feet jointed legs with which to jump on the earth.
3_11_21.NLT: You may, however, eat winged insects that walk along the ground and have jointed legs so they can jump.
3_11_21.WEB: Yet you may eat these: of all winged creeping things that go on all fours, which have legs above their feet, with which to hop on the earth.
3_11_21.CSB17: But you may eat these kinds of all the winged insects that walk on all fours: those that have jointed legs above their feet for hopping on the ground.
3_11_21.AMP: yet of all winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those which have legs above their feet with which to leap on the ground.
3_11_21.MSG: But you can eat some of these, namely, those that have jointed legs for hopping on the ground:
3_11_21.NIV: There are, however, some winged creatures that walk on all fours that you may eat: those that have jointed legs for hopping on the ground.
3_11_21.NRSVCE: But among the winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to leap on the ground.

3_11_22.NKJV: These you may eat: the locust after its kind, the destroying locust after its kind, the cricket after its kind, and the grasshopper after its kind.
3_11_22.KJV: Even these of them ye may eat; the locust after his kind, and the bald locust after his kind, and the beetle after his kind, and the grasshopper after his kind.
3_11_22.ESV: Of them you may eat: the locust of any kind, the bald locust of any kind, the cricket of any kind, and the grasshopper of any kind.
3_11_22.NASB: 'These of them you may eat: the locust in its kinds, and the devastating locust in its kinds, and the cricket in its kinds, and the grasshopper in its kinds.
3_11_22.NLT: The insects you are permitted to eat include all kinds of locusts, bald locusts, crickets, and grasshoppers.
3_11_22.WEB: Even of these you may eat: any kind of locust, any kind of katydid, any kind of cricket, and any kind of grasshopper.
3_11_22.CSB17: You may eat these: any kind of locust, katydid, cricket, and grasshopper.
3_11_22.AMP: Of these you may eat: the whole species of migratory locust, of bald locust, of cricket, and of grasshopper.
3_11_22.MSG: all locusts, katydids, crickets, and grasshoppers.
3_11_22.NIV: Of these you may eat any kind of locust, katydid, cricket or grasshopper.
3_11_22.NRSVCE: Of them you may eat: the locust according to its kind, the bald locust according to its kind, the cricket according to its kind, and the grasshopper according to its kind.

3_11_23.NKJV: But all other flying insects which have four feet shall be an abomination to you.
3_11_23.KJV: But all other flying creeping things, which have four feet, shall be an abomination unto you.
3_11_23.ESV: But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
3_11_23.NASB: 'But all other winged insects which are four-footed are detestable to you.
3_11_23.NLT: All other winged insects that walk along the ground are detestable to you.
3_11_23.WEB: But all winged creeping things which have four feet, are an abomination to you.
3_11_23.CSB17: All other winged insects that have four feet are to be abhorrent to you.
3_11_23.AMP: But all other winged insects which are four footed are detestable to you.
3_11_23.MSG: But all the other flying insects that have four legs you are to detest.
3_11_23.NIV: But all other winged creatures that have four legs you are to detest.
3_11_23.NRSVCE: But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.

3_11_24.NKJV: ‘By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until evening;
3_11_24.KJV: And for these ye shall be unclean: whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the even.
3_11_24.ESV: “And by these you shall become unclean. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
3_11_24.NASB: 'By these, moreover, you will be made unclean: whoever touches their carcasses becomes unclean until evening,
3_11_24.NLT: “The following creatures will make you ceremonially unclean. If any of you touch their carcasses, you will be defiled until evening.
3_11_24.WEB: “‘By these you will become unclean: whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening.
3_11_24.CSB17: “These will make you unclean. Whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening,
3_11_24.AMP: 'By [contact with] these you will become unclean; whoever touches their carcasses becomes unclean until the evening (dusk),
3_11_24.MSG: "You will make yourselves ritually unclean until evening if you touch their carcasses.
3_11_24.NIV: “‘You will make yourselves unclean by these; whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean till evening.
3_11_24.NRSVCE: By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until the evening,

3_11_25.NKJV: whoever carries part of the carcass of any of them shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening:
3_11_25.KJV: And whosoever beareth ought of the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
3_11_25.ESV: and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_25.NASB: and whoever picks up any of their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
3_11_25.NLT: If you pick up their carcasses, you must wash your clothes, and you will remain defiled until evening.
3_11_25.WEB: Whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_25.CSB17: and whoever carries any of their carcasses is to wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening.
3_11_25.AMP: and whoever picks up any of their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_25.MSG: If you pick up one of their carcasses you must wash your clothes and you'll be unclean until evening.
3_11_25.NIV: Whoever picks up one of their carcasses must wash his clothes, and he will be unclean till evening.
3_11_25.NRSVCE: and whoever carries any part of the carcass of any of them shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.

3_11_26.NKJV: The carcass of any animal which divides the foot, but is not cloven-hoofed or does not chew the cud, is unclean to you. Everyone who touches it shall be unclean.
3_11_26.KJV: The carcases of every beast which divideth the hoof, and is not clovenfooted , nor cheweth the cud, are unclean unto you: every one that toucheth them shall be unclean.
3_11_26.ESV: Every animal that parts the hoof but is not cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you. Everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
3_11_26.NASB: 'Concerning all the animals which divide the hoof but do not make a split [hoof], or which do not chew cud, they are unclean to you: whoever touches them becomes unclean.
3_11_26.NLT: “Any animal that has split hooves that are not evenly divided or that does not chew the cud is unclean for you. If you touch the carcass of such an animal, you will be defiled.
3_11_26.WEB: “‘Every animal which parts the hoof, and is not cloven-footed, nor chews the cud, is unclean to you. Everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
3_11_26.CSB17: All animals that have hooves but do not have a divided hoof and do not chew the cud are unclean for you. Whoever touches them becomes unclean.
3_11_26.AMP: Concerning all the animals which divide the hoof, but do not have a split hoof, or which do not chew the cud, they are unclean to you; whoever touches them becomes unclean.
3_11_26.MSG: "Every animal that has a split hoof that's not completely divided, or that doesn't chew the cud is unclean for you; if you touch the carcass of any of them you become unclean.
3_11_26.NIV: “‘Every animal that has a split hoof not completely divided or that does not chew the cud is unclean for you; whoever touches the carcass of any of them will be unclean.
3_11_26.NRSVCE: Every animal that has divided hoofs but is not cleft-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean for you; everyone who touches one of them shall be unclean.

3_11_27.NKJV: And whatever goes on its paws, among all kinds of animals that go on all fours, those are unclean to you. Whoever touches any such carcass shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_27.KJV: And whatsoever goeth upon his paws, among all manner of beasts that go on all four, those are unclean unto you: whoso toucheth their carcase shall be unclean until the even.
3_11_27.ESV: And all that walk on their paws, among the animals that go on all fours, are unclean to you. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
3_11_27.NASB: 'Also whatever walks on its paws, among all the creatures that walk on [all] fours, are unclean to you; whoever touches their carcasses becomes unclean until evening,
3_11_27.NLT: Of the animals that walk on all fours, those that have paws are unclean. If you touch the carcass of such an animal, you will be defiled until evening.
3_11_27.WEB: Whatever goes on its paws, among all animals that go on all fours, they are unclean to you. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening.
3_11_27.CSB17: All the four-footed animals that walk on their paws are unclean for you. Whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening,
3_11_27.AMP: Also all animals that walk on their paws, among all kinds of animals that walk on four legs, are unclean to you; whoever touches their carcasses becomes unclean until the evening,
3_11_27.MSG: "Every four-footed animal that goes on its paws is unclean for you; if you touch its carcass you are unclean until evening.
3_11_27.NIV: Of all the animals that walk on all fours, those that walk on their paws are unclean for you; whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean till evening.
3_11_27.NRSVCE: All that walk on their paws, among the animals that walk on all fours, are unclean for you; whoever touches the carcass of any of them shall be unclean until the evening,

3_11_28.NKJV: Whoever carries any such carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. It is unclean to you.
3_11_28.KJV: And he that beareth the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: they are unclean unto you.
3_11_28.ESV: and he who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
3_11_28.NASB: and the one who picks up their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening; they are unclean to you.
3_11_28.NLT: If you pick up its carcass, you must wash your clothes, and you will remain defiled until evening. These animals are unclean for you.
3_11_28.WEB: He who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening. They are unclean to you.
3_11_28.CSB17: and anyone who carries their carcasses is to wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.
3_11_28.AMP: and the one who picks up their carcasses shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
3_11_28.MSG: If you pick up its carcass you must wash your clothes and are unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.
3_11_28.NIV: Anyone who picks up their carcasses must wash his clothes, and he will be unclean till evening. They are unclean for you.
3_11_28.NRSVCE: and the one who carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean for you.

3_11_29.NKJV: ‘These also shall be unclean to you among the creeping things that creep on the earth: the mole, the mouse, and the large lizard after its kind;
3_11_29.KJV: These also shall be unclean unto you among the creeping things that creep upon the earth; the weasel, and the mouse, and the tortoise after his kind,
3_11_29.ESV: “And these are unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm on the ground: the mole rat, the mouse, the great lizard of any kind,
3_11_29.NASB: 'Now these are to you the unclean among the swarming things which swarm on the earth: the mole, and the mouse, and the great lizard in its kinds,
3_11_29.NLT: “Of the small animals that scurry along the ground, these are unclean for you: the mole rat, the rat, large lizards of all kinds,
3_11_29.WEB: “‘These are they which are unclean to you among the creeping things that creep on the earth: the weasel, the rat, any kind of great lizard,
3_11_29.CSB17: “These creatures that swarm on the ground are unclean for you: weasels, mice, any kind of large lizard,
3_11_29.AMP: 'These also are unclean to you among the swarming things that crawl around on the ground [and multiply profusely]: the mole, the mouse, and any kind of great lizard,
3_11_29.MSG: "Among the creatures that crawl on the ground, the following are unclean for you: weasel, rat, all lizards,
3_11_29.NIV: “‘Of the animals that move about on the ground, these are unclean for you: the weasel, the rat, any kind of great lizard,
3_11_29.NRSVCE: These are unclean for you among the creatures that swarm upon the earth: the weasel, the mouse, the great lizard according to its kind,

3_11_30.NKJV: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the sand reptile, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.KJV: And the ferret, and the chameleon, and the lizard, and the snail, and the mole.
3_11_30.ESV: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.NASB: and the gecko, and the crocodile, and the lizard, and the sand reptile, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.NLT: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the common lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.WEB: the gecko, and the monitor lizard, the wall lizard, the skink, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.CSB17: geckos, monitor lizards, common lizards, skinks, and chameleons.
3_11_30.AMP: the gecko, the crocodile, the lizard, the sand reptile, and the chameleon.
3_11_30.MSG: gecko, monitor lizard, wall lizard, skink, chameleon.
3_11_30.NIV: the gecko, the monitor lizard, the wall lizard, the skink and the chameleon.
3_11_30.NRSVCE: the gecko, the land crocodile, the lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.

3_11_31.NKJV: These are unclean to you among all that creep. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_31.KJV: These are unclean to you among all that creep: whosoever doth touch them, when they be dead, shall be unclean until the even.
3_11_31.ESV: These are unclean to you among all that swarm. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
3_11_31.NASB: 'These are to you the unclean among all the swarming things; whoever touches them when they are dead becomes unclean until evening.
3_11_31.NLT: All these small animals are unclean for you. If any of you touch the dead body of such an animal, you will be defiled until evening.
3_11_31.WEB: These are they which are unclean to you among all that creep. Whoever touches them when they are dead, shall be unclean until the evening.
3_11_31.CSB17: These are unclean for you among all the swarming creatures. Whoever touches them when they are dead will be unclean until evening.
3_11_31.AMP: These [creatures] are unclean to you among all that swarm; whoever touches them when they are dead becomes unclean until evening.
3_11_31.MSG: Among the crawling creatures, these are unclean for you. If you touch them when they are dead, you are ritually unclean until evening.
3_11_31.NIV: Of all those that move along the ground, these are unclean for you. Whoever touches them when they are dead will be unclean till evening.
3_11_31.NRSVCE: These are unclean for you among all that swarm; whoever touches one of them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.

3_11_32.NKJV: Anything on which any of them falls, when they are dead shall be unclean, whether it is any item of wood or clothing or skin or sack, whatever item it is, in which any work is done, it must be put in water. And it shall be unclean until evening; then it shall be clean.
3_11_32.KJV: And upon whatsoever any of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be unclean; whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack, whatsoever vessel it be, wherein any work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; so it shall be cleansed.
3_11_32.ESV: And anything on which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether it is an article of wood or a garment or a skin or a sack, any article that is used for any purpose. It must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening; then it shall be clean.
3_11_32.NASB: 'Also anything on which one of them may fall when they are dead becomes unclean, including any wooden article, or clothing, or a skin, or a sack-- any article of which use is made-- it shall be put in the water and be unclean until evening, then it becomes clean.
3_11_32.NLT: If such an animal dies and falls on something, that object will be unclean. This is true whether the object is made of wood, cloth, leather, or burlap. Whatever its use, you must dip it in water, and it will remain defiled until evening. After that, it will be ceremonially clean and may be used again.
3_11_32.WEB: On whatever any of them falls when they are dead, it shall be unclean; whether it is any vessel of wood, or clothing, or skin, or sack, whatever vessel it is, with which any work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening; then it will be clean.
3_11_32.CSB17: When any one of them dies and falls on anything it becomes unclean — any item of wood, clothing, leather, sackcloth, or any implement used for work. It is to be rinsed with water and will remain unclean until evening; then it will be clean.
3_11_32.AMP: Also anything on which one of them falls after dying becomes unclean, whether it is an article of wood or clothing, or a skin, or a sack—any article that is used—it must be put in water, and will be unclean until the evening; then it becomes clean.
3_11_32.MSG: When one of them dies and falls on something, that becomes unclean no matter what it's used for, whether it's made of wood, cloth, hide, or sackcloth. Put it in the water—it's unclean until evening, and then it's clean.
3_11_32.NIV: When one of them dies and falls on something, that article, whatever its use, will be unclean, whether it is made of wood, cloth, hide or sackcloth. Put it in water; it will be unclean till evening, and then it will be clean.
3_11_32.NRSVCE: And anything upon which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether an article of wood or cloth or skin or sacking, any article that is used for any purpose; it shall be dipped into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening, and then it shall be clean.

3_11_33.NKJV: Any earthen vessel into which any of them falls you shall break; and whatever is in it shall be unclean:
3_11_33.KJV: And every earthen vessel, whereinto any of them falleth , whatsoever is in it shall be unclean; and ye shall break it.
3_11_33.ESV: And if any of them falls into any earthenware vessel, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break it.
3_11_33.NASB: 'As for any earthenware vessel into which one of them may fall, whatever is in it becomes unclean and you shall break the vessel.
3_11_33.NLT: “If such an animal falls into a clay pot, everything in the pot will be defiled, and the pot must be smashed.
3_11_33.WEB: Every earthen vessel, into which any of them falls, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break it.
3_11_33.CSB17: If any of them falls into any clay pot, everything in it will become unclean; you are to break it.
3_11_33.AMP: As for any earthenware container into which any of these [crawling things] falls, whatever is in it becomes unclean, and you shall break the container.
3_11_33.MSG: If one of these dead creatures falls into a clay pot, everything in the pot is unclean and you must break the pot.
3_11_33.NIV: If one of them falls into a clay pot, everything in it will be unclean, and you must break the pot.
3_11_33.NRSVCE: And if any of them falls into any earthen vessel, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break the vessel.

3_11_34.NKJV: in such a vessel, any edible food upon which water falls becomes unclean, and any drink that may be drunk from it becomes unclean.
3_11_34.KJV: Of all meat which may be eaten, that on which such water cometh shall be unclean: and all drink that may be drunk in every such vessel shall be unclean.
3_11_34.ESV: Any food in it that could be eaten, on which water comes, shall be unclean. And all drink that could be drunk from every such vessel shall be unclean.
3_11_34.NASB: 'Any of the food which may be eaten, on which water comes, shall become unclean, and any liquid which may be drunk in every vessel shall become unclean.
3_11_34.NLT: If the water from such a container spills on any food, the food will be defiled. And any beverage in such a container will be defiled.
3_11_34.WEB: All food which may be eaten, that on which water comes, shall be unclean; and all drink that may be drunk in every such vessel shall be unclean.
3_11_34.CSB17: Any edible food coming into contact with that unclean water will become unclean, and any drinkable liquid in any container will become unclean.
3_11_34.AMP: Any of the food which may be eaten, but on which [unclean] water falls, shall become unclean, and any liquid that may be drunk in every container shall become unclean.
3_11_34.MSG: Any food that could be eaten but has water on it from such a pot is unclean, and any liquid that could be drunk from it is unclean.
3_11_34.NIV: Any food that could be eaten but has water on it from such a pot is unclean, and any liquid that could be drunk from it is unclean.
3_11_34.NRSVCE: Any food that could be eaten shall be unclean if water from any such vessel comes upon it; and any liquid that could be drunk shall be unclean if it was in any such vessel.

3_11_35.NKJV: And everything on which a part of any such carcass falls shall be unclean; whether it is an oven or cooking stove, it shall be broken down; for they are unclean, and shall be unclean to you.
3_11_35.KJV: And every thing whereupon any part of their carcase falleth shall be unclean; whether it be oven, or ranges for pots, they shall be broken down: for they are unclean, and shall be unclean unto you.
3_11_35.ESV: And everything on which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean. Whether oven or stove, it shall be broken in pieces. They are unclean and shall remain unclean for you.
3_11_35.NASB: 'Everything, moreover, on which part of their carcass may fall becomes unclean; an oven or a stove shall be smashed; they are unclean and shall continue as unclean to you.
3_11_35.NLT: Any object on which the carcass of such an animal falls will be defiled. If it is an oven or hearth, it must be destroyed, for it is defiled, and you must treat it accordingly.
3_11_35.WEB: Everything whereupon part of their carcass falls shall be unclean; whether oven, or range for pots, it shall be broken in pieces: they are unclean, and shall be unclean to you.
3_11_35.CSB17: Anything one of their carcasses falls on will become unclean. If it is an oven or stove, it is to be smashed; it is unclean and will remain unclean for you.
3_11_35.AMP: Everything that part of their carcass falls on becomes unclean; an oven, or a small stove shall be smashed; they are unclean, and shall be unclean to you.
3_11_35.MSG: Anything that one of these carcasses falls on is unclean—an oven or cooking pot must be broken up; they're unclean and must be treated as unclean.
3_11_35.NIV: Anything that one of their carcasses falls on becomes unclean; an oven or cooking pot must be broken up. They are unclean, and you are to regard them as unclean.
3_11_35.NRSVCE: Everything on which any part of the carcass falls shall be unclean; whether an oven or stove, it shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and shall remain unclean for you.

3_11_36.NKJV: Nevertheless a spring or a cistern, in which there is plenty of water, shall be clean, but whatever touches any such carcass becomes unclean.
3_11_36.KJV: Nevertheless a fountain or pit, wherein there is plenty of water, shall be clean: but that which toucheth their carcase shall be unclean. wherein: Heb. a gathering together of waters
3_11_36.ESV: Nevertheless, a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean, but whoever touches a carcass in them shall be unclean.
3_11_36.NASB: 'Nevertheless a spring or a cistern collecting water shall be clean, though the one who touches their carcass shall be unclean.
3_11_36.NLT: “However, if the carcass of such an animal falls into a spring or a cistern, the water will still be clean. But anyone who touches the carcass will be defiled.
3_11_36.WEB: Nevertheless a spring or a cistern in which water is gathered shall be clean: but that which touches their carcass shall be unclean.
3_11_36.CSB17: A spring or cistern containing water will remain clean, but someone who touches a carcass in it will become unclean.
3_11_36.AMP: Nevertheless a spring or a cistern (reservoir) collecting water shall be clean; but whoever touches one of these carcasses shall be unclean.
3_11_36.MSG: A spring, though, or a cistern for collecting water remains clean, but if you touch one of these carcasses you're ritually unclean.
3_11_36.NIV: A spring, however, or a cistern for collecting water remains clean, but anyone who touches one of these carcasses is unclean.
3_11_36.NRSVCE: But a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean, while whatever touches the carcass in it shall be unclean.

3_11_37.NKJV: And if a part of any such carcass falls on any planting seed which is to be sown, it remains clean.
3_11_37.KJV: And if any part of their carcase fall upon any sowing seed which is to be sown, it shall be clean.
3_11_37.ESV: And if any part of their carcass falls upon any seed grain that is to be sown, it is clean,
3_11_37.NASB: 'If a part of their carcass falls on any seed for sowing which is to be sown, it is clean.
3_11_37.NLT: If the carcass falls on seed grain to be planted in the field, the seed will still be considered clean.
3_11_37.WEB: If part of their carcass falls on any sowing seed which is to be sown, it is clean.
3_11_37.CSB17: If one of their carcasses falls on any seed that is to be sown, it is clean;
3_11_37.AMP: If a part of their carcass falls on any seed for sowing which is to be sown, it is clean;
3_11_37.MSG: If a carcass falls on any seeds that are to be planted, they remain clean.
3_11_37.NIV: If a carcass falls on any seeds that are to be planted, they remain clean.
3_11_37.NRSVCE: If any part of their carcass falls upon any seed set aside for sowing, it is clean;

3_11_38.NKJV: But if water is put on the seed, and if a part of any such carcass falls on it, it becomes unclean to you.
3_11_38.KJV: But if any water be put upon the seed, and any part of their carcase fall thereon, it shall be unclean unto you.
3_11_38.ESV: but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean to you.
3_11_38.NASB: 'Though if water is put on the seed and a part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean to you.
3_11_38.NLT: But if the seed is wet when the carcass falls on it, the seed will be defiled.
3_11_38.WEB: But if water is put on the seed, and part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean to you.
3_11_38.CSB17: but if water has been put on the seed and one of their carcasses falls on it, it is unclean for you.
3_11_38.AMP: but if water is put on the seed and a part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean to you.
3_11_38.MSG: But if water has been put on the seed and a carcass falls on it, you must treat it as unclean.
3_11_38.NIV: But if water has been put on the seed and a carcass falls on it, it is unclean for you.
3_11_38.NRSVCE: but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean for you.

3_11_39.NKJV: ‘And if any animal which you may eat dies, he who touches its carcass shall be unclean until evening.
3_11_39.KJV: And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die; he that toucheth the carcase thereof shall be unclean until the even.
3_11_39.ESV: “And if any animal which you may eat dies, whoever touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
3_11_39.NASB: 'Also if one of the animals dies which you have for food, the one who touches its carcass becomes unclean until evening.
3_11_39.NLT: “If an animal you are permitted to eat dies and you touch its carcass, you will be defiled until evening.
3_11_39.WEB: “‘If any animal, of which you may eat, dies; he who touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening.
3_11_39.CSB17: “If one of the animals that you use for food dies, anyone who touches its carcass will be unclean until evening.
3_11_39.AMP: 'If one of the animals that you may eat dies [of natural causes], whoever touches its carcass becomes unclean until the evening.
3_11_39.MSG: "If an animal that you are permitted to eat dies, anyone who touches the carcass is ritually unclean until evening.
3_11_39.NIV: “‘If an animal that you are allowed to eat dies, anyone who touches the carcass will be unclean till evening.
3_11_39.NRSVCE: If an animal of which you may eat dies, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening.

3_11_40.NKJV: He who eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. He also who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
3_11_40.KJV: And he that eateth of the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: he also that beareth the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
3_11_40.ESV: and whoever eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening. And whoever carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_40.NASB: 'He too, who eats some of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening, and the one who picks up its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening.
3_11_40.NLT: If you eat any of its meat or carry away its carcass, you must wash your clothes, and you will remain defiled until evening.
3_11_40.WEB: He who eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening. He also who carries its carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_40.CSB17: Anyone who eats some of its carcass is to wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening. Anyone who carries its carcass must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening.
3_11_40.AMP: And whoever eats some of its meat shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until evening; also whoever picks up its carcass shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.
3_11_40.MSG: If you eat some of the carcass you must wash your clothes and you are unclean until evening. If you pick up the carcass you must wash your clothes and are unclean until evening.
3_11_40.NIV: Anyone who eats some of the carcass must wash his clothes, and he will be unclean till evening. Anyone who picks up the carcass must wash his clothes, and he will be unclean till evening.
3_11_40.NRSVCE: Those who eat of its carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening; and those who carry the carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening.

3_11_41.NKJV: ‘And every creeping thing that creeps on the earth shall be an abomination. It shall not be eaten.
3_11_41.KJV: And every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth shall be an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
3_11_41.ESV: “Every swarming thing that swarms on the ground is detestable; it shall not be eaten.
3_11_41.NASB: 'Now every swarming thing that swarms on the earth is detestable, not to be eaten.
3_11_41.NLT: “All small animals that scurry along the ground are detestable, and you must never eat them.
3_11_41.WEB: “‘Every creeping thing that creeps on the earth is an abomination. It shall not be eaten.
3_11_41.CSB17: “All the creatures that swarm on the earth are abhorrent; they must not be eaten.
3_11_41.AMP: 'Now everything that swarms on the ground is detestable; it is not to be eaten.
3_11_41.MSG: "Creatures that crawl on the ground are detestable and not to be eaten.
3_11_41.NIV: “‘Every creature that moves about on the ground is detestable; it is not to be eaten.
3_11_41.NRSVCE: All creatures that swarm upon the earth are detestable; they shall not be eaten.

3_11_42.NKJV: Whatever crawls on its belly, whatever goes on all fours, or whatever has many feet among all creeping things that creep on the earth—these you shall not eat, for they are an abomination.
3_11_42.KJV: Whatsoever goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon all four, or whatsoever hath more feet among all creeping things that creep upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they are an abomination. hath: Heb. doth multiply feet
3_11_42.ESV: Whatever goes on its belly, and whatever goes on all fours, or whatever has many feet, any swarming thing that swarms on the ground, you shall not eat, for they are detestable.
3_11_42.NASB: 'Whatever crawls on its belly, and whatever walks on [all] fours, whatever has many feet, in respect to every swarming thing that swarms on the earth, you shall not eat them, for they are detestable.
3_11_42.NLT: This includes all animals that slither along on their bellies, as well as those with four legs and those with many feet. All such animals that scurry along the ground are detestable, and you must never eat them.
3_11_42.WEB: Whatever goes on its belly, and whatever goes on all fours, or whatever has many feet, even all creeping things that creep on the earth, them you shall not eat; for they are an abomination.
3_11_42.CSB17: Do not eat any of the creatures that swarm on the earth, anything that moves on its belly or walks on all fours or on many feet, for they are abhorrent.
3_11_42.AMP: Whatever crawls on its belly, and whatever walks on all fours, and whatever has many feet among all things that swarm on the ground, you shall not eat; for they are detestable.
3_11_42.MSG: Don't eat creatures that crawl on the ground, whether on their belly or on all fours or on many feet—they are detestable.
3_11_42.NIV: You are not to eat any creature that moves about on the ground, whether it moves on its belly or walks on all fours or on many feet; it is detestable.
3_11_42.NRSVCE: Whatever moves on its belly, and whatever moves on all fours, or whatever has many feet, all the creatures that swarm upon the earth, you shall not eat; for they are detestable.

3_11_43.NKJV: You shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creeps; nor shall you make yourselves unclean with them, lest you be defiled by them.
3_11_43.KJV: Ye shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creepeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby. yourselves abominable: Heb. your souls, etc
3_11_43.ESV: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any swarming thing that swarms, and you shall not defile yourselves with them, and become unclean through them.
3_11_43.NASB: 'Do not render yourselves detestable through any of the swarming things that swarm; and you shall not make yourselves unclean with them so that you become unclean.
3_11_43.NLT: Do not defile yourselves by touching them. You must not make yourselves ceremonially unclean because of them.
3_11_43.WEB: You shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creeps. You shall not make yourselves unclean with them, that you should be defiled thereby.
3_11_43.CSB17: Do not become contaminated by any creature that swarms; do not become unclean or defiled by them.
3_11_43.AMP: Do not make yourselves loathsome (impure, repulsive) by [eating] any swarming thing; you shall not make yourselves unclean by them so as to defile yourselves.
3_11_43.MSG: Don't make yourselves unclean or be defiled by them, because I am your God.
3_11_43.NIV: Do not defile yourselves by any of these creatures. Do not make yourselves unclean by means of them or be made unclean by them.
3_11_43.NRSVCE: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any creature that swarms; you shall not defile yourselves with them, and so become unclean.

3_11_44.NKJV: For I am the LORD your God. You shall therefore consecrate yourselves, and you shall be holy; for I am holy. Neither shall you defile yourselves with any creeping thing that creeps on the earth.
3_11_44.KJV: For I am the LORD your God: ye shall therefore sanctify yourselves, and ye shall be holy; for I am holy: neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
3_11_44.ESV: For I am the LORD your God. Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming thing that crawls on the ground.
3_11_44.NASB: 'For I am the LORD your God. Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. And you shall not make yourselves unclean with any of the swarming things that swarm on the earth.
3_11_44.NLT: For I am the LORD your God. You must consecrate yourselves and be holy, because I am holy. So do not defile yourselves with any of these small animals that scurry along the ground.
3_11_44.WEB: For I am Yahweh your God. Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be holy; for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any kind of creeping thing that moves on the earth.
3_11_44.CSB17: For I am the LORD your God, so you must consecrate yourselves and be holy because I am holy. Do not defile yourselves by any swarming creature that crawls on the ground.
3_11_44.AMP: For I am the LORD your God; so consecrate yourselves and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not make yourselves unclean with any of the swarming things that swarm or crawls on the ground.
3_11_44.MSG: "Make yourselves holy for I am holy. Don't make yourselves ritually unclean by any creature that crawls on the ground.
3_11_44.NIV: I am the Lord your God; consecrate yourselves and be holy, because I am holy. Do not make yourselves unclean by any creature that moves about on the ground.
3_11_44.NRSVCE: For I am the LORD your God; sanctify yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming creature that moves on the earth.

3_11_45.NKJV: For I am the LORD who brings you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
3_11_45.KJV: For I am the LORD that bringeth you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
3_11_45.ESV: For I am the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.”
3_11_45.NASB: 'For I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God; thus you shall be holy, for I am holy.''
3_11_45.NLT: For I, the LORD, am the one who brought you up from the land of Egypt, that I might be your God. Therefore, you must be holy because I am holy.
3_11_45.WEB: For I am Yahweh who brought you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God. You shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
3_11_45.CSB17: For I am the LORD, who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God, so you must be holy because I am holy.
3_11_45.AMP: For I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt to be your God; therefore you shall be holy, for I am holy.'"
3_11_45.MSG: I am God who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Be holy because I am holy.
3_11_45.NIV: I am the Lord who brought you up out of Egypt to be your God; therefore be holy, because I am holy.
3_11_45.NRSVCE: For I am the LORD who brought you up from the land of Egypt, to be your God; you shall be holy, for I am holy.

3_11_46.NKJV: ‘This is the law of the animals and the birds and every living creature that moves in the waters, and of every creature that creeps on the earth,
3_11_46.KJV: This is the law of the beasts, and of the fowl, and of every living creature that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that creepeth upon the earth:
3_11_46.ESV: This is the law about beast and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms on the ground,
3_11_46.NASB: This is the law regarding the animal and the bird, and every living thing that moves in the waters and everything that swarms on the earth,
3_11_46.NLT: “These are the instructions regarding land animals, birds, marine creatures, and animals that scurry along the ground.
3_11_46.WEB: “‘This is the law of the animal, and of the bird, and of every living creature that moves in the waters, and of every creature that creeps on the earth,

3_12_2.NKJV: “Speak to the children of Israel, saying: ‘If a woman has conceived, and borne a male child, then she shall be unclean seven days; as in the days of her customary impurity she shall be unclean.

3_12_2.NASB: 'Speak to the sons of Israel, saying: 'When a woman gives birth and bears a male [child], then she shall be unclean for seven days, as in the days of her menstruation she shall be unclean.
3_12_2.NLT: “Give the following instructions to the people of Israel. If a woman becomes pregnant and gives birth to a son, she will be ceremonially unclean for seven days, just as she is unclean during her menstrual period.

3_12_2.MSG: "Tell the People of Israel: A woman who conceives and gives birth to a boy is ritually unclean for seven days, the same as during her menstruation.
3_12_2.NIV: “Say to the Israelites: ‘A woman who becomes pregnant and gives birth to a son will be ceremonially unclean for seven days, just as she is unclean during her monthly period.
3_12_2.NRSVCE: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: If a woman conceives and bears a male child, she shall be ceremonially unclean seven days; as at the time of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.

3_12_4.NKJV: She shall then continue in the blood of her purification thirty-three days. She shall not touch any hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary until the days of her purification are fulfilled.

3_12_4.ESV: Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying. She shall not touch anything holy, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying are completed.
3_12_4.NASB: 'Then she shall remain in the blood of [her] purification for thirty-three days; she shall not touch any consecrated thing, nor enter the sanctuary until the days of her purification are completed.
3_12_4.NLT: After waiting thirty-three days, she will be purified from the bleeding of childbirth. During this time of purification, she must not touch anything that is set apart as holy. And she must not enter the sanctuary until her time of purification is over.

3_12_4.NIV: Then the woman must wait thirty-three days to be purified from her bleeding. She must not touch anything sacred or go to the sanctuary until the days of her purification are over.
3_12_4.NRSVCE: Her time of blood purification shall be thirty-three days; she shall not touch any holy thing, or come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purification are completed.

3_12_5.NKJV: ‘But if she bears a female child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her customary impurity, and she shall continue in the blood of her purification sixty-six days.

3_12_5.CSB17: But if she gives birth to a female child, she will be unclean for two weeks as she is during her menstrual impurity. She will continue in purification from her bleeding for sixty-six days.

3_12_5.NIV: If she gives birth to a daughter, for two weeks the woman will be unclean, as during her period. Then she must wait sixty-six days to be purified from her bleeding.
3_12_5.NRSVCE: If she bears a female child, she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her menstruation; her time of blood purification shall be sixty-six days.

3_13_1.NKJV: And the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
3_13_1.KJV: And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron, saying,
3_13_1.ESV: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
3_13_1.NASB: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
3_13_1.NLT: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron,
3_13_1.WEB: Yahweh spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
3_13_1.CSB17: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
3_13_1.AMP: Then the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
3_13_1.MSG: God spoke to Moses and Aaron,
3_13_1.NIV: Regulations About Infectious Skin DiseasesThe Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
3_13_1.NRSVCE: The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:

3_13_2.NKJV: “When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling, a scab, or a bright spot, and it becomes on the skin of his body like a leprous sore, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.
3_13_2.KJV: When a man shall have in the skin of his flesh a rising, a scab, or bright spot, and it be in the skin of his flesh like the plague of leprosy; then he shall be brought unto Aaron the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests: rising: or, swelling
3_13_2.ESV: “When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot, and it turns into a case of leprous disease on the skin of his body, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests,
3_13_2.NASB: 'When a man has on the skin of his body a swelling or a scab or a bright spot, and it becomes an infection of leprosy on the skin of his body, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.
3_13_2.NLT: “If anyone has a swelling or a rash or discolored skin that might develop into a serious skin disease, that person must be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons.
3_13_2.WEB: “When a man shall have a rising in his body’s skin, or a scab, or a bright spot, and it becomes in the skin of his body the plague of leprosy, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest, or to one of his sons, the priests:
3_13_2.CSB17: “When a person has a swelling, scab, or spot on the skin of his body, and it may be a serious disease on the skin of his body, he is to be brought to the priest Aaron or to one of his sons, the priests.
3_13_2.AMP: "When a man has a swelling on the skin of his body, a scab, or a bright spot, and it becomes the infection of leprosy on the skin of his body, then he shall be brought to Aaron the priest, or to one of his sons the priests.
3_13_2.MSG: "When someone has a swelling or a blister or a shiny spot on the skin that might signal a serious skin disease on the body, bring him to Aaron the priest or to one of his priest sons.
3_13_2.NIV: “When anyone has a swelling or a rash or a bright spot on his skin that may become an infectious skin disease, he must be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons who is a priest.
3_13_2.NRSVCE: When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot, and it turns into a leprous disease on the skin of his body, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.

3_13_3.NKJV: The priest shall examine the sore on the skin of the body; and if the hair on the sore has turned white, and the sore appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a leprous sore. Then the priest shall examine him, and pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.KJV: And the priest shall look on the plague in the skin of the flesh: and when the hair in the plague is turned white, and the plague in sight be deeper than the skin of his flesh, it is a plague of leprosy: and the priest shall look on him, and pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.ESV: and the priest shall examine the diseased area on the skin of his body. And if the hair in the diseased area has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a case of leprous disease. When the priest has examined him, he shall pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.NASB: 'The priest shall look at the mark on the skin of the body, and if the hair in the infection has turned white and the infection appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is an infection of leprosy; when the priest has looked at him, he shall pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.NLT: The priest will examine the affected area of the skin. If the hair in the affected area has turned white and the problem appears to be more than skin-deep, it is a serious skin disease, and the priest who examines it must pronounce the person ceremonially unclean.
3_13_3.WEB: and the priest shall examine the plague in the skin of the body: and if the hair in the plague has turned white, and the appearance of the plague is deeper than the body’s skin, it is the plague of leprosy; and the priest shall examine him, and pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.CSB17: The priest will examine the sore on the skin of his body. If the hair in the sore has turned white and the sore appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is in fact a serious skin disease. After the priest examines him, he must pronounce him unclean.
3_13_3.AMP: The priest shall look at the diseased spot on the skin of his body, and if the hair in the infection has turned white and the infection appears deeper than the skin of his body, it is an infection of leprosy; when the priest has looked at him, he shall pronounce him [ceremonially] unclean.
3_13_3.MSG: The priest will examine the sore on the skin. If the hair in the sore has turned white and the sore appears more than skin deep, it is a serious skin disease and infectious. After the priest has examined it, he will pronounce the person unclean.
3_13_3.NIV: The priest is to examine the sore on his skin, and if the hair in the sore has turned white and the sore appears to be more than skin deep, it is an infectious skin disease. When the priest examines him, he shall pronounce him ceremonially unclean.
3_13_3.NRSVCE: The priest shall examine the disease on the skin of his body, and if the hair in the diseased area has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a leprous disease; after the priest has examined him he shall pronounce him ceremonially unclean.

3_13_4.NKJV: But if the bright spot is white on the skin of his body, and does not appear to be deeper than the skin, and its hair has not turned white, then the priest shall isolate the one who has the sore seven days.
3_13_4.KJV: If the bright spot be white in the skin of his flesh, and in sight be not deeper than the skin, and the hair thereof be not turned white; then the priest shall shut up him that hath the plague seven days:
3_13_4.ESV: But if the spot is white in the skin of his body and appears no deeper than the skin, and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest shall shut up the diseased person for seven days.
3_13_4.NASB: 'But if the bright spot is white on the skin of his body, and it does not appear to be deeper than the skin, and the hair on it has not turned white, then the priest shall isolate [him who has] the infection for seven days.
3_13_4.NLT: “But if the affected area of the skin is only a white discoloration and does not appear to be more than skin-deep, and if the hair on the spot has not turned white, the priest will quarantine the person for seven days.
3_13_4.WEB: If the bright spot is white in the skin of his body, and its appearance isn’t deeper than the skin, and its hair hasn’t turned white, then the priest shall isolate the infected person for seven days.
3_13_4.CSB17: But if the spot on the skin of his body is white and does not appear to be deeper than the skin, and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest will quarantine the stricken person for seven days.
3_13_4.AMP: If the bright spot is white on the skin of his body and does not appear to be deeper than the skin, and the hair on it has not turned white, the priest shall isolate the person who has the infection for seven days.
3_13_4.MSG: "If the shiny spot on the skin is white but appears to be only on the surface and the hair has not turned white, the priest will quarantine the person for seven days.
3_13_4.NIV: If the spot on his skin is white but does not appear to be more than skin deep and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest is to put the infected person in isolation for seven days.
3_13_4.NRSVCE: But if the spot is white in the skin of his body, and appears no deeper than the skin, and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest shall confine the diseased person for seven days.

3_13_5.NKJV: And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day; and indeed if the sore appears to be as it was, and the sore has not spread on the skin, then the priest shall isolate him another seven days.
3_13_5.KJV: And the priest shall look on him the seventh day: and, behold, if the plague in his sight be at a stay, and the plague spread not in the skin; then the priest shall shut him up seven days more:
3_13_5.ESV: And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if in his eyes the disease is checked and the disease has not spread in the skin, then the priest shall shut him up for another seven days.
3_13_5.NASB: 'The priest shall look at him on the seventh day, and if in his eyes the infection has not changed [and] the infection has not spread on the skin, then the priest shall isolate him for seven more days.
3_13_5.NLT: On the seventh day the priest will make another examination. If he finds the affected area has not changed and the problem has not spread on the skin, the priest will quarantine the person for seven more days.
3_13_5.WEB: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and, behold, if in his eyes the plague is arrested, and the plague hasn’t spread in the skin, then the priest shall isolate him for seven more days.
3_13_5.CSB17: The priest will then reexamine him on the seventh day. If he sees that the sore remains unchanged and has not spread on the skin, the priest will quarantine him for another seven days.
3_13_5.AMP: The priest shall examine it on the seventh day, and if in his estimation the infection has not changed and has not spread on the skin, then the priest shall isolate him for seven more days.
3_13_5.MSG: On the seventh day the priest will examine it again; if, in his judgment, the sore is the same and has not spread, the priest will keep him in quarantine for another seven days.
3_13_5.NIV: On the seventh day the priest is to examine him, and if he sees that the sore is unchanged and has not spread in the skin, he is to keep him in isolation another seven days.
3_13_5.NRSVCE: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if he sees that the disease is checked and the disease has not spread in the skin, then the priest shall confine him seven days more.
