3_23_21.NASB: 'On this same day you shall make a proclamation as well; you are to have a holy convocation. You shall do no laborious work. It is to be a perpetual statute in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.
3_23_21.NLT: That same day will be proclaimed an official day for holy assembly, a day on which you do no ordinary work. This is a permanent law for you, and it must be observed from generation to generation wherever you live.
3_23_21.WEB: You shall make proclamation on the same day: there shall be a holy convocation to you; you shall do no regular work. This is a statute forever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
3_23_21.CSB17: On that same day you are to make a proclamation and hold a sacred assembly. You are not to do any daily work. This is to be a permanent statute wherever you live throughout your generations.

3_23_21.NIV: On that same day you are to proclaim a sacred assembly and do no regular work. This is to be a lasting ordinance for the generations to come, wherever you live.
3_23_21.NRSVCE: On that same day you shall make proclamation; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. This is a statute forever in all your settlements throughout your generations.

3_23_22.NKJV: ‘When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not wholly reap the corners of your field when you reap, nor shall you gather any gleaning from your harvest. You shall leave them for the poor and for the stranger: I am the LORD your God.’ ”
3_23_22.KJV: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not make clean riddance of the corners of thy field when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy harvest: thou shalt leave them unto the poor, and to the stranger: I am the LORD your God.
3_23_22.ESV: “And when you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap your field right up to its edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings after your harvest. You shall leave them for the poor and for the sojourner: I am the LORD your God.”
3_23_22.NASB: 'When you reap the harvest of your land, moreover, you shall not reap to the very corners of your field nor gather the gleaning of your harvest; you are to leave them for the needy and the alien. I am the LORD your God.''
3_23_22.NLT: “When you harvest the crops of your land, do not harvest the grain along the edges of your fields, and do not pick up what the harvesters drop. Leave it for the poor and the foreigners living among you. I am the LORD your God.”
3_23_22.WEB: “‘When you reap the harvest of your land, you must not wholly reap into the corners of your field, and you must not gather the gleanings of your harvest. You must leave them for the poor, and for the foreigner. I am Yahweh your God.’”
3_23_22.CSB17: When you reap the harvest of your land, you are not to reap all the way to the edge of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest. Leave them for the poor and the resident alien; I am the LORD your God.”
3_23_22.AMP: 'When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the edges of your field, nor gather the gleaning of your harvest; you are to leave them for the poor and for the stranger. I am the LORD your God.'"
3_23_22.MSG: "When you reap the harvest of your land, don't reap the corners of your field or gather the gleanings. Leave them for the poor and the foreigners. I am God, your God."
3_23_22.NIV: “‘When you reap the harvest of your land, do not reap to the very edges of your field or gather the gleanings of your harvest. Leave them for the poor and the alien. I am the Lord your God.’”
3_23_22.NRSVCE: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest; you shall leave them for the poor and for the alien: I am the LORD your God.

3_23_27.NKJV: “Also the tenth day of this seventh month shall be the Day of Atonement. It shall be a holy convocation for you; you shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire to the LORD.

3_23_39.NKJV: ‘Also on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the fruit of the land, you shall keep the feast of the LORD for seven days; on the first day there shall be a sabbath-rest, and on the eighth day a sabbath-rest.
3_23_39.KJV: Also in the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the fruit of the land, ye shall keep a feast unto the LORD seven days: on the first day shall be a sabbath, and on the eighth day shall be a sabbath.

3_23_39.NLT: “Remember that this seven-day festival to the LORD — the Festival of Shelters — begins on the fifteenth day of the appointed month, after you have harvested all the produce of the land. The first day and the eighth day of the festival will be days of complete rest.
3_23_39.WEB: “‘So on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the fruits of the land, you shall keep the feast of Yahweh seven days: on the first day shall be a solemn rest, and on the eighth day shall be a solemn rest.
3_23_39.CSB17: “You are to celebrate the LORD’s festival on the fifteenth day of the seventh month for seven days after you have gathered the produce of the land. There will be complete rest on the first day and complete rest on the eighth day.
3_23_39.AMP: 'On exactly the fifteenth day of the seventh month (nearly October), when you have gathered in the crops of the land, you shall celebrate the feast of the LORD for seven days, with a Sabbath rest on the first day and a Sabbath rest on the eighth day.
3_23_39.MSG: "So, summing up: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, after you have brought your crops in from your fields, celebrate the Feast of God for seven days. The first day is a complete rest and the eighth day is a complete rest.
3_23_39.NIV: “‘So beginning with the fifteenth day of the seventh month, after you have gathered the crops of the land, celebrate the festival to the Lord for seven days; the first day is a day of rest, and the eighth day also is a day of rest.
3_23_39.NRSVCE: Now, the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall keep the festival of the LORD, lasting seven days; a complete rest on the first day, and a complete rest on the eighth day.

3_24_11.NLT: During the fight, this son of an Israelite woman blasphemed the Name of the LORD with a curse. So the man was brought to Moses for judgment. His mother was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri of the tribe of Dan.
3_24_11.WEB: The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name, and cursed; and they brought him to Moses. His mother’s name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.
3_24_11.CSB17: Her son cursed and blasphemed the Name, and they brought him to Moses. (His mother’s name was Shelomith, a daughter of Dibri of the tribe of Dan. )
3_24_11.AMP: The Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name [of the LORD] and cursed. So they brought him to Moses. (Now his mother's name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan.)
3_24_11.MSG: The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name of God and cursed. They brought him to Moses. His mother's name was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri of the tribe of Dan.
3_24_11.NIV: The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name with a curse; so they brought him to Moses. (His mother’s name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri the Danite.)
3_24_11.NRSVCE: The Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the Name in a curse. And they brought him to Moses — now his mother’s name was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan —

3_24_12.NKJV: Then they put him in custody, that the mind of the LORD might be shown to them.
3_24_12.KJV: And they put him in ward, that the mind of the LORD might be shewed them. that: Heb. to expound unto them according to the mouth of the LORD
3_24_12.ESV: And they put him in custody, till the will of the LORD should be clear to them.
3_24_12.NASB: They put him in custody so that the command of the LORD might be made clear to them.
3_24_12.NLT: They kept the man in custody until the LORD’s will in the matter should become clear to them.
3_24_12.WEB: They put him in custody, until Yahweh’s will should be declared to them.
3_24_12.CSB17: They put him in custody until the LORD’s decision could be made clear to them.
3_24_12.AMP: They put him in custody until the will and command of the LORD might be made clear to them.
3_24_12.MSG: They put him in custody waiting for God's will to be revealed to them.
3_24_12.NIV: They put him in custody until the will of the Lord should be made clear to them.
3_24_12.NRSVCE: and they put him in custody, until the decision of the LORD should be made clear to them.

3_24_13.NKJV: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_24_13.KJV: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
3_24_13.ESV: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_24_13.NASB: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_24_13.NLT: Then the LORD said to Moses,
3_24_13.CSB17: Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
3_24_13.AMP: Then the LORD spoke to Moses, saying,
3_24_13.NIV: Then the Lord said to Moses:
3_24_13.NRSVCE: The LORD said to Moses, saying:

3_24_14.NKJV: “Take outside the camp him who has cursed; then let all who heard him lay their hands on his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.KJV: Bring forth him that hath cursed without the camp; and let all that heard him lay their hands upon his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.ESV: “Bring out of the camp the one who cursed, and let all who heard him lay their hands on his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.NASB: 'Bring the one who has cursed outside the camp, and let all who heard him lay their hands on his head; then let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.NLT: “Take the blasphemer outside the camp, and tell all those who heard the curse to lay their hands on his head. Then let the entire community stone him to death.
3_24_14.WEB: “Bring out of the camp him who cursed; and let all who heard him lay their hands on his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.CSB17: “Bring the one who has cursed to the outside of the camp and have all who heard him lay their hands on his head; then have the whole community stone him.
3_24_14.AMP: "Bring the one who has cursed [the LORD] outside the camp, and let all who heard him lay their hands on his head [as witnesses to his guilt]; then let all the congregation stone him.
3_24_14.MSG: "Take the blasphemer outside the camp. Have all those who heard him place their hands on his head; then have the entire congregation stone him.
3_24_14.NIV: “Take the blasphemer outside the camp. All those who heard him are to lay their hands on his head, and the entire assembly is to stone him.
3_24_14.NRSVCE: Take the blasphemer outside the camp; and let all who were within hearing lay their hands on his head, and let the whole congregation stone him.

3_24_15.NKJV: “Then you shall speak to the children of Israel, saying: ‘Whoever curses his God shall bear his sin.
3_24_15.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Whosoever curseth his God shall bear his sin.
3_24_15.ESV: And speak to the people of Israel, saying, Whoever curses his God shall bear his sin.
3_24_15.NASB: 'You shall speak to the sons of Israel, saying, 'If anyone curses his God, then he will bear his sin.
3_24_15.NLT: Say to the people of Israel: Those who curse their God will be punished for their sin.
3_24_15.WEB: You shall speak to the children of Israel, saying, ‘Whoever curses his God shall bear his sin.
3_24_15.CSB17: And tell the Israelites: If anyone curses his God, he will bear the consequences of his sin.
3_24_15.AMP: You shall speak to the Israelites, saying, 'Whoever curses his God will bear his sin [through his own death].
3_24_15.MSG: Then tell the Israelites, Anyone who curses God will be held accountable;
3_24_15.NIV: Say to the Israelites: ‘If anyone curses his God, he will be held responsible;
3_24_15.NRSVCE: And speak to the people of Israel, saying: Anyone who curses God shall bear the sin.

3_24_16.NKJV: And whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall certainly stone him, the stranger as well as him who is born in the land. When he blasphemes the name of the LORD, he shall be put to death.
3_24_16.KJV: And he that blasphemeth the name of the LORD, he shall surely be put to death, and all the congregation shall certainly stone him: as well the stranger, as he that is born in the land, when he blasphemeth the name of the LORD, shall be put to death.
3_24_16.ESV: Whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall stone him. The sojourner as well as the native, when he blasphemes the Name, shall be put to death.
3_24_16.NASB: 'Moreover, the one who blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death; all the congregation shall certainly stone him. The alien as well as the native, when he blasphemes the Name, shall be put to death.
3_24_16.NLT: Anyone who blasphemes the Name of the LORD must be stoned to death by the whole community of Israel. Any native-born Israelite or foreigner among you who blasphemes the Name of the LORD must be put to death.
3_24_16.WEB: He who blasphemes Yahweh’s name, he shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall certainly stone him. The foreigner as well as the native-born, when he blasphemes the Name, shall be put to death.
3_24_16.CSB17: Whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD must be put to death; the whole community is to stone him. If he blasphemes the Name, he is to be put to death, whether the resident alien or the native.
3_24_16.AMP: Further, the one who blasphemes the name of the LORD shall most certainly be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him. The stranger as well as the native-born shall be put to death when he blasphemes the Name [of the LORD].
3_24_16.MSG: anyone who blasphemes the Name of God must be put to death. The entire congregation must stone him. It makes no difference whether he is a foreigner or a native, if he blasphemes the Name, he will be put to death.
3_24_16.NIV: anyone who blasphemes the name of the Lord must be put to death. The entire assembly must stone him. Whether an alien or native-born, when he blasphemes the Name, he must be put to death.
3_24_16.NRSVCE: One who blasphemes the name of the LORD shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone the blasphemer. Aliens as well as citizens, when they blaspheme the Name, shall be put to death.

3_24_17.NKJV: ‘Whoever kills any man shall surely be put to death.
3_24_17.KJV: And he that killeth any man shall surely be put to death. killeth: Heb. smiteth the life of a man
3_24_17.ESV: “Whoever takes a human life shall surely be put to death.
3_24_17.NASB: 'If a man takes the life of any human being, he shall surely be put to death.
3_24_17.NLT: “Anyone who takes another person’s life must be put to death.
3_24_17.WEB: “‘He who strikes any man mortally shall surely be put to death.
3_24_17.CSB17: “If a man kills anyone, he must be put to death.
3_24_17.AMP: 'If a man takes the life of any human being [unlawfully], he shall most certainly be put to death.
3_24_17.MSG: "Anyone who hits and kills a fellow human must be put to death.
3_24_17.NIV: “‘If anyone takes the life of a human being, he must be put to death.
3_24_17.NRSVCE: Anyone who kills a human being shall be put to death.

3_24_18.NKJV: Whoever kills an animal shall make it good, animal for animal.
3_24_18.KJV: And he that killeth a beast shall make it good; beast for beast. beast for: Heb. life for life
3_24_18.ESV: Whoever takes an animal's life shall make it good, life for life.
3_24_18.NASB: 'The one who takes the life of an animal shall make it good, life for life.
3_24_18.NLT: “Anyone who kills another person’s animal must pay for it in full — a live animal for the animal that was killed.
3_24_18.WEB: He who strikes an animal mortally shall make it good, life for life.
3_24_18.CSB17: Whoever kills an animal is to make restitution for it, life for life.
3_24_18.AMP: The one who kills an animal shall replace it, animal for animal.
3_24_18.MSG: Anyone who kills someone's animal must make it good—a life for a life.
3_24_18.NIV: Anyone who takes the life of someone’s animal must make restitution—life for life.
3_24_18.NRSVCE: Anyone who kills an animal shall make restitution for it, life for life.

3_24_19.NKJV: ‘If a man causes disfigurement of his neighbor, as he has done, so shall it be done to him—
3_24_19.KJV: And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour; as he hath done, so shall it be done to him;
3_24_19.ESV: If anyone injures his neighbor, as he has done it shall be done to him,
3_24_19.NASB: 'If a man injures his neighbor, just as he has done, so it shall be done to him:
3_24_19.NLT: “Anyone who injures another person must be dealt with according to the injury inflicted —
3_24_19.WEB: If anyone injures his neighbor; as he has done, so shall it be done to him:
3_24_19.CSB17: If any man inflicts a permanent injury on his neighbor, whatever he has done is to be done to him:
3_24_19.AMP: If a man injures his neighbor (fellow citizen), whatever he has done shall be done to him:
3_24_19.MSG: Anyone who injures his neighbor will get back the same as he gave:
3_24_19.NIV: If anyone injures his neighbor, whatever he has done must be done to him:
3_24_19.NRSVCE: Anyone who maims another shall suffer the same injury in return:

3_24_20.NKJV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; as he has caused disfigurement of a man, so shall it be done to him.
3_24_20.KJV: Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth: as he hath caused a blemish in a man, so shall it be done to him again.
3_24_20.ESV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; whatever injury he has given a person shall be given to him.
3_24_20.NASB: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; just as he has injured a man, so it shall be inflicted on him.
3_24_20.NLT: a fracture for a fracture, an eye for an eye, a tooth for a tooth. Whatever anyone does to injure another person must be paid back in kind.
3_24_20.WEB: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; as he has injured someone, so shall it be done to him.
3_24_20.CSB17: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. Whatever injury he inflicted on the person, the same is to be inflicted on him.
3_24_20.AMP: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; just as he has injured a man, so shall the same be done to him.
3_24_20.MSG: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. What he did to hurt that person will be done to him.
3_24_20.NIV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. As he has injured the other, so he is to be injured.
3_24_20.NRSVCE: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; the injury inflicted is the injury to be suffered.

3_24_21.NKJV: And whoever kills an animal shall restore it; but whoever kills a man shall be put to death.
3_24_21.KJV: And he that killeth a beast, he shall restore it: and he that killeth a man, he shall be put to death.
3_24_21.ESV: Whoever kills an animal shall make it good, and whoever kills a person shall be put to death.
3_24_21.NASB: 'Thus the one who kills an animal shall make it good, but the one who kills a man shall be put to death.
3_24_21.NLT: “Whoever kills an animal must pay for it in full, but whoever kills another person must be put to death.
3_24_21.WEB: He who kills an animal shall make it good; and he who kills a man shall be put to death.
3_24_21.CSB17: Whoever kills an animal is to make restitution for it, but whoever kills a person is to be put to death.
3_24_21.AMP: The one who kills an animal shall replace it; but he who kills a human being [unlawfully] shall be put to death.
3_24_21.MSG: Anyone who hits and kills an animal must make it good, but whoever hits and kills a fellow human will be put to death.
3_24_21.NIV: Whoever kills an animal must make restitution, but whoever kills a man must be put to death.
3_24_21.NRSVCE: One who kills an animal shall make restitution for it; but one who kills a human being shall be put to death.

3_24_22.NKJV: You shall have the same law for the stranger and for one from your own country; for I am the LORD your God.’ ”
3_24_22.KJV: Ye shall have one manner of law, as well for the stranger, as for one of your own country: for I am the LORD your God.
3_24_22.ESV: You shall have the same rule for the sojourner and for the native, for I am the LORD your God.”
3_24_22.NASB: 'There shall be one standard for you; it shall be for the stranger as well as the native, for I am the LORD your God.''
3_24_22.NLT: “This same standard applies both to native-born Israelites and to the foreigners living among you. I am the LORD your God.”
3_24_22.WEB: You shall have one kind of law for the foreigner as well as the native-born; for I am Yahweh your God.’”
3_24_22.CSB17: You are to have the same law for the resident alien and the native, because I am the LORD your God.”
3_24_22.AMP: You shall have one standard of law for the stranger among you as well as for the native, for I am the LORD your God.'"
3_24_22.MSG: And no double standards: the same rule goes for foreigners and natives. I am God, your God."
3_24_22.NIV: You are to have the same law for the alien and the native-born. I am the Lord your God.’”
3_24_22.NRSVCE: You shall have one law for the alien and for the citizen: for I am the LORD your God.

3_24_23.NKJV: Then Moses spoke to the children of Israel; and they took outside the camp him who had cursed, and stoned him with stones. So the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses.
3_24_23.KJV: And Moses spake to the children of Israel, that they should bring forth him that had cursed out of the camp, and stone him with stones. And the children of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses.
3_24_23.ESV: So Moses spoke to the people of Israel, and they brought out of the camp the one who had cursed and stoned him with stones. Thus the people of Israel did as the LORD commanded Moses.
3_24_23.NASB: Then Moses spoke to the sons of Israel, and they brought the one who had cursed outside the camp and stoned him with stones. Thus the sons of Israel did, just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_24_23.NLT: After Moses gave all these instructions to the Israelites, they took the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him to death. The Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_24_23.WEB: Moses spoke to the children of Israel; and they brought out him who had cursed out of the camp, and stoned him with stones. The children of Israel did as Yahweh commanded Moses.
3_24_23.CSB17: After Moses spoke to the Israelites, they brought the one who had cursed to the outside of the camp and stoned him. So the Israelites did as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_24_23.AMP: Then Moses spoke to the Israelites, and they brought the one who had cursed [the LORD] outside the camp and stoned him with stones. Thus the Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
3_24_23.MSG: Moses then spoke to the People of Israel. They brought the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him. The People of Israel followed the orders God had given Moses.
3_24_23.NIV: Then Moses spoke to the Israelites, and they took the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him. The Israelites did as the Lord commanded Moses.
3_24_23.NRSVCE: Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; and they took the blasphemer outside the camp, and stoned him to death. The people of Israel did as the LORD had commanded Moses.

3_25_1.NKJV: And the LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_1.KJV: And the LORD spake unto Moses in mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_1.ESV: The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_1.NASB: The LORD then spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_1.NLT: While Moses was on Mount Sinai, the LORD said to him,
3_25_1.WEB: Yahweh said to Moses in Mount Sinai,
3_25_1.CSB17: The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai:
3_25_1.AMP: The LORD spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai, saying,
3_25_1.MSG: God spoke to Moses at Mount Sinai,
3_25_1.NIV: The Sabbath YearThe Lord said to Moses on Mount Sinai,
3_25_1.NRSVCE: The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying:

3_25_2.NKJV: “Speak to the children of Israel, and say to them: ‘When you come into the land which I give you, then the land shall keep a sabbath to the LORD.
3_25_2.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land which I give you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto the LORD. keep: Heb. rest
3_25_2.ESV: “Speak to the people of Israel and say to them, When you come into the land that I give you, the land shall keep a Sabbath to the LORD.
3_25_2.NASB: 'Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them, 'When you come into the land which I shall give you, then the land shall have a sabbath to the LORD.
3_25_2.NLT: “Give the following instructions to the people of Israel. When you have entered the land I am giving you, the land itself must observe a Sabbath rest before the LORD every seventh year.
3_25_2.WEB: “Speak to the children of Israel, and tell them, ‘When you come into the land which I give you, then the land shall keep a Sabbath to Yahweh.
3_25_2.CSB17: “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you, the land will observe a Sabbath to the LORD.
3_25_2.AMP: "Speak to the children of Israel and say to them, 'When you come into the land which I am giving you, then the land shall keep a Sabbath to the LORD.
3_25_2.MSG: "Speak to the People of Israel. Tell them: When you enter the land which I am going to give you, the land will observe a Sabbath to God.
3_25_2.NIV: “Speak to the Israelites and say to them: ‘When you enter the land I am going to give you, the land itself must observe a sabbath to the Lord.
3_25_2.NRSVCE: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you, the land shall observe a sabbath for the LORD.

3_25_3.NKJV: Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard, and gather its fruit;
3_25_3.KJV: Six years thou shalt sow thy field, and six years thou shalt prune thy vineyard, and gather in the fruit thereof;
3_25_3.ESV: For six years you shall sow your field, and for six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather in its fruits,
3_25_3.NASB: 'Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather in its crop,
3_25_3.NLT: For six years you may plant your fields and prune your vineyards and harvest your crops,
3_25_3.WEB: You shall sow your field six years, and you shall prune your vineyard six years, and gather in its fruits;
3_25_3.CSB17: You may sow your field for six years, and you may prune your vineyard and gather its produce for six years.
3_25_3.AMP: For six years you shall sow your field, and for six years you shall prune your vineyard and gather in its crop.
3_25_3.MSG: Sow your fields, prune your vineyards, and take in your harvests for six years.
3_25_3.NIV: For six years sow your fields, and for six years prune your vineyards and gather their crops.
3_25_3.NRSVCE: Six years you shall sow your field, and six years you shall prune your vineyard, and gather in their yield;

3_25_4.NKJV: but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a sabbath to the LORD. You shall neither sow your field nor prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.KJV: But in the seventh year shall be a sabbath of rest unto the land, a sabbath for the LORD: thou shalt neither sow thy field, nor prune thy vineyard.
3_25_4.ESV: but in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to the LORD. You shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.NASB: but during the seventh year the land shall have a sabbath rest, a sabbath to the LORD; you shall not sow your field nor prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.NLT: but during the seventh year the land must have a Sabbath year of complete rest. It is the LORD’s Sabbath. Do not plant your fields or prune your vineyards during that year.
3_25_4.WEB: but in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to Yahweh. You shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.CSB17: But there will be a Sabbath of complete rest for the land in the seventh year, a Sabbath to the LORD: you are not to sow your field or prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.AMP: But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of rest for the land, a Sabbath to the LORD; you shall not sow [seed in] your field nor prune your vineyard.
3_25_4.MSG: But the seventh year the land will take a Sabbath of complete and total rest, a Sabbath to God; you will not sow your fields or prune your vineyards.
3_25_4.NIV: But in the seventh year the land is to have a sabbath of rest, a sabbath to the Lord. Do not sow your fields or prune your vineyards.
3_25_4.NRSVCE: but in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of complete rest for the land, a sabbath for the LORD: you shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.

3_25_5.NKJV: What grows of its own accord of your harvest you shall not reap, nor gather the grapes of your untended vine, for it is a year of rest for the land.
3_25_5.KJV: That which groweth of its own accord of thy harvest thou shalt not reap, neither gather the grapes of thy vine undressed: for it is a year of rest unto the land. of thy vine: Heb. of thy separation
3_25_5.ESV: You shall not reap what grows of itself in your harvest, or gather the grapes of your undressed vine. It shall be a year of solemn rest for the land.
3_25_5.NASB: 'Your harvest's aftergrowth you shall not reap, and your grapes of untrimmed vines you shall not gather; the land shall have a sabbatical year.
3_25_5.NLT: And don’t store away the crops that grow on their own or gather the grapes from your unpruned vines. The land must have a year of complete rest.
3_25_5.WEB: What grows of itself in your harvest you shall not reap, and you shall not gather the grapes of your undressed vine. It shall be a year of solemn rest for the land.
3_25_5.CSB17: You are not to reap what grows by itself from your crop, or harvest the grapes of your untended vines. It is to be a year of complete rest for the land.
3_25_5.AMP: Whatever reseeds itself (uncultivated) in your harvest you shall not reap, nor shall you gather the grapes from your uncultivated vine, it shall be a year of sabbatical rest for the land.
3_25_5.MSG: Don't reap what grows of itself; don't harvest the grapes of your untended vines. The land gets a year of complete and total rest.
3_25_5.NIV: Do not reap what grows of itself or harvest the grapes of your untended vines. The land is to have a year of rest.
3_25_5.NRSVCE: You shall not reap the aftergrowth of your harvest or gather the grapes of your unpruned vine: it shall be a year of complete rest for the land.

3_25_6.NKJV: And the sabbath produce of the land shall be food for you: for you, your male and female servants, your hired man, and the stranger who dwells with you,
3_25_6.KJV: And the sabbath of the land shall be meat for you; for thee, and for thy servant, and for thy maid, and for thy hired servant, and for thy stranger that sojourneth with thee,
3_25_6.ESV: The Sabbath of the land shall provide food for you, for yourself and for your male and female slaves and for your hired worker and the sojourner who lives with you,
3_25_6.NASB: 'All of you shall have the sabbath [products] of the land for food; yourself, and your male and female slaves, and your hired man and your foreign resident, those who live as aliens with you.
3_25_6.NLT: But you may eat whatever the land produces on its own during its Sabbath. This applies to you, your male and female servants, your hired workers, and the temporary residents who live with you.
3_25_6.WEB: The Sabbath of the land shall be for food for you; for yourself, for your servant, for your maid, for your hired servant, and for your stranger, who lives as a foreigner with you.
3_25_6.CSB17: Whatever the land produces during the Sabbath year can be food for you — for yourself, your male or female slave, and the hired worker or alien who resides with you.
3_25_6.AMP: And all of you shall have for food whatever the [untilled] land produces during its Sabbath year; yourself, and your male and female slaves, your hired servant, and the foreigners who reside among you,
3_25_6.MSG: But you can eat from what the land volunteers during the Sabbath year—you and your men and women servants, your hired hands, and the foreigners who live in the country,
3_25_6.NIV: Whatever the land yields during the sabbath year will be food for you—for yourself, your manservant and maidservant, and the hired worker and temporary resident who live among you,
3_25_6.NRSVCE: You may eat what the land yields during its sabbath — you, your male and female slaves, your hired and your bound laborers who live with you;

3_25_7.NKJV: for your livestock and the beasts that are in your land—all its produce shall be for food.
3_25_7.KJV: And for thy cattle, and for the beast that are in thy land, shall all the increase thereof be meat.
3_25_7.ESV: and for your cattle and for the wild animals that are in your land: all its yield shall be for food.
3_25_7.NASB: 'Even your cattle and the animals that are in your land shall have all its crops to eat.
3_25_7.NLT: Your livestock and the wild animals in your land will also be allowed to eat what the land produces.
3_25_7.WEB: For your livestock also, and for the animals that are in your land, shall all its increase be for food.
3_25_7.CSB17: All of its growth may serve as food for your livestock and the wild animals in your land.
3_25_7.AMP: even your domestic animals and the [wild] animals that are in your land shall have all its crops to eat.
3_25_7.MSG: and, of course, also your livestock and the wild animals in the land can eat from it. Whatever the land volunteers of itself can be eaten.
3_25_7.NIV: as well as for your livestock and the wild animals in your land. Whatever the land produces may be eaten.
3_25_7.NRSVCE: for your livestock also, and for the wild animals in your land all its yield shall be for food.

3_25_8.NKJV: ‘And you shall count seven sabbaths of years for yourself, seven times seven years; and the time of the seven sabbaths of years shall be to you forty-nine years.
3_25_8.KJV: And thou shalt number seven sabbaths of years unto thee, seven times seven years; and the space of the seven sabbaths of years shall be unto thee forty and nine years.
3_25_8.ESV: “You shall count seven weeks of years, seven times seven years, so that the time of the seven weeks of years shall give you forty-nine years.
3_25_8.NASB: 'You are also to count off seven sabbaths of years for yourself, seven times seven years, so that you have the time of the seven sabbaths of years, [namely], forty-nine years.
3_25_8.NLT: “In addition, you must count off seven Sabbath years, seven sets of seven years, adding up to forty-nine years in all.
3_25_8.WEB: “‘You shall count off seven Sabbaths of years, seven times seven years; and there shall be to you the days of seven Sabbaths of years, even forty-nine years.
3_25_8.CSB17: “You are to count seven sabbatical years, seven times seven years, so that the time period of the seven sabbatical years amounts to forty-nine.
3_25_8.AMP: 'You are also to count off seven Sabbaths of years for yourself, seven times seven years, so that you have the time of the seven Sabbaths of years, namely, forty-nine years.
3_25_8.MSG: "Count off seven Sabbaths of years—seven times seven years: Seven Sabbaths of years adds up to forty-nine years.
3_25_8.NIV: The Year of Jubilee“‘Count off seven sabbaths of years—seven times seven years—so that the seven sabbaths of years amount to a period of forty-nine years.
3_25_8.NRSVCE: You shall count off seven weeks of years, seven times seven years, so that the period of seven weeks of years gives forty-nine years.

3_25_9.NKJV: Then you shall cause the trumpet of the Jubilee to sound on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the Day of Atonement you shall make the trumpet to sound throughout all your land.
3_25_9.KJV: Then shalt thou cause the trumpet of the jubile to sound on the tenth day of the seventh month, in the day of atonement shall ye make the trumpet sound throughout all your land. of the jubile: Heb. loud of sound
3_25_9.ESV: Then you shall sound the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month. On the Day of Atonement you shall sound the trumpet throughout all your land.
3_25_9.NASB: 'You shall then sound a ram's horn abroad on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the day of atonement you shall sound a horn all through your land.
3_25_9.NLT: Then on the Day of Atonement in the fiftieth year, blow the ram’s horn loud and long throughout the land.
3_25_9.WEB: Then you shall sound the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month. On the Day of Atonement you shall sound the trumpet throughout all your land.
3_25_9.CSB17: Then you are to sound a trumpet loudly in the seventh month, on the tenth day of the month; you will sound it throughout your land on the Day of Atonement.
3_25_9.AMP: Then you shall sound the ram's horn everywhere on the tenth day of the seventh month (almost October); on the Day of Atonement you shall sound the trumpet throughout your land.
3_25_9.MSG: Then sound loud blasts on the ram's horn on the tenth day of the seventh month, the Day of Atonement. Sound the ram's horn all over the land.
3_25_9.NIV: Then have the trumpet sounded everywhere on the tenth day of the seventh month; on the Day of Atonement sound the trumpet throughout your land.
3_25_9.NRSVCE: Then you shall have the trumpet sounded loud; on the tenth day of the seventh month — on the day of atonement — you shall have the trumpet sounded throughout all your land.

3_25_10.NKJV: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout all the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a Jubilee for you; and each of you shall return to his possession, and each of you shall return to his family.
3_25_10.KJV: And ye shall hallow the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout all the land unto all the inhabitants thereof: it shall be a jubile unto you; and ye shall return every man unto his possession, and ye shall return every man unto his family.
3_25_10.ESV: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, when each of you shall return to his property and each of you shall return to his clan.
3_25_10.NASB: 'You shall thus consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim a release through the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, and each of you shall return to his own property, and each of you shall return to his family.
3_25_10.NLT: Set this year apart as holy, a time to proclaim freedom throughout the land for all who live there. It will be a jubilee year for you, when each of you may return to the land that belonged to your ancestors and return to your own clan.
3_25_10.WEB: You shall make the fiftieth year holy, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee to you; and each of you shall return to his own property, and each of you shall return to his family.
3_25_10.CSB17: You are to consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim freedom in the land for all its inhabitants. It will be your Jubilee, when each of you is to return to his property and each of you to his clan.
3_25_10.AMP: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim freedom [for the slaves] throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a Jubilee (year of remission) for you, and each of you shall return to his own [ancestral] property [that was sold to another because of poverty], and each of you shall return to his family [from whom he was separated by bondage].
3_25_10.MSG: Sanctify the fiftieth year; make it a holy year. Proclaim freedom all over the land to everyone who lives in it—a Jubilee for you: Each person will go back to his family's property and reunite with his extended family.
3_25_10.NIV: Consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you; each one of you is to return to his family property and each to his own clan.
3_25_10.NRSVCE: And you shall hallow the fiftieth year and you shall proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you: you shall return, every one of you, to your property and every one of you to your family.

3_25_11.NKJV: That fiftieth year shall be a Jubilee to you; in it you shall neither sow nor reap what grows of its own accord, nor gather the grapes of your untended vine.
3_25_11.KJV: A jubile shall that fiftieth year be unto you: ye shall not sow, neither reap that which groweth of itself in it, nor gather the grapes in it of thy vine undressed.
3_25_11.ESV: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you; in it you shall neither sow nor reap what grows of itself nor gather the grapes from the undressed vines.
3_25_11.NASB: 'You shall have the fiftieth year as a jubilee; you shall not sow, nor reap its aftergrowth, nor gather in [from] its untrimmed vines.
3_25_11.NLT: This fiftieth year will be a jubilee for you. During that year you must not plant your fields or store away any of the crops that grow on their own, and don’t gather the grapes from your unpruned vines.
3_25_11.WEB: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee to you. In it you shall not sow, neither reap that which grows of itself, nor gather from the undressed vines.
3_25_11.CSB17: The fiftieth year will be your Jubilee; you are not to sow, reap what grows by itself, or harvest its untended vines.
3_25_11.AMP: That fiftieth year shall be a Jubilee for you; you shall not sow [seed], nor reap what reseeds itself, nor gather the grapes of the uncultivated vines.
3_25_11.MSG: The fiftieth year is your Jubilee year: Don't sow; don't reap what volunteers itself in the fields; don't harvest the untended vines
3_25_11.NIV: The fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you; do not sow and do not reap what grows of itself or harvest the untended vines.
3_25_11.NRSVCE: That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you: you shall not sow, or reap the aftergrowth, or harvest the unpruned vines.

3_25_12.NKJV: For it is the Jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you shall eat its produce from the field.
3_25_12.KJV: For it is the jubile; it shall be holy unto you: ye shall eat the increase thereof out of the field.
3_25_12.ESV: For it is a jubilee. It shall be holy to you. You may eat the produce of the field.
3_25_12.NASB: 'For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you. You shall eat its crops out of the field.
3_25_12.NLT: It will be a jubilee year for you, and you must keep it holy. But you may eat whatever the land produces on its own.
3_25_12.WEB: For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you. You shall eat of its increase out of the field.
3_25_12.CSB17: It is to be holy to you because it is the Jubilee; you may only eat its produce directly from the field.
3_25_12.AMP: For it is the Jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you shall eat its crops out of the field.
3_25_12.MSG: because it's the Jubilee and a holy year for you. You're permitted to eat from whatever volunteers itself in the fields.
3_25_12.NIV: For it is a jubilee and is to be holy for you; eat only what is taken directly from the fields.
3_25_12.NRSVCE: For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you: you shall eat only what the field itself produces.

3_25_13.NKJV: ‘In this Year of Jubilee, each of you shall return to his possession.
3_25_13.KJV: In the year of this jubile ye shall return every man unto his possession.
3_25_13.ESV: “In this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his property.
3_25_13.NASB: 'On this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his own property.
3_25_13.NLT: In the Year of Jubilee each of you may return to the land that belonged to your ancestors.
3_25_13.WEB: “‘In this Year of Jubilee each of you shall return to his property.
3_25_13.CSB17: “In this Year of Jubilee, each of you will return to his property.
3_25_13.AMP: 'In this Year of Jubilee each of you shall return to his own [ancestral] property.
3_25_13.MSG: "In this year of Jubilee everyone returns home to his family property.
3_25_13.NIV: “‘In this Year of Jubilee everyone is to return to his own property.
3_25_13.NRSVCE: In this year of jubilee you shall return, every one of you, to your property.

3_25_14.NKJV: And if you sell anything to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor’s hand, you shall not oppress one another.
3_25_14.KJV: And if thou sell ought unto thy neighbour, or buyest ought of thy neighbour's hand, ye shall not oppress one another:
3_25_14.ESV: And if you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor, you shall not wrong one another.
3_25_14.NASB: 'If you make a sale, moreover, to your friend or buy from your friend's hand, you shall not wrong one another.
3_25_14.NLT: “When you make an agreement with your neighbor to buy or sell property, you must not take advantage of each other.
3_25_14.WEB: “‘If you sell anything to your neighbor, or buy from your neighbor, you shall not wrong one another.
3_25_14.CSB17: If you make a sale to your neighbor or a purchase from him, do not cheat one another.
3_25_14.AMP: If you sell anything to your friend or buy from your friend, you shall not wrong one another.
3_25_14.MSG: "If you sell or buy property from one of your countrymen, don't cheat him.
3_25_14.NIV: “‘If you sell land to one of your countrymen or buy any from him, do not take advantage of each other.
3_25_14.NRSVCE: When you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor, you shall not cheat one another.

3_25_15.NKJV: According to the number of years after the Jubilee you shall buy from your neighbor, and according to the number of years of crops he shall sell to you.
3_25_15.KJV: According to the number of years after the jubile thou shalt buy of thy neighbour, and according unto the number of years of the fruits he shall sell unto thee:
3_25_15.ESV: You shall pay your neighbor according to the number of years after the jubilee, and he shall sell to you according to the number of years for crops.
3_25_15.NASB: 'Corresponding to the number of years after the jubilee, you shall buy from your friend; he is to sell to you according to the number of years of crops.
3_25_15.NLT: When you buy land from your neighbor, the price you pay must be based on the number of years since the last jubilee. The seller must set the price by taking into account the number of years remaining until the next Year of Jubilee.
3_25_15.WEB: According to the number of years after the Jubilee you shall buy from your neighbor. According to the number of years of the crops he shall sell to you.
3_25_15.CSB17: You are to make the purchase from your neighbor based on the number of years since the last Jubilee. He is to sell to you based on the number of remaining harvest years.
3_25_15.AMP: According to the number of years after the Jubilee, you shall buy from your friend. And he is to sell to you according to the number of years of crops [which may be harvested before you must restore the property to him].
3_25_15.MSG: Calculate the purchase price on the basis of the number of years since the Jubilee. He is obliged to set the sale price on the basis of the number of harvests remaining until the next Jubilee.
3_25_15.NIV: You are to buy from your countryman on the basis of the number of years since the Jubilee. And he is to sell to you on the basis of the number of years left for harvesting crops.
3_25_15.NRSVCE: When you buy from your neighbor, you shall pay only for the number of years since the jubilee; the seller shall charge you only for the remaining crop years.

3_25_16.NKJV: According to the multitude of years you shall increase its price, and according to the fewer number of years you shall diminish its price; for he sells to you according to the number of the years of the crops.
3_25_16.KJV: According to the multitude of years thou shalt increase the price thereof, and according to the fewness of years thou shalt diminish the price of it: for according to the number of the years of the fruits doth he sell unto thee.
3_25_16.ESV: If the years are many, you shall increase the price, and if the years are few, you shall reduce the price, for it is the number of the crops that he is selling to you.
3_25_16.NASB: 'In proportion to the extent of the years you shall increase its price, and in proportion to the fewness of the years you shall diminish its price, for [it is] a number of crops he is selling to you.
3_25_16.NLT: The more years until the next jubilee, the higher the price; the fewer years, the lower the price. After all, the person selling the land is actually selling you a certain number of harvests.
3_25_16.WEB: According to the length of the years you shall increase its price, and according to the shortness of the years you shall diminish its price; for he is selling the number of the crops to you.
3_25_16.CSB17: You are to increase its price in proportion to a greater amount of years, and decrease its price in proportion to a lesser amount of years, because what he is selling to you is a number of harvests.
3_25_16.AMP: If the years [until the next Jubilee] are many, you shall increase the price, but if the years remaining are few, you shall reduce the price, because it is the number of crops that he is selling to you.
3_25_16.MSG: The more years left, the more money; you can raise the price. But the fewer years left, the less money; decrease the price. What you are buying and selling in fact is the number of crops you're going to harvest.
3_25_16.NIV: When the years are many, you are to increase the price, and when the years are few, you are to decrease the price, because what he is really selling you is the number of crops.
3_25_16.NRSVCE: If the years are more, you shall increase the price, and if the years are fewer, you shall diminish the price; for it is a certain number of harvests that are being sold to you.

3_25_17.NKJV: Therefore you shall not oppress one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.KJV: Ye shall not therefore oppress one another; but thou shalt fear thy God: for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.ESV: You shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God, for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.NASB: 'So you shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.NLT: Show your fear of God by not taking advantage of each other. I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.WEB: You shall not wrong one another; but you shall fear your God: for I am Yahweh your God.
3_25_17.CSB17: You are not to cheat one another, but fear your God, for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.AMP: You shall not wrong one another, but you shall fear your God [with profound reverence]; for I am the LORD your God.
3_25_17.MSG: Don't cheat each other. Fear your God. I am God, your God.
3_25_17.NIV: Do not take advantage of each other, but fear your God. I am the Lord your God.
3_25_17.NRSVCE: You shall not cheat one another, but you shall fear your God; for I am the LORD your God.

3_25_18.NKJV: ‘So you shall observe My statutes and keep My judgments, and perform them; and you will dwell in the land in safety.
3_25_18.KJV: Wherefore ye shall do my statutes, and keep my judgments, and do them; and ye shall dwell in the land in safety.
3_25_18.ESV: “Therefore you shall do my statutes and keep my rules and perform them, and then you will dwell in the land securely.
3_25_18.NASB: 'You shall thus observe My statutes and keep My judgments, so as to carry them out, that you may live securely on the land.
3_25_18.NLT: “If you want to live securely in the land, follow my decrees and obey my regulations.
3_25_18.WEB: “‘Therefore you shall do my statutes, and keep my ordinances and do them; and you shall dwell in the land in safety.
3_25_18.CSB17: “You are to keep my statutes and ordinances and carefully observe them, so that you may live securely in the land.
3_25_18.AMP: 'Therefore you shall carry out My statutes and keep My ordinances and do them, so that you may live securely on the land.
3_25_18.MSG: "Keep my decrees and observe my laws and you will live secure in the land.
3_25_18.NIV: “‘Follow my decrees and be careful to obey my laws, and you will live safely in the land.
3_25_18.NRSVCE: You shall observe my statutes and faithfully keep my ordinances, so that you may live on the land securely.

3_25_19.NKJV: Then the land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill, and dwell there in safety.
3_25_19.KJV: And the land shall yield her fruit, and ye shall eat your fill, and dwell therein in safety.
3_25_19.ESV: The land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and dwell in it securely.
3_25_19.NASB: 'Then the land will yield its produce, so that you can eat your fill and live securely on it.
3_25_19.NLT: Then the land will yield large crops, and you will eat your fill and live securely in it.
3_25_19.WEB: The land shall yield its fruit, and you shall eat your fill, and dwell therein in safety.
3_25_19.CSB17: Then the land will yield its fruit, so that you can eat, be satisfied, and live securely in the land.
3_25_19.AMP: Then the land will yield its produce, so that you can eat your fill and live securely on it.
3_25_19.MSG: The land will yield its fruit; you will have all you can eat and will live safe and secure.
3_25_19.NIV: Then the land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and live there in safety.
3_25_19.NRSVCE: The land will yield its fruit, and you will eat your fill and live on it securely.

3_25_20.NKJV: ‘And if you say, “What shall we eat in the seventh year, since we shall not sow nor gather in our produce?”
3_25_20.KJV: And if ye shall say, What shall we eat the seventh year? behold, we shall not sow, nor gather in our increase:
3_25_20.ESV: And if you say, ‘What shall we eat in the seventh year, if we may not sow or gather in our crop?’
3_25_20.NASB: 'But if you say, 'What are we going to eat on the seventh year if we do not sow or gather in our crops?'
3_25_20.NLT: But you might ask, ‘What will we eat during the seventh year, since we are not allowed to plant or harvest crops that year?’
3_25_20.WEB: If you said, “What shall we eat the seventh year? Behold, we shall not sow, nor gather in our increase”;
3_25_20.CSB17: If you wonder: ‘What will we eat in the seventh year if we don’t sow or gather our produce?’
3_25_20.AMP: And if you say, "What are we going to eat in the seventh year if we do not sow [seed] or gather in our crops?"
3_25_20.MSG: Do I hear you ask, 'What are we going to eat in the seventh year if we don't plant or harvest?'
3_25_20.NIV: You may ask, “What will we eat in the seventh year if we do not plant or harvest our crops?”
3_25_20.NRSVCE: Should you ask, “What shall we eat in the seventh year, if we may not sow or gather in our crop?”

3_25_21.NKJV: Then I will command My blessing on you in the sixth year, and it will bring forth produce enough for three years.
3_25_21.KJV: Then I will command my blessing upon you in the sixth year, and it shall bring forth fruit for three years.
3_25_21.ESV: I will command my blessing on you in the sixth year, so that it will produce a crop sufficient for three years.
3_25_21.NASB: then I will so order My blessing for you in the sixth year that it will bring forth the crop for three years.
3_25_21.NLT: Be assured that I will send my blessing for you in the sixth year, so the land will produce a crop large enough for three years.
3_25_21.WEB: then I will command my blessing on you in the sixth year, and it shall bear fruit for the three years.
3_25_21.CSB17: I will appoint my blessing for you in the sixth year, so that it will produce a crop sufficient for three years.
3_25_21.AMP: then [this is My answer:] I will order My [special] blessing for you in the sixth year, so that it will produce [sufficient] crops for three years.
3_25_21.MSG: I assure you, I will send such a blessing in the sixth year that the land will yield enough for three years.
3_25_21.NIV: I will send you such a blessing in the sixth year that the land will yield enough for three years.
3_25_21.NRSVCE: I will order my blessing for you in the sixth year, so that it will yield a crop for three years.

3_25_22.NKJV: And you shall sow in the eighth year, and eat old produce until the ninth year; until its produce comes in, you shall eat of the old harvest.
3_25_22.KJV: And ye shall sow the eighth year, and eat yet of old fruit until the ninth year; until her fruits come in ye shall eat of the old store.
3_25_22.ESV: When you sow in the eighth year, you will be eating some of the old crop; you shall eat the old until the ninth year, when its crop arrives.
3_25_22.NASB: 'When you are sowing the eighth year, you can still eat old things from the crop, eating [the old] until the ninth year when its crop comes in.
3_25_22.NLT: When you plant your fields in the eighth year, you will still be eating from the large crop of the sixth year. In fact, you will still be eating from that large crop when the new crop is harvested in the ninth year.
3_25_22.WEB: You shall sow the eighth year, and eat of the fruits, the old store; until the ninth year, until its fruits come in, you shall eat the old store.
3_25_22.CSB17: When you sow in the eighth year, you will be eating from the previous harvest. You will be eating this until the ninth year when its harvest comes in.
3_25_22.AMP: When you are sowing the eighth year, you can still eat old things from the crops, eating the old until the ninth year when its crop comes in.
3_25_22.MSG: While you plant in the eighth year, you will eat from the old crop and continue until the harvest of the ninth year comes in.
3_25_22.NIV: While you plant during the eighth year, you will eat from the old crop and will continue to eat from it until the harvest of the ninth year comes in.
3_25_22.NRSVCE: When you sow in the eighth year, you will be eating from the old crop; until the ninth year, when its produce comes in, you shall eat the old.

3_25_23.NKJV: ‘The land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; for you are strangers and sojourners with Me.
3_25_23.KJV: The land shall not be sold for ever: for the land is mine; for ye are strangers and sojourners with me. for ever: or, to be quite cut off: Heb. for cutting off
3_25_23.ESV: “The land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine. For you are strangers and sojourners with me.
3_25_23.NASB: 'The land, moreover, shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; for you are [but] aliens and sojourners with Me.
3_25_23.NLT: “The land must never be sold on a permanent basis, for the land belongs to me. You are only foreigners and tenant farmers working for me.
3_25_23.WEB: “‘The land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine; for you are strangers and live as foreigners with me.
3_25_23.CSB17: “The land is not to be permanently sold because it is mine, and you are only aliens and temporary residents on my land.
3_25_23.AMP: 'The land shall not be sold permanently, for the land is Mine; you are [only] foreigners and temporary residents with Me.
3_25_23.MSG: "The land cannot be sold permanently because the land is mine and you are foreigners—you're my tenants.
3_25_23.NIV: “‘The land must not be sold permanently, because the land is mine and you are but aliens and my tenants.
3_25_23.NRSVCE: The land shall not be sold in perpetuity, for the land is mine; with me you are but aliens and tenants.

3_25_24.NKJV: And in all the land of your possession you shall grant redemption of the land.
3_25_24.KJV: And in all the land of your possession ye shall grant a redemption for the land.
3_25_24.ESV: And in all the country you possess, you shall allow a redemption of the land.
3_25_24.NASB: 'Thus for every piece of your property, you are to provide for the redemption of the land.
3_25_24.NLT: “With every purchase of land you must grant the seller the right to buy it back.
3_25_24.WEB: In all the land of your possession you shall grant a redemption for the land.
3_25_24.CSB17: You are to allow the redemption of any land you occupy.
3_25_24.AMP: So in all the country that you possess, you are to provide for the redemption of the land [in the Year of Jubilee].
3_25_24.MSG: You must provide for the right of redemption for any of the land that you own.
3_25_24.NIV: Throughout the country that you hold as a possession, you must provide for the redemption of the land.
3_25_24.NRSVCE: Throughout the land that you hold, you shall provide for the redemption of the land.

3_25_25.NKJV: ‘If one of your brethren becomes poor, and has sold some of his possession, and if his redeeming relative comes to redeem it, then he may redeem what his brother sold.
3_25_25.KJV: If thy brother be waxen poor, and hath sold away some of his possession, and if any of his kin come to redeem it, then shall he redeem that which his brother sold.
3_25_25.ESV: “If your brother becomes poor and sells part of his property, then his nearest redeemer shall come and redeem what his brother has sold.
3_25_25.NASB: 'If a fellow countryman of yours becomes so poor he has to sell part of his property, then his nearest kinsman is to come and buy back what his relative has sold.
3_25_25.NLT: If one of your fellow Israelites falls into poverty and is forced to sell some family land, then a close relative should buy it back for him.
3_25_25.WEB: “‘If your brother becomes poor, and sells some of his possessions, then his kinsman who is next to him shall come, and redeem that which his brother has sold.
3_25_25.CSB17: If your brother becomes destitute and sells part of his property, his nearest relative may come and redeem what his brother has sold.
3_25_25.AMP: 'If a fellow countryman of yours becomes so poor he has to sell some of his property, then his nearest relative is to come and buy back (redeem) what his relative has sold.
3_25_25.MSG: "If one of your brothers becomes poor and has to sell any of his land, his nearest relative is to come and buy back what his brother sold.
3_25_25.NIV: “‘If one of your countrymen becomes poor and sells some of his property, his nearest relative is to come and redeem what his countryman has sold.
3_25_25.NRSVCE: If anyone of your kin falls into difficulty and sells a piece of property, then the next of kin shall come and redeem what the relative has sold.

3_25_26.NKJV: Or if the man has no one to redeem it, but he himself becomes able to redeem it,
3_25_26.KJV: And if the man have none to redeem it, and himself be able to redeem it; himself: Heb. his hand hath attained and found sufficiency
3_25_26.ESV: If a man has no one to redeem it and then himself becomes prosperous and finds sufficient means to redeem it,
3_25_26.NASB: 'Or in case a man has no kinsman, but so recovers his means as to find sufficient for its redemption,
3_25_26.NLT: If there is no close relative to buy the land, but the person who sold it gets enough money to buy it back,
3_25_26.WEB: If a man has no one to redeem it, and he becomes prosperous and finds sufficient means to redeem it;
3_25_26.CSB17: If a man has no family redeemer, but he prospers and obtains enough to redeem his land,
3_25_26.AMP: Or in case a man has no relative [to redeem his property], but he has become more prosperous and has enough to buy it back,
3_25_26.MSG: If a man has no one to redeem it but he later prospers and earns enough for its redemption,
3_25_26.NIV: If, however, a man has no one to redeem it for him but he himself prospers and acquires sufficient means to redeem it,
3_25_26.NRSVCE: If the person has no one to redeem it, but then prospers and finds sufficient means to do so,

3_25_27.NKJV: then let him count the years since its sale, and restore the remainder to the man to whom he sold it, that he may return to his possession.
3_25_27.KJV: Then let him count the years of the sale thereof, and restore the overplus unto the man to whom he sold it; that he may return unto his possession.
3_25_27.ESV: let him calculate the years since he sold it and pay back the balance to the man to whom he sold it, and then return to his property.
3_25_27.NASB: then he shall calculate the years since its sale and refund the balance to the man to whom he sold it, and so return to his property.
3_25_27.NLT: he then has the right to redeem it from the one who bought it. The price of the land will be discounted according to the number of years until the next Year of Jubilee. In this way the original owner can then return to the land.
3_25_27.WEB: then let him reckon the years since its sale, and restore the surplus to the man to whom he sold it; and he shall return to his property.
3_25_27.CSB17: he may calculate the years since its sale, repay the balance to the man he sold it to, and return to his property.
3_25_27.AMP: then he shall calculate the years since its sale and refund the balance to the man to whom he sold it, and so return to his [ancestral] property.
3_25_27.MSG: he is to calculate the value since he sold it and refund the balance to the man to whom he sold it; he can then go back to his own land.
3_25_27.NIV: he is to determine the value for the years since he sold it and refund the balance to the man to whom he sold it; he can then go back to his own property.
3_25_27.NRSVCE: the years since its sale shall be computed and the difference shall be refunded to the person to whom it was sold, and the property shall be returned.

3_25_28.NKJV: But if he is not able to have it restored to himself, then what was sold shall remain in the hand of him who bought it until the Year of Jubilee; and in the Jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his possession.
3_25_28.KJV: But if he be not able to restore it to him, then that which is sold shall remain in the hand of him that hath bought it until the year of jubile: and in the jubile it shall go out, and he shall return unto his possession.
3_25_28.ESV: But if he does not have sufficient means to recover it, then what he sold shall remain in the hand of the buyer until the year of jubilee. In the jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his property.
3_25_28.NLT: But if the original owner cannot afford to buy back the land, it will remain with the new owner until the next Year of Jubilee. In the jubilee year, the land must be returned to the original owners so they can return to their family land.
3_25_28.WEB: But if he isn’t able to get it back for himself, then what he has sold shall remain in the hand of him who has bought it until the Year of Jubilee: and in the Jubilee it shall be released, and he shall return to his property.
3_25_28.CSB17: But if he cannot obtain enough to repay him, what he sold will remain in the possession of its purchaser until the Year of Jubilee. It is to be released at the Jubilee, so that he may return to his property.
3_25_28.AMP: But if he is unable to redeem it, then what he has sold shall remain in the hands of the purchaser until the Year of Jubilee; but at the Jubilee it shall revert, and he may return to his property.
3_25_28.MSG: If he doesn't get together enough money to repay him, what he sold remains in the possession of the buyer until the year of Jubilee. In the Jubilee it will be returned and he can go back and live on his land.
3_25_28.NIV: But if he does not acquire the means to repay him, what he sold will remain in the possession of the buyer until the Year of Jubilee. It will be returned in the Jubilee, and he can then go back to his property.
3_25_28.NRSVCE: But if there are not sufficient means to recover it, what was sold shall remain with the purchaser until the year of jubilee; in the jubilee it shall be released, and the property shall be returned.

3_25_29.NKJV: ‘If a man sells a house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it is sold; within a full year he may redeem it.
3_25_29.KJV: And if a man sell a dwelling house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it is sold; within a full year may he redeem it.
3_25_29.ESV: “If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, he may redeem it within a year of its sale. For a full year he shall have the right of redemption.
3_25_29.NASB: 'Likewise, if a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, then his redemption right remains valid until a full year from its sale; his right of redemption lasts a full year.
3_25_29.NLT: “Anyone who sells a house inside a walled town has the right to buy it back for a full year after its sale. During that year, the seller retains the right to buy it back.
3_25_29.WEB: “‘If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city, then he may redeem it within a whole year after it has been sold. For a full year he shall have the right of redemption.
3_25_29.CSB17: “If a man sells a residence in a walled city, his right of redemption will last until a year has passed after its sale; his right of redemption will last a year.
3_25_29.AMP: 'If a man sells a house in a walled city, then his right of redemption remains valid for a full year after its sale; his right of redemption lasts a full year.
3_25_29.MSG: "If a man sells a house in a walled city, he retains the right to buy it back for a full year after the sale. At any time during that year he can redeem it.
3_25_29.NIV: “‘If a man sells a house in a walled city, he retains the right of redemption a full year after its sale. During that time he may redeem it.
3_25_29.NRSVCE: If anyone sells a dwelling house in a walled city, it may be redeemed until a year has elapsed since its sale; the right of redemption shall be one year.

3_25_30.NKJV: But if it is not redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house in the walled city shall belong permanently to him who bought it, throughout his generations. It shall not be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_30.KJV: And if it be not redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house that is in the walled city shall be established for ever to him that bought it throughout his generations: it shall not go out in the jubile.
3_25_30.ESV: If it is not redeemed within a full year, then the house in the walled city shall belong in perpetuity to the buyer, throughout his generations; it shall not be released in the jubilee.
3_25_30.NASB: 'But if it is not bought back for him within the space of a full year, then the house that is in the walled city passes permanently to its purchaser throughout his generations; it does not revert in the jubilee.
3_25_30.NLT: But if it is not bought back within a year, the sale of the house within the walled town cannot be reversed. It will become the permanent property of the buyer. It will not be returned to the original owner in the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_30.WEB: If it isn’t redeemed within the space of a full year, then the house that is in the walled city shall be made sure in perpetuity to him who bought it, throughout his generations. It shall not be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_30.CSB17: If it is not redeemed by the end of a full year, then the house in the walled city is permanently transferred to its purchaser throughout his generations. It is not to be released on the Jubilee.
3_25_30.AMP: But if it is not redeemed for him within a full year, then the house that is in the walled city passes permanently and irrevocably to the purchaser throughout his generations. It does not revert back in the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_30.MSG: But if it is not redeemed before the full year has passed, it becomes the permanent possession of the buyer and his descendants. It is not returned in the Jubilee.
3_25_30.NIV: If it is not redeemed before a full year has passed, the house in the walled city shall belong permanently to the buyer and his descendants. It is not to be returned in the Jubilee.
3_25_30.NRSVCE: If it is not redeemed before a full year has elapsed, a house that is in a walled city shall pass in perpetuity to the purchaser, throughout the generations; it shall not be released in the jubilee.

3_25_31.NKJV: However the houses of villages which have no wall around them shall be counted as the fields of the country. They may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_31.KJV: But the houses of the villages which have no wall round about them shall be counted as the fields of the country: they may be redeemed, and they shall go out in the jubile. they may: Heb. redemption belongeth unto it
3_25_31.ESV: But the houses of the villages that have no wall around them shall be classified with the fields of the land. They may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the jubilee.
3_25_31.NASB: 'The houses of the villages, however, which have no surrounding wall shall be considered as open fields; they have redemption rights and revert in the jubilee.
3_25_31.NLT: But a house in a village — a settlement without fortified walls — will be treated like property in the countryside. Such a house may be bought back at any time, and it must be returned to the original owner in the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_31.WEB: But the houses of the villages which have no wall around them shall be accounted for with the fields of the country: they may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the Jubilee.
3_25_31.CSB17: But houses in settlements that have no walls around them are to be classified as open fields. The right to redeem such houses stays in effect, and they are to be released at the Jubilee.
3_25_31.AMP: The houses of the villages that have no surrounding walls, however, shall be considered as open fields. They may be redeemed, and revert in the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_31.MSG: However, houses in unwalled villages are treated the same as fields. They can be redeemed and have to be returned at the Jubilee.
3_25_31.NIV: But houses in villages without walls around them are to be considered as open country. They can be redeemed, and they are to be returned in the Jubilee.
3_25_31.NRSVCE: But houses in villages that have no walls around them shall be classed as open country; they may be redeemed, and they shall be released in the jubilee.

3_25_32.NKJV: Nevertheless the cities of the Levites, and the houses in the cities of their possession, the Levites may redeem at any time.
3_25_32.KJV: Notwithstanding the cities of the Levites, and the houses of the cities of their possession, may the Levites redeem at any time.
3_25_32.ESV: As for the cities of the Levites, the Levites may redeem at any time the houses in the cities they possess.
3_25_32.NASB: 'As for cities of the Levites, the Levites have a permanent right of redemption for the houses of the cities which are their possession.
3_25_32.NLT: “The Levites always have the right to buy back a house they have sold within the towns allotted to them.
3_25_32.WEB: “‘Nevertheless the cities of the Levites, the houses in the cities of their possession, the Levites may redeem at any time.
3_25_32.CSB17: “Concerning the Levitical cities, the Levites always have the right to redeem houses in the cities they possess.
3_25_32.AMP: As for the cities of the Levites, the Levites have a permanent right of redemption for the houses in the cities which they possess.
3_25_32.MSG: "As to the Levitical cities, houses in the cities owned by the Levites are always subject to redemption.
3_25_32.NIV: “‘The Levites always have the right to redeem their houses in the Levitical towns, which they possess.
3_25_32.NRSVCE: As for the cities of the Levites, the Levites shall forever have the right of redemption of the houses in the cities belonging to them.

3_25_33.NKJV: And if a man purchases a house from the Levites, then the house that was sold in the city of his possession shall be released in the Jubilee; for the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the children of Israel.
3_25_33.KJV: And if a man purchase of the Levites, then the house that was sold, and the city of his possession, shall go out in the year of jubile: for the houses of the cities of the Levites are their possession among the children of Israel. a man: or, one of the Levites redeem them
3_25_33.ESV: And if one of the Levites exercises his right of redemption, then the house that was sold in a city they possess shall be released in the jubilee. For the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the people of Israel.
3_25_33.NASB: 'What, therefore, belongs to the Levites may be redeemed and a house sale in the city of this possession reverts in the jubilee, for the houses of the cities of the Levites are their possession among the sons of Israel.
3_25_33.NLT: And any property that is sold by the Levites — all houses within the Levitical towns — must be returned in the Year of Jubilee. After all, the houses in the towns reserved for the Levites are the only property they own in all Israel.
3_25_33.WEB: The Levites may redeem the house that was sold, and the city of his possession, and it shall be released in the Jubilee; for the houses of the cities of the Levites are their possession among the children of Israel.
3_25_33.CSB17: Whatever property one of the Levites can redeem — a house sold in a city they possess — is to be released at the Jubilee, because the houses in the Levitical cities are their possession among the Israelites.
3_25_33.AMP: Therefore, what is [purchased] from the Levites may be redeemed [by a Levite], and the house that was sold in the city they possess reverts in the Year of Jubilee, for the houses in the Levite cities are their [ancestral] property among the Israelites.
3_25_33.MSG: Levitical property is always redeemable if it is sold in a town that they hold and reverts to them in the Jubilee, because the houses in the towns of the Levites are their property among the People of Israel.
3_25_33.NIV: So the property of the Levites is redeemable—that is, a house sold in any town they hold—and is to be returned in the Jubilee, because the houses in the towns of the Levites are their property among the Israelites.
3_25_33.NRSVCE: Such property as may be redeemed from the Levites — houses sold in a city belonging to them — shall be released in the jubilee; because the houses in the cities of the Levites are their possession among the people of Israel.

3_25_34.NKJV: But the field of the common-land of their cities may not be sold, for it is their perpetual possession.
3_25_34.KJV: But the field of the suburbs of their cities may not be sold; for it is their perpetual possession.
3_25_34.ESV: But the fields of pastureland belonging to their cities may not be sold, for that is their possession forever.
3_25_34.NASB: 'But pasture fields of their cities shall not be sold, for that is their perpetual possession.
3_25_34.NLT: The open pastureland around the Levitical towns may never be sold. It is their permanent possession.
3_25_34.WEB: But the field of the suburbs of their cities may not be sold; for it is their perpetual possession.
3_25_34.CSB17: The open pastureland around their cities may not be sold, for it is their permanent possession.
3_25_34.AMP: But the pasture lands of their cities may not be sold, for that is their permanent possession.
3_25_34.MSG: The pastures belonging to their cities may not be sold; they are their permanent possession.
3_25_34.NIV: But the pastureland belonging to their towns must not be sold; it is their permanent possession.
3_25_34.NRSVCE: But the open land around their cities may not be sold; for that is their possession for all time.
