3_25_35.NKJV: ‘If one of your brethren becomes poor, and falls into poverty among you, then you shall help him, like a stranger or a sojourner, that he may live with you.
3_25_35.KJV: And if thy brother be waxen poor, and fallen in decay with thee; then thou shalt relieve him: yea, though he be a stranger, or a sojourner; that he may live with thee. fallen: Heb. his hand faileth relieve: Heb. strengthen
3_25_35.ESV: “If your brother becomes poor and cannot maintain himself with you, you shall support him as though he were a stranger and a sojourner, and he shall live with you.
3_25_35.NASB: 'Now in case a countryman of yours becomes poor and his means with regard to you falter, then you are to sustain him, like a stranger or a sojourner, that he may live with you.
3_25_35.NLT: “If one of your fellow Israelites falls into poverty and cannot support himself, support him as you would a foreigner or a temporary resident and allow him to live with you.
3_25_35.WEB: “‘If your brother has become poor, and his hand can’t support himself among you; then you shall uphold him. He shall live with you like an alien and a temporary resident.
3_25_35.CSB17: “If your brother becomes destitute and cannot sustain himself among you, you are to support him as an alien or temporary resident, so that he can continue to live among you.
3_25_35.AMP: 'Now if your fellow countryman becomes poor and his hand falters with you [that is, he has trouble repaying you for something], then you are to help and sustain him, [with courtesy and consideration] like [you would] a stranger or a temporary resident [without property], so that he may live among you.
3_25_35.MSG: "If one of your brothers becomes indigent and cannot support himself, help him, the same as you would a foreigner or a guest so that he can continue to live in your neighborhood.
3_25_35.NIV: “‘If one of your countrymen becomes poor and is unable to support himself among you, help him as you would an alien or a temporary resident, so he can continue to live among you.
3_25_35.NRSVCE: If any of your kin fall into difficulty and become dependent on you, you shall support them; they shall live with you as though resident aliens.

3_25_36.NKJV: Take no usury or interest from him; but fear your God, that your brother may live with you.
3_25_36.KJV: Take thou no usury of him, or increase: but fear thy God; that thy brother may live with thee.
3_25_36.ESV: Take no interest from him or profit, but fear your God, that your brother may live beside you.
3_25_36.NASB: 'Do not take usurious interest from him, but revere your God, that your countryman may live with you.
3_25_36.NLT: Do not charge interest or make a profit at his expense. Instead, show your fear of God by letting him live with you as your relative.
3_25_36.WEB: Take no interest from him or profit, but fear your God; that your brother may live among you.
3_25_36.CSB17: Do not profit or take interest from him, but fear your God and let your brother live among you.
3_25_36.AMP: Do not charge him usurious interest, but fear your God [with profound reverence], so your countryman may [continue to] live among you.
3_25_36.MSG: Don't gouge him with interest charges; out of reverence for your God help your brother to continue to live with you in the neighborhood.
3_25_36.NIV: Do not take interest of any kind from him, but fear your God, so that your countryman may continue to live among you.
3_25_36.NRSVCE: Do not take interest in advance or otherwise make a profit from them, but fear your God; let them live with you.

3_25_37.NKJV: You shall not lend him your money for usury, nor lend him your food at a profit.
3_25_37.KJV: Thou shalt not give him thy money upon usury, nor lend him thy victuals for increase.
3_25_37.ESV: You shall not lend him your money at interest, nor give him your food for profit.
3_25_37.NASB: 'You shall not give him your silver at interest, nor your food for gain.
3_25_37.NLT: Remember, do not charge interest on money you lend him or make a profit on food you sell him.
3_25_37.WEB: You shall not lend him your money at interest, nor give him your food for profit.
3_25_37.CSB17: You are not to lend him your silver with interest or sell him your food for profit.
3_25_37.AMP: You shall not give him your money at interest, nor your food at a profit.
3_25_37.MSG: Don't take advantage of his plight by running up big interest charges on his loans, and don't give him food for profit.
3_25_37.NIV: You must not lend him money at interest or sell him food at a profit.
3_25_37.NRSVCE: You shall not lend them your money at interest taken in advance, or provide them food at a profit.

3_25_38.NKJV: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.KJV: I am the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.
3_25_38.ESV: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.
3_25_38.NASB: 'I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan [and] to be your God.
3_25_38.NLT: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.WEB: I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, and to be your God.
3_25_38.CSB17: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.AMP: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.MSG: I am your God who brought you out of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.NIV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of Egypt to give you the land of Canaan and to be your God.
3_25_38.NRSVCE: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to give you the land of Canaan, to be your God.

3_25_39.NKJV: ‘And if one of your brethren who dwells by you becomes poor, and sells himself to you, you shall not compel him to serve as a slave.
3_25_39.KJV: And if thy brother that dwelleth by thee be waxen poor, and be sold unto thee; thou shalt not compel him to serve as a bondservant: compel: Heb. serve thyself with him with the service, etc
3_25_39.ESV: “If your brother becomes poor beside you and sells himself to you, you shall not make him serve as a slave:
3_25_39.NASB: 'If a countryman of yours becomes so poor with regard to you that he sells himself to you, you shall not subject him to a slave's service.
3_25_39.NLT: “If one of your fellow Israelites falls into poverty and is forced to sell himself to you, do not treat him as a slave.
3_25_39.WEB: “‘If your brother has grown poor among you, and sells himself to you; you shall not make him to serve as a slave.
3_25_39.CSB17: “If your brother among you becomes destitute and sells himself to you, you must not force him to do slave labor.
3_25_39.AMP: 'And if your fellow countryman becomes so poor [in his dealings] with you that he sells himself to you [as payment for a debt], you shall not let him do the work of a slave [who is ineligible for redemption],
3_25_39.MSG: "If one of your brothers becomes indigent and has to sell himself to you, don't make him work as a slave.
3_25_39.NIV: “‘If one of your countrymen becomes poor among you and sells himself to you, do not make him work as a slave.
3_25_39.NRSVCE: If any who are dependent on you become so impoverished that they sell themselves to you, you shall not make them serve as slaves.

3_25_40.NKJV: As a hired servant and a sojourner he shall be with you, and shall serve you until the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_40.KJV: But as an hired servant, and as a sojourner, he shall be with thee, and shall serve thee unto the year of jubile:
3_25_40.ESV: he shall be with you as a hired worker and as a sojourner. He shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.
3_25_40.NASB: 'He shall be with you as a hired man, as if he were a sojourner; he shall serve with you until the year of jubilee.
3_25_40.NLT: Treat him instead as a hired worker or as a temporary resident who lives with you, and he will serve you only until the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_40.WEB: As a hired servant, and as a temporary resident, he shall be with you; he shall serve with you until the Year of Jubilee:
3_25_40.CSB17: Let him stay with you as a hired worker or temporary resident; he may work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_40.AMP: but he is to be with you as a hired man, as if he were a temporary resident; he shall serve with you until the Year of Jubilee,
3_25_40.MSG: Treat him as a hired hand or a guest among you. He will work for you until the Jubilee,
3_25_40.NIV: He is to be treated as a hired worker or a temporary resident among you; he is to work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_40.NRSVCE: They shall remain with you as hired or bound laborers. They shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.

3_25_41.NKJV: And then he shall depart from you—he and his children with him—and shall return to his own family. He shall return to the possession of his fathers.
3_25_41.KJV: And then shall he depart from thee, both he and his children with him, and shall return unto his own family, and unto the possession of his fathers shall he return.
3_25_41.ESV: Then he shall go out from you, he and his children with him, and go back to his own clan and return to the possession of his fathers.
3_25_41.NASB: 'He shall then go out from you, he and his sons with him, and shall go back to his family, that he may return to the property of his forefathers.
3_25_41.NLT: At that time he and his children will no longer be obligated to you, and they will return to their clans and go back to the land originally allotted to their ancestors.
3_25_41.WEB: then he shall go out from you, he and his children with him, and shall return to his own family, and to the possession of his fathers.
3_25_41.CSB17: Then he and his children are to be released from you, and he may return to his clan and his ancestral property.
3_25_41.AMP: and then he shall leave you, he and his children with him, and shall go back to his own family and return to the property of his fathers.
3_25_41.MSG: after which he and his children are set free to go back to his clan and his ancestral land.
3_25_41.NIV: Then he and his children are to be released, and he will go back to his own clan and to the property of his forefathers.
3_25_41.NRSVCE: Then they and their children with them shall be free from your authority; they shall go back to their own family and return to their ancestral property.

3_25_42.NKJV: For they are My servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.KJV: For they are my servants, which I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: they shall not be sold as bondmen. as: Heb. with the sale of a bondman
3_25_42.ESV: For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.NASB: 'For they are My servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt; they are not to be sold [in] a slave sale.
3_25_42.NLT: The people of Israel are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt, so they must never be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.WEB: For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. They shall not be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.CSB17: They are not to be sold as slaves, because they are my servants that I brought out of the land of Egypt.
3_25_42.AMP: For the Israelites are My servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold in a slave sale.
3_25_42.MSG: Because the People of Israel are my servants whom I brought out of Egypt, they must never be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.NIV: Because the Israelites are my servants, whom I brought out of Egypt, they must not be sold as slaves.
3_25_42.NRSVCE: For they are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as slaves are sold.

3_25_43.NKJV: You shall not rule over him with rigor, but you shall fear your God.
3_25_43.KJV: Thou shalt not rule over him with rigour; but shalt fear thy God.
3_25_43.ESV: You shall not rule over him ruthlessly but shall fear your God.
3_25_43.NASB: 'You shall not rule over him with severity, but are to revere your God.
3_25_43.NLT: Show your fear of God by not treating them harshly.
3_25_43.WEB: You shall not rule over him with harshness, but shall fear your God.
3_25_43.CSB17: You are not to rule over them harshly but fear your God.
3_25_43.AMP: You shall not rule over him with harshness (severity, oppression), but you are to fear your God [with profound reverence].
3_25_43.MSG: Don't tyrannize them; fear your God.
3_25_43.NIV: Do not rule over them ruthlessly, but fear your God.
3_25_43.NRSVCE: You shall not rule over them with harshness, but shall fear your God.

3_25_44.NKJV: And as for your male and female slaves whom you may have—from the nations that are around you, from them you may buy male and female slaves.
3_25_44.KJV: Both thy bondmen, and thy bondmaids, which thou shalt have, shall be of the heathen that are round about you; of them shall ye buy bondmen and bondmaids.
3_25_44.ESV: As for your male and female slaves whom you may have: you may buy male and female slaves from among the nations that are around you.
3_25_44.NASB: 'As for your male and female slaves whom you may have-- you may acquire male and female slaves from the pagan nations that are around you.
3_25_44.NLT: “However, you may purchase male and female slaves from among the nations around you.
3_25_44.WEB: “‘As for your male and your female slaves, whom you may have; of the nations that are around you, from them you may buy male and female slaves.
3_25_44.CSB17: Your male and female slaves are to be from the nations around you; you may purchase male and female slaves.
3_25_44.AMP: As for your male and female slaves whom you may have—you may acquire male and female slaves from the pagan nations that are around you.
3_25_44.MSG: "The male and female slaves which you have are to come from the surrounding nations; you are permitted to buy slaves from them.
3_25_44.NIV: “‘Your male and female slaves are to come from the nations around you; from them you may buy slaves.
3_25_44.NRSVCE: As for the male and female slaves whom you may have, it is from the nations around you that you may acquire male and female slaves.

3_25_45.NKJV: Moreover you may buy the children of the strangers who dwell among you, and their families who are with you, which they beget in your land; and they shall become your property.
3_25_45.KJV: Moreover of the children of the strangers that do sojourn among you, of them shall ye buy, and of their families that are with you, which they begat in your land: and they shall be your possession.
3_25_45.ESV: You may also buy from among the strangers who sojourn with you and their clans that are with you, who have been born in your land, and they may be your property.
3_25_45.NASB: 'Then, too, [it is] out of the sons of the sojourners who live as aliens among you that you may gain acquisition, and out of their families who are with you, whom they will have produced in your land; they also may become your possession.
3_25_45.NLT: You may also purchase the children of temporary residents who live among you, including those who have been born in your land. You may treat them as your property,
3_25_45.WEB: Moreover of the children of the aliens who live among you, of them you may buy, and of their families who are with you, which they have conceived in your land; and they will be your property.
3_25_45.CSB17: You may also purchase them from the aliens residing with you, or from their families living among you — those born in your land. These may become your property.
3_25_45.AMP: Moreover, from the children of the strangers who live as aliens among you, from them you may buy slaves and from their families who are with you, whom they have produced in your land; they may become your possession.
3_25_45.MSG: You may also buy the children of foreign workers who are living among you temporarily and from their clans which are living among you and have been born in your land. They become your property.
3_25_45.NIV: You may also buy some of the temporary residents living among you and members of their clans born in your country, and they will become your property.
3_25_45.NRSVCE: You may also acquire them from among the aliens residing with you, and from their families that are with you, who have been born in your land; and they may be your property.

3_25_46.NKJV: And you may take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherit them as a possession; they shall be your permanent slaves. But regarding your brethren, the children of Israel, you shall not rule over one another with rigor.
3_25_46.KJV: And ye shall take them as an inheritance for your children after you, to inherit them for a possession; they shall be your bondmen for ever: but over your brethren the children of Israel, ye shall not rule one over another with rigour. they: Heb. ye shall serve yourselves with them
3_25_46.ESV: You may bequeath them to your sons after you to inherit as a possession forever. You may make slaves of them, but over your brothers the people of Israel you shall not rule, one over another ruthlessly.
3_25_46.NASB: 'You may even bequeath them to your sons after you, to receive as a possession; you can use them as permanent slaves. But in respect to your countrymen, the sons of Israel, you shall not rule with severity over one another.
3_25_46.NLT: passing them on to your children as a permanent inheritance. You may treat them as slaves, but you must never treat your fellow Israelites this way.
3_25_46.WEB: You may make them an inheritance for your children after you, to hold for a possession; of them may you take your slaves forever; but over your brothers the children of Israel you shall not rule, one over another, with harshness.
3_25_46.CSB17: You may leave them to your sons after you to inherit as property; you can make them slaves for life. But concerning your brothers, the Israelites, you must not rule over one another harshly.
3_25_46.AMP: You may even bequeath them as an inheritance to your children after you, to receive as a possession; you can use them as permanent slaves. But in respect to your fellow countrymen, the children of Israel, you shall not rule over one another with harshness (severity, oppression).
3_25_46.MSG: You may will them to your children as property and make them slaves for life. But you must not tyrannize your brother Israelites.
3_25_46.NIV: You can will them to your children as inherited property and can make them slaves for life, but you must not rule over your fellow Israelites ruthlessly.
3_25_46.NRSVCE: You may keep them as a possession for your children after you, for them to inherit as property. These you may treat as slaves, but as for your fellow Israelites, no one shall rule over the other with harshness.

3_25_47.NKJV: ‘Now if a sojourner or stranger close to you becomes rich, and one of your brethren who dwells by him becomes poor, and sells himself to the stranger or sojourner close to you, or to a member of the stranger’s family,
3_25_47.KJV: And if a sojourner or stranger wax rich by thee, and thy brother that dwelleth by him wax poor, and sell himself unto the stranger or sojourner by thee, or to the stock of the stranger's family: wax rich: Heb. his hand obtain, etc
3_25_47.ESV: “If a stranger or sojourner with you becomes rich, and your brother beside him becomes poor and sells himself to the stranger or sojourner with you or to a member of the stranger's clan,
3_25_47.NASB: 'Now if the means of a stranger or of a sojourner with you becomes sufficient, and a countryman of yours becomes so poor with regard to him as to sell himself to a stranger who is sojourning with you, or to the descendants of a stranger's family,
3_25_47.NLT: “Suppose a foreigner or temporary resident becomes rich while living among you. If any of your fellow Israelites fall into poverty and are forced to sell themselves to such a foreigner or to a member of his family,
3_25_47.WEB: “‘If an alien or temporary resident with you becomes rich, and your brother beside him has grown poor, and sells himself to the stranger or foreigner living among you, or to a member of the stranger’s family;
3_25_47.CSB17: “If an alien or temporary resident living among you prospers, but your brother living near him becomes destitute and sells himself to the alien living among you, or to a member of the resident alien’s clan,
3_25_47.AMP: 'Now if the financial means of a stranger or temporary resident among you become sufficient, and your fellow countryman becomes poor in comparison to him and sells himself to the stranger who is living among you or to the descendants of the stranger's family,
3_25_47.MSG: "If a foreigner or temporary resident among you becomes rich and one of your brothers becomes poor and sells himself to the foreigner who lives among you or to a member of the foreigner's clan,
3_25_47.NIV: “‘If an alien or a temporary resident among you becomes rich and one of your countrymen becomes poor and sells himself to the alien living among you or to a member of the alien’s clan,
3_25_47.NRSVCE: If resident aliens among you prosper, and if any of your kin fall into difficulty with one of them and sell themselves to an alien, or to a branch of the alien’s family,

3_25_48.NKJV: after he is sold he may be redeemed again. One of his brothers may redeem him;
3_25_48.KJV: After that he is sold he may be redeemed again; one of his brethren may redeem him:
3_25_48.ESV: then after he is sold he may be redeemed. One of his brothers may redeem him,
3_25_48.NASB: then he shall have redemption right after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him,
3_25_48.NLT: they still retain the right to be bought back, even after they have been purchased. They may be bought back by a brother,

3_25_48.CSB17: he has the right of redemption after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him.
3_25_48.AMP: then after he is sold he shall have the right of redemption. One of his relatives may redeem him:

3_25_49.NKJV: or his uncle or his uncle’s son may redeem him; or anyone who is near of kin to him in his family may redeem him; or if he is able he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.KJV: Either his uncle, or his uncle's son, may redeem him, or any that is nigh of kin unto him of his family may redeem him; or if he be able, he may redeem himself.
