3_25_49.NASB: or his uncle, or his uncle's son, may redeem him, or one of his blood relatives from his family may redeem him; or if he prospers, he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.NLT: an uncle, or a cousin. In fact, anyone from the extended family may buy them back. They may also redeem themselves if they have prospered.
3_25_49.WEB: or his uncle, or his uncle’s son, may redeem him, or any who is a close relative to him of his family may redeem him; or if he has grown rich, he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.CSB17: His uncle or cousin may redeem him, or any of his close relatives from his clan may redeem him. If he prospers, he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.AMP: either his uncle or his uncle's son may redeem him, or one of his blood relatives from his family may redeem him; or if he prospers, he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.MSG: An uncle or cousin or any close relative of his extended family may redeem him. Or, if he gets the money together, he can redeem himself.
3_25_49.NIV: An uncle or a cousin or any blood relative in his clan may redeem him. Or if he prospers, he may redeem himself.
3_25_49.NRSVCE: or their uncle or their uncle’s son may redeem them, or anyone of their family who is of their own flesh may redeem them; or if they prosper they may redeem themselves.

3_25_50.NKJV: Thus he shall reckon with him who bought him: The price of his release shall be according to the number of years, from the year that he was sold to him until the Year of Jubilee; it shall be according to the time of a hired servant for him.
3_25_50.KJV: And he shall reckon with him that bought him from the year that he was sold to him unto the year of jubile: and the price of his sale shall be according unto the number of years, according to the time of an hired servant shall it be with him.
3_25_50.ESV: He shall calculate with his buyer from the year when he sold himself to him until the year of jubilee, and the price of his sale shall vary with the number of years. The time he was with his owner shall be rated as the time of a hired worker.
3_25_50.NASB: 'He then with his purchaser shall calculate from the year when he sold himself to him up to the year of jubilee; and the price of his sale shall correspond to the number of years. [It is] like the days of a hired man [that] he shall be with him.
3_25_50.NLT: They will negotiate the price of their freedom with the person who bought them. The price will be based on the number of years from the time they were sold until the next Year of Jubilee — whatever it would cost to hire a worker for that period of time.
3_25_50.WEB: He shall reckon with him who bought him from the year that he sold himself to him to the Year of Jubilee. The price of his sale shall be according to the number of years; he shall be with him according to the time of a hired servant.
3_25_50.CSB17: The one who purchased him is to calculate the time from the year he sold himself to him until the Year of Jubilee. The price of his sale will be determined by the number of years. It will be set for him like the daily wages of a hired worker.
3_25_50.AMP: Then he [or his redeemer] shall calculate with his purchaser from the year when he sold himself to the purchaser to the Year of Jubilee, and the [original] price of his sale shall be adjusted according to the number of years. The time he was with his owner shall be considered as that of a hired man.
3_25_50.MSG: What happens then is that he and his owner count out the time from the year he sold himself to the year of Jubilee; the buy-back price is set according to the wages of a hired hand for that number of years.
3_25_50.NIV: He and his buyer are to count the time from the year he sold himself up to the Year of Jubilee. The price for his release is to be based on the rate paid to a hired man for that number of years.
3_25_50.NRSVCE: They shall compute with the purchaser the total from the year when they sold themselves to the alien until the jubilee year; the price of the sale shall be applied to the number of years: the time they were with the owner shall be rated as the time of a hired laborer.

3_25_51.NKJV: If there are still many years remaining, according to them he shall repay the price of his redemption from the money with which he was bought.
3_25_51.KJV: If there be yet many years behind, according unto them he shall give again the price of his redemption out of the money that he was bought for.
3_25_51.ESV: If there are still many years left, he shall pay proportionately for his redemption some of his sale price.
3_25_51.NASB: 'If there are still many years, he shall refund part of his purchase price in proportion to them for his own redemption;
3_25_51.NLT: If many years still remain until the jubilee, they will repay the proper proportion of what they received when they sold themselves.
3_25_51.WEB: If there are yet many years, according to them he shall give back the price of his redemption out of the money that he was bought for.
3_25_51.CSB17: If many years are still left, he must pay his redemption price in proportion to them based on his purchase price.
3_25_51.AMP: If there are still many years [before the Year of Jubilee], in proportion to them he must refund [to the purchaser] part of the price of his sale for his redemption and release.
3_25_51.MSG: If many years remain before the Jubilee, he must pay back a larger share of his purchase price,
3_25_51.NIV: If many years remain, he must pay for his redemption a larger share of the price paid for him.
3_25_51.NRSVCE: If many years remain, they shall pay for their redemption in proportion to the purchase price;

3_25_52.NKJV: And if there remain but a few years until the Year of Jubilee, then he shall reckon with him, and according to his years he shall repay him the price of his redemption.
3_25_52.KJV: And if there remain but few years unto the year of jubile, then he shall count with him, and according unto his years shall he give him again the price of his redemption.
3_25_52.ESV: If there remain but a few years until the year of jubilee, he shall calculate and pay for his redemption in proportion to his years of service.
3_25_52.NASB: and if few years remain until the year of jubilee, he shall so calculate with him. In proportion to his years he is to refund [the amount for] his redemption.
3_25_52.NLT: If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee, they will repay a small amount for their redemption.
3_25_52.WEB: If there remain but a few years to the year of jubilee, then he shall reckon with him; according to his years of service he shall give back the price of his redemption.
3_25_52.CSB17: If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee, he will calculate and pay the price of his redemption in proportion to his remaining years.
3_25_52.AMP: And if only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee, he shall so calculate it with him. He is to refund the proportionate amount for his release.
3_25_52.MSG: but if only a few years remain until the Jubilee, he is to calculate his redemption price accordingly.
3_25_52.NIV: If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee, he is to compute that and pay for his redemption accordingly.
3_25_52.NRSVCE: and if few years remain until the jubilee year, they shall compute thus: according to the years involved they shall make payment for their redemption.

3_25_53.NKJV: He shall be with him as a yearly hired servant, and he shall not rule with rigor over him in your sight.
3_25_53.KJV: And as a yearly hired servant shall he be with him: and the other shall not rule with rigour over him in thy sight.
3_25_53.ESV: He shall treat him as a worker hired year by year. He shall not rule ruthlessly over him in your sight.
3_25_53.NASB: 'Like a man hired year by year he shall be with him; he shall not rule over him with severity in your sight.
3_25_53.NLT: The foreigner must treat them as workers hired on a yearly basis. You must not allow a foreigner to treat any of your fellow Israelites harshly.
3_25_53.WEB: As a servant hired year by year shall he be with him. He shall not rule with harshness over him in your sight.
3_25_53.CSB17: He will stay with him like a man hired year by year. A resident alien is not to rule over him harshly in your sight.
3_25_53.AMP: Like a man hired year by year he shall deal with him; he shall not rule over him with harshness in your sight.
3_25_53.MSG: He is to be treated as a man hired from year to year. You must make sure that his owner does not tyrannize him.
3_25_53.NIV: He is to be treated as a man hired from year to year; you must see to it that his owner does not rule over him ruthlessly.
3_25_53.NRSVCE: As a laborer hired by the year they shall be under the alien’s authority, who shall not, however, rule with harshness over them in your sight.

3_25_54.NKJV: And if he is not redeemed in these years, then he shall be released in the Year of Jubilee—he and his children with him.
3_25_54.KJV: And if he be not redeemed in these years, then he shall go out in the year of jubile, both he, and his children with him. in these: or, by these means
3_25_54.ESV: And if he is not redeemed by these means, then he and his children with him shall be released in the year of jubilee.
3_25_54.NASB: 'Even if he is not redeemed by these [means], he shall still go out in the year of jubilee, he and his sons with him.
3_25_54.NLT: If any Israelites have not been bought back by the time the Year of Jubilee arrives, they and their children must be set free at that time.
3_25_54.WEB: If he isn’t redeemed by these means, then he shall be released in the Year of Jubilee, he, and his children with him.
3_25_54.CSB17: If he is not redeemed in any of these ways, he and his children are to be released at the Year of Jubilee.
3_25_54.AMP: Even if he is not redeemed during these years and under these provisions, then he shall go free in the Year of Jubilee, he and his children with him.
3_25_54.MSG: "If he is not redeemed in any of these ways, he goes free in the year of Jubilee, he and his children,
3_25_54.NIV: “‘Even if he is not redeemed in any of these ways, he and his children are to be released in the Year of Jubilee,
3_25_54.NRSVCE: And if they have not been redeemed in any of these ways, they and their children with them shall go free in the jubilee year.

3_25_55.NKJV: For the children of Israel are servants to Me; they are My servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.KJV: For unto me the children of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.ESV: For it is to me that the people of Israel are servants. They are my servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.NASB: 'For the sons of Israel are My servants; they are My servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.NLT: For the people of Israel belong to me. They are my servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.WEB: For to me the children of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. I am Yahweh your God.
3_25_55.CSB17: For the Israelites are my servants. They are my servants that I brought out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.AMP: For the children of Israel are My servants; My servants, whom I brought out of the land of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
3_25_55.MSG: because the People of Israel are my servants, my servants whom I brought out of Egypt. I am God, your God.
3_25_55.NIV: for the Israelites belong to me as servants. They are my servants, whom I brought out of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
3_25_55.NRSVCE: For to me the people of Israel are servants; they are my servants whom I brought out from the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.

3_26_1.NKJV: ‘You shall not make idols for yourselves; neither a carved image nor a sacred pillar shall you rear up for yourselves; nor shall you set up an engraved stone in your land, to bow down to it; for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.KJV: Ye shall make you no idols nor graven image, neither rear you up a standing image, neither shall ye set up any image of stone in your land, to bow down unto it: for I am the LORD your God. standing: or, pillar image of: or, figured stone: Heb. a stone of picture
3_26_1.ESV: “You shall not make idols for yourselves or erect an image or pillar, and you shall not set up a figured stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.NASB: 'You shall not make for yourselves idols, nor shall you set up for yourselves an image or a [sacred] pillar, nor shall you place a figured stone in your land to bow down to it; for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.NLT: “Do not make idols or set up carved images, or sacred pillars, or sculptured stones in your land so you may worship them. I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.WEB: “‘You shall make for yourselves no idols, and you shall not raise up an engraved image or a pillar, and you shall not place any figured stone in your land, to bow down to it; for I am Yahweh your God.
3_26_1.CSB17: “Do not make idols for yourselves, set up a carved image or sacred pillar for yourselves, or place a sculpted stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.AMP: 'You shall not make idols for yourselves, nor shall you erect an image, a sacred pillar or an obelisk, nor shall you place any figured stone in your land so that you may bow down to it; for I am the LORD your God.
3_26_1.MSG: "Don't make idols for yourselves; don't set up an image or a sacred pillar for yourselves, and don't place a carved stone in your land that you can bow down to in worship. I am God, your God.
3_26_1.NIV: Reward for Obedience“‘Do not make idols or set up an image or a sacred stone for yourselves, and do not place a carved stone in your land to bow down before it. I am the Lord your God.
3_26_1.NRSVCE: You shall make for yourselves no idols and erect no carved images or pillars, and you shall not place figured stones in your land, to worship at them; for I am the LORD your God.

3_26_2.NKJV: You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary: I am the LORD.
3_26_2.KJV: Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
3_26_2.ESV: You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
3_26_2.NASB: 'You shall keep My sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary; I am the LORD.
3_26_2.NLT: You must keep my Sabbath days of rest and show reverence for my sanctuary. I am the LORD.
3_26_2.WEB: “‘You shall keep my Sabbaths, and have reverence for my sanctuary. I am Yahweh.
3_26_2.CSB17: Keep my Sabbaths and revere my sanctuary; I am the LORD.
3_26_2.AMP: You shall keep My Sabbaths and have reverence for My sanctuary. I am the LORD.
3_26_2.MSG: "Keep my Sabbaths; treat my Sanctuary with reverence. I am God.
3_26_2.NIV: “‘Observe my Sabbaths and have reverence for my sanctuary. I am the Lord.
3_26_2.NRSVCE: You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.

3_26_3.NKJV: ‘If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments, and perform them,
3_26_3.KJV: If ye walk in my statutes, and keep my commandments, and do them;
3_26_3.ESV: “If you walk in my statutes and observe my commandments and do them,
3_26_3.NASB: 'If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments so as to carry them out,
3_26_3.NLT: “If you follow my decrees and are careful to obey my commands,
3_26_3.WEB: “‘If you walk in my statutes, and keep my commandments, and do them;
3_26_3.CSB17: “If you follow my statutes and faithfully observe my commands,
3_26_3.AMP: If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments and [obediently] do them,
3_26_3.MSG: "If you live by my decrees and obediently keep my commandments,
3_26_3.NIV: “‘If you follow my decrees and are careful to obey my commands,
3_26_3.NRSVCE: If you follow my statutes and keep my commandments and observe them faithfully,

3_26_4.NKJV: then I will give you rain in its season, the land shall yield its produce, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
3_26_4.KJV: Then I will give you rain in due season, and the land shall yield her increase, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
3_26_4.ESV: then I will give you your rains in their season, and the land shall yield its increase, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
3_26_4.NASB: then I shall give you rains in their season, so that the land will yield its produce and the trees of the field will bear their fruit.
3_26_4.NLT: I will send you the seasonal rains. The land will then yield its crops, and the trees of the field will produce their fruit.
3_26_4.WEB: then I will give you your rains in their season, and the land shall yield its increase, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.
3_26_4.CSB17: I will give you rain at the right time, and the land will yield its produce, and the trees of the field will bear their fruit.
3_26_4.AMP: then I will give you rain in its season, and the land will yield her produce and the trees of the field bear their fruit.
3_26_4.MSG: I will send the rains in their seasons, the ground will yield its crops and the trees of the field their fruit.
3_26_4.NIV: I will send you rain in its season, and the ground will yield its crops and the trees of the field their fruit.
3_26_4.NRSVCE: I will give you your rains in their season, and the land shall yield its produce, and the trees of the field shall yield their fruit.

3_26_5.NKJV: Your threshing shall last till the time of vintage, and the vintage shall last till the time of sowing; you shall eat your bread to the full, and dwell in your land safely.
3_26_5.KJV: And your threshing shall reach unto the vintage, and the vintage shall reach unto the sowing time: and ye shall eat your bread to the full, and dwell in your land safely.
3_26_5.ESV: Your threshing shall last to the time of the grape harvest, and the grape harvest shall last to the time for sowing. And you shall eat your bread to the full and dwell in your land securely.
3_26_5.NASB: 'Indeed, your threshing will last for you until grape gathering, and grape gathering will last until sowing time. You will thus eat your food to the full and live securely in your land.
3_26_5.NLT: Your threshing season will overlap with the grape harvest, and your grape harvest will overlap with the season of planting grain. You will eat your fill and live securely in your own land.
3_26_5.WEB: Your threshing shall reach to the vintage, and the vintage shall reach to the sowing time. You shall eat your bread to the full, and dwell in your land safely.
3_26_5.CSB17: Your threshing will continue until grape harvest, and the grape harvest will continue until sowing time; you will have plenty of food to eat and live securely in your land.
3_26_5.AMP: And your threshing season will last until grape gathering and the grape gathering [time] will last until planting, and you will eat your bread and be filled and live securely in your land.
3_26_5.MSG: You will thresh until the grape harvest and the grape harvest will continue until planting time; you'll have more than enough to eat and will live safe and secure in your land.
3_26_5.NIV: Your threshing will continue until grape harvest and the grape harvest will continue until planting, and you will eat all the food you want and live in safety in your land.
3_26_5.NRSVCE: Your threshing shall overtake the vintage, and the vintage shall overtake the sowing; you shall eat your bread to the full, and live securely in your land.

3_26_6.NKJV: I will give peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and none will make you afraid; I will rid the land of evil beasts, and the sword will not go through your land.
3_26_6.KJV: And I will give peace in the land, and ye shall lie down, and none shall make you afraid: and I will rid evil beasts out of the land, neither shall the sword go through your land. rid: Heb. cause to cease
3_26_6.ESV: I will give peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and none shall make you afraid. And I will remove harmful beasts from the land, and the sword shall not go through your land.
3_26_6.NASB: 'I shall also grant peace in the land, so that you may lie down with no one making [you] tremble. I shall also eliminate harmful beasts from the land, and no sword will pass through your land.
3_26_6.NLT: “I will give you peace in the land, and you will be able to sleep with no cause for fear. I will rid the land of wild animals and keep your enemies out of your land.
3_26_6.WEB: “‘I will give peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and no one will make you afraid. I will remove evil animals out of the land, neither shall the sword go through your land.
3_26_6.CSB17: I will give peace to the land, and you will lie down with nothing to frighten you. I will remove dangerous animals from the land, and no sword will pass through your land.
3_26_6.AMP: I will also grant peace in the land, so that you may lie down and there will be no one to make you afraid. I will also eliminate harmful animals from the land, and no sword will pass through your land.
3_26_6.MSG: "I'll make the country a place of peace—you'll be able to go to sleep at night without fear; I'll get rid of the wild beasts; I'll eliminate war.
3_26_6.NIV: “‘I will grant peace in the land, and you will lie down and no one will make you afraid. I will remove savage beasts from the land, and the sword will not pass through your country.
3_26_6.NRSVCE: And I will grant peace in the land, and you shall lie down, and no one shall make you afraid; I will remove dangerous animals from the land, and no sword shall go through your land.

3_26_7.NKJV: You will chase your enemies, and they shall fall by the sword before you.
3_26_7.KJV: And ye shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_7.ESV: You shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_7.NASB: 'But you will chase your enemies and they will fall before you by the sword;
3_26_7.NLT: In fact, you will chase down your enemies and slaughter them with your swords.
3_26_7.WEB: You shall chase your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_7.CSB17: You will pursue your enemies, and they will fall before you by the sword.
3_26_7.AMP: And you will chase your enemies, and they will fall before you by the sword.
3_26_7.MSG: You'll chase out your enemies and defeat them:
3_26_7.NIV: You will pursue your enemies, and they will fall by the sword before you.
3_26_7.NRSVCE: You shall give chase to your enemies, and they shall fall before you by the sword.

3_26_8.NKJV: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight; your enemies shall fall by the sword before you.
3_26_8.KJV: And five of you shall chase an hundred, and an hundred of you shall put ten thousand to flight: and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.ESV: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall chase ten thousand, and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.NASB: five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will chase ten thousand, and your enemies will fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.NLT: Five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will chase ten thousand! All your enemies will fall beneath your sword.
3_26_8.WEB: Five of you shall chase a hundred, and a hundred of you shall chase ten thousand; and your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.CSB17: Five of you will pursue a hundred, and a hundred of you will pursue ten thousand; your enemies will fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.AMP: Five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will put ten thousand to flight; your enemies will fall before you by the sword.
3_26_8.MSG: Five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will chase ten thousand and do away with them.
3_26_8.NIV: Five of you will chase a hundred, and a hundred of you will chase ten thousand, and your enemies will fall by the sword before you.
3_26_8.NRSVCE: Five of you shall give chase to a hundred, and a hundred of you shall give chase to ten thousand; your enemies shall fall before you by the sword.

3_26_9.NKJV: ‘For I will look on you favorably and make you fruitful, multiply you and confirm My covenant with you.
3_26_9.KJV: For I will have respect unto you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you, and establish my covenant with you.
3_26_9.ESV: I will turn to you and make you fruitful and multiply you and will confirm my covenant with you.
3_26_9.NASB: 'So I will turn toward you and make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will confirm My covenant with you.
3_26_9.NLT: “I will look favorably upon you, making you fertile and multiplying your people. And I will fulfill my covenant with you.
3_26_9.WEB: “‘I will have respect for you, and make you fruitful, and multiply you, and will establish my covenant with you.
3_26_9.CSB17: “I will turn to you, make you fruitful and multiply you, and confirm my covenant with you.
3_26_9.AMP: For I will turn toward you [with favor and regard] and make you fruitful and multiply you, and I will establish and confirm My covenant with you.
3_26_9.MSG: I'll give you my full attention: I'll make sure you prosper, make sure you grow in numbers, and keep my covenant with you in good working order.
3_26_9.NIV: “‘I will look on you with favor and make you fruitful and increase your numbers, and I will keep my covenant with you.
3_26_9.NRSVCE: I will look with favor upon you and make you fruitful and multiply you; and I will maintain my covenant with you.

3_26_10.NKJV: You shall eat the old harvest, and clear out the old because of the new.
3_26_10.KJV: And ye shall eat old store, and bring forth the old because of the new.
3_26_10.ESV: You shall eat old store long kept, and you shall clear out the old to make way for the new.
3_26_10.NASB: 'You will eat the old supply and clear out the old because of the new.
3_26_10.NLT: You will have such a surplus of crops that you will need to clear out the old grain to make room for the new harvest!
3_26_10.WEB: You shall eat old store long kept, and you shall move out the old because of the new.
3_26_10.CSB17: You will eat the old grain of the previous year and will clear out the old to make room for the new.
3_26_10.AMP: You will eat the old supply of [abundant] produce, and clear out the old [to make room] for the new.
3_26_10.MSG: You'll still be eating from last year's harvest when you have to clean out the barns to make room for the new crops.
3_26_10.NIV: You will still be eating last year’s harvest when you will have to move it out to make room for the new.
3_26_10.NRSVCE: You shall eat old grain long stored, and you shall have to clear out the old to make way for the new.

3_26_11.NKJV: I will set My tabernacle among you, and My soul shall not abhor you.
3_26_11.KJV: And I will set my tabernacle among you: and my soul shall not abhor you.
3_26_11.ESV: I will make my dwelling among you, and my soul shall not abhor you.
3_26_11.NASB: 'Moreover, I will make My dwelling among you, and My soul will not reject you.
3_26_11.NLT: I will live among you, and I will not despise you.
3_26_11.WEB: I will set my tent among you, and my soul won’t abhor you.
3_26_11.CSB17: I will place my residence among you, and I will not reject you.
3_26_11.AMP: I will make My dwelling among you, and My soul will not reject nor separate itself from you.
3_26_11.MSG: "I'll set up my residence in your neighborhood; I won't avoid or shun you;
3_26_11.NIV: I will put my dwelling place among you, and I will not abhor you.
3_26_11.NRSVCE: I will place my dwelling in your midst, and I shall not abhor you.

3_26_12.NKJV: I will walk among you and be your God, and you shall be My people.
3_26_12.KJV: And I will walk among you, and will be your God, and ye shall be my people.
3_26_12.ESV: And I will walk among you and will be your God, and you shall be my people.
3_26_12.NASB: 'I will also walk among you and be your God, and you shall be My people.
3_26_12.NLT: I will walk among you; I will be your God, and you will be my people.
3_26_12.WEB: I will walk among you, and will be your God, and you will be my people.
3_26_12.CSB17: I will walk among you and be your God, and you will be my people.
3_26_12.AMP: I will walk among you and be your God, and you shall be My people.
3_26_12.MSG: I'll stroll through your streets. I'll be your God; you'll be my people.
3_26_12.NIV: I will walk among you and be your God, and you will be my people.
3_26_12.NRSVCE: And I will walk among you, and will be your God, and you shall be my people.

3_26_13.NKJV: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that you should not be their slaves; I have broken the bands of your yoke and made you walk upright.
3_26_13.KJV: I am the LORD your God, which brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that ye should not be their bondmen; and I have broken the bands of your yoke, and made you go upright.
3_26_13.ESV: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that you should not be their slaves. And I have broken the bars of your yoke and made you walk erect.
3_26_13.NASB: 'I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt so that [you] would not be their slaves, and I broke the bars of your yoke and made you walk erect.
3_26_13.NLT: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt so you would no longer be their slaves. I broke the yoke of slavery from your neck so you can walk with your heads held high.
3_26_13.WEB: I am Yahweh your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, that you should not be their slaves. I have broken the bars of your yoke, and made you go upright.
3_26_13.CSB17: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, so that you would no longer be their slaves. I broke the bars of your yoke and enabled you to live in freedom.
3_26_13.AMP: I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt so that you would not be their slaves; and I broke the bars of your yoke and made you walk upright [with heads held high as free men].
3_26_13.MSG: I am God, your personal God who rescued you from Egypt so that you would no longer be slaves to the Egyptians. I ripped off the harness of your slavery so that you can move about freely.
3_26_13.NIV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of Egypt so that you would no longer be slaves to the Egyptians; I broke the bars of your yoke and enabled you to walk with heads held high.
3_26_13.NRSVCE: I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be their slaves no more; I have broken the bars of your yoke and made you walk erect.

3_26_14.NKJV: ‘But if you do not obey Me, and do not observe all these commandments,
3_26_14.KJV: But if ye will not hearken unto me, and will not do all these commandments;
3_26_14.ESV: “But if you will not listen to me and will not do all these commandments,
3_26_14.NASB: 'But if you do not obey Me and do not carry out all these commandments,
3_26_14.NLT: “However, if you do not listen to me or obey all these commands,
3_26_14.WEB: “‘But if you will not listen to me, and will not do all these commandments;
3_26_14.CSB17: “But if you do not obey me and observe all these commands —
3_26_14.AMP: 'But if you do not obey Me and do not [obediently] do all these commandments,
3_26_14.MSG: "But if you refuse to obey me and won't observe my commandments,
3_26_14.NIV: Punishment for Disobedience“‘But if you will not listen to me and carry out all these commands,
3_26_14.NRSVCE: But if you will not obey me, and do not observe all these commandments,

3_26_15.NKJV: and if you despise My statutes, or if your soul abhors My judgments, so that you do not perform all My commandments, but break My covenant,
3_26_15.KJV: And if ye shall despise my statutes, or if your soul abhor my judgments, so that ye will not do all my commandments, but that ye break my covenant:
3_26_15.ESV: if you spurn my statutes, and if your soul abhors my rules, so that you will not do all my commandments, but break my covenant,
3_26_15.NASB: if, instead, you reject My statutes, and if your soul abhors My ordinances so as not to carry out all My commandments, [and] so break My covenant,
3_26_15.NLT: and if you break my covenant by rejecting my decrees, treating my regulations with contempt, and refusing to obey my commands,
3_26_15.WEB: and if you shall reject my statutes, and if your soul abhors my ordinances, so that you will not do all my commandments, but break my covenant;
3_26_15.CSB17: if you reject my statutes and despise my ordinances, and do not observe all my commands — and break my covenant,
3_26_15.AMP: if, instead, you reject My statutes, and if your soul rejects My ordinances, so that you will not [obediently] do all My commandments, and in this way break My covenant,
3_26_15.MSG: despising my decrees and holding my laws in contempt by your disobedience, making a shambles of my covenant,
3_26_15.NIV: and if you reject my decrees and abhor my laws and fail to carry out all my commands and so violate my covenant,
3_26_15.NRSVCE: if you spurn my statutes, and abhor my ordinances, so that you will not observe all my commandments, and you break my covenant,

3_26_16.NKJV: I also will do this to you: I will even appoint terror over you, wasting disease and fever which shall consume the eyes and cause sorrow of heart. And you shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
3_26_16.KJV: I also will do this unto you; I will even appoint over you terror, consumption, and the burning ague, that shall consume the eyes, and cause sorrow of heart: and ye shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it. over: Heb. upon
3_26_16.ESV: then I will do this to you: I will visit you with panic, with wasting disease and fever that consume the eyes and make the heart ache. And you shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.
3_26_16.NASB: I, in turn, will do this to you: I will appoint over you a sudden terror, consumption and fever that will waste away the eyes and cause the soul to pine away; also, you will sow your seed uselessly, for your enemies will eat it up.
3_26_16.NLT: I will punish you. I will bring sudden terrors upon you — wasting diseases and burning fevers that will cause your eyes to fail and your life to ebb away. You will plant your crops in vain because your enemies will eat them.
3_26_16.WEB: I also will do this to you: I will appoint terror over you, even consumption and fever, that shall consume the eyes, and make the soul to pine away. You will sow your seed in vain, for your enemies will eat it.
3_26_16.CSB17: then I will do this to you: I will bring terror on you — wasting disease and fever that will cause your eyes to fail and your life to ebb away. You will sow your seed in vain because your enemies will eat it.
3_26_16.AMP: I, in turn, will do this to you: I will appoint over you sudden terror, consumption, and fever that will waste away the eyes and cause the soul to languish also. And you will sow your seed uselessly, for your enemies will eat what you plant.
3_26_16.MSG: I'll step in and pour on the trouble: debilitating disease, high fevers, blindness, your life leaking out bit by bit. You'll plant seed but your enemies will eat the crops.
3_26_16.NIV: then I will do this to you: I will bring upon you sudden terror, wasting diseases and fever that will destroy your sight and drain away your life. You will plant seed in vain, because your enemies will eat it.
3_26_16.NRSVCE: I in turn will do this to you: I will bring terror on you; consumption and fever that waste the eyes and cause life to pine away. You shall sow your seed in vain, for your enemies shall eat it.

3_26_17.NKJV: I will set My face against you, and you shall be defeated by your enemies. Those who hate you shall reign over you, and you shall flee when no one pursues you.
3_26_17.KJV: And I will set my face against you, and ye shall be slain before your enemies: they that hate you shall reign over you; and ye shall flee when none pursueth you.
3_26_17.ESV: I will set my face against you, and you shall be struck down before your enemies. Those who hate you shall rule over you, and you shall flee when none pursues you.
3_26_17.NASB: 'I will set My face against you so that you will be struck down before your enemies; and those who hate you will rule over you, and you will flee when no one is pursuing you.
3_26_17.NLT: I will turn against you, and you will be defeated by your enemies. Those who hate you will rule over you, and you will run even when no one is chasing you!
3_26_17.WEB: I will set my face against you, and you will be struck before your enemies. Those who hate you will rule over you; and you will flee when no one pursues you.
3_26_17.CSB17: I will turn against you, so that you will be defeated by your enemies. Those who hate you will rule over you, and you will flee even though no one is pursuing you.
3_26_17.AMP: I will set My face against you so that you will be struck down before your enemies; those who hate you will rule over you, and you will flee when no one is pursuing you.
3_26_17.MSG: I'll turn my back on you and stand by while your enemies defeat you. People who hate you will govern you. You'll run scared even when there's no one chasing you.
3_26_17.NIV: I will set my face against you so that you will be defeated by your enemies; those who hate you will rule over you, and you will flee even when no one is pursuing you.
3_26_17.NRSVCE: I will set my face against you, and you shall be struck down by your enemies; your foes shall rule over you, and you shall flee though no one pursues you.

3_26_18.NKJV: ‘And after all this, if you do not obey Me, then I will punish you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_18.KJV: And if ye will not yet for all this hearken unto me, then I will punish you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_18.ESV: And if in spite of this you will not listen to me, then I will discipline you again sevenfold for your sins,
3_26_18.NASB: 'If also after these things you do not obey Me, then I will punish you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_18.NLT: “And if, in spite of all this, you still disobey me, I will punish you seven times over for your sins.
3_26_18.WEB: “‘If you in spite of these things will not listen to me, then I will chastise you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_18.CSB17: “But if after these things you will not obey me, I will proceed to discipline you seven times for your sins.
3_26_18.AMP: If in spite of all this you still will not listen to Me and be obedient, then I will punish you seven times more for your sins.
3_26_18.MSG: "And if none of this works in getting your attention, I'll discipline you seven times over for your sins.
3_26_18.NIV: “‘If after all this you will not listen to me, I will punish you for your sins seven times over.
3_26_18.NRSVCE: And if in spite of this you will not obey me, I will continue to punish you sevenfold for your sins.

3_26_19.NKJV: I will break the pride of your power; I will make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze.
3_26_19.KJV: And I will break the pride of your power; and I will make your heaven as iron, and your earth as brass:
3_26_19.ESV: and I will break the pride of your power, and I will make your heavens like iron and your earth like bronze.
3_26_19.NASB: 'I will also break down your pride of power; I will also make your sky like iron and your earth like bronze.
3_26_19.NLT: I will break your proud spirit by making the skies as unyielding as iron and the earth as hard as bronze.
3_26_19.WEB: I will break the pride of your power, and I will make your sky like iron, and your soil like brass.
3_26_19.CSB17: I will break down your strong pride. I will make your sky like iron and your land like bronze,
3_26_19.AMP: I will break your pride in your power, and I will make your sky like iron [giving no rain and blocking all prayers] and your ground like bronze [hard to plow and yielding no produce].
3_26_19.MSG: I'll break your strong pride: I'll make the skies above you like a sheet of tin and the ground under you like cast iron.
3_26_19.NIV: I will break down your stubborn pride and make the sky above you like iron and the ground beneath you like bronze.
3_26_19.NRSVCE: I will break your proud glory, and I will make your sky like iron and your earth like copper.

3_26_20.NKJV: And your strength shall be spent in vain; for your land shall not yield its produce, nor shall the trees of the land yield their fruit.
3_26_20.KJV: And your strength shall be spent in vain: for your land shall not yield her increase, neither shall the trees of the land yield their fruits.
3_26_20.ESV: And your strength shall be spent in vain, for your land shall not yield its increase, and the trees of the land shall not yield their fruit.
3_26_20.NASB: 'Your strength will be spent uselessly, for your land will not yield its produce and the trees of the land will not yield their fruit.
3_26_20.NLT: All your work will be for nothing, for your land will yield no crops, and your trees will bear no fruit.
3_26_20.WEB: Your strength will be spent in vain; for your land won’t yield its increase, neither will the trees of the land yield their fruit.
3_26_20.CSB17: and your strength will be used up for nothing. Your land will not yield its produce, and the trees of the land will not bear their fruit.
3_26_20.AMP: Your strength will be spent uselessly, for your land will not yield its produce and the trees of the land will not yield their fruit.
3_26_20.MSG: No matter how hard you work, nothing will come of it: No crops out of the ground, no fruit off the trees.
3_26_20.NIV: Your strength will be spent in vain, because your soil will not yield its crops, nor will the trees of the land yield their fruit.
3_26_20.NRSVCE: Your strength shall be spent to no purpose: your land shall not yield its produce, and the trees of the land shall not yield their fruit.

3_26_21.NKJV: ‘Then, if you walk contrary to Me, and are not willing to obey Me, I will bring on you seven times more plagues, according to your sins.
3_26_21.KJV: And if ye walk contrary unto me, and will not hearken unto me; I will bring seven times more plagues upon you according to your sins. contrary: or, at all adventures with me
3_26_21.ESV: “Then if you walk contrary to me and will not listen to me, I will continue striking you, sevenfold for your sins.
3_26_21.NASB: 'If then, you act with hostility against Me and are unwilling to obey Me, I will increase the plague on you seven times according to your sins.
3_26_21.NLT: “If even then you remain hostile toward me and refuse to obey me, I will inflict disaster on you seven times over for your sins.
3_26_21.WEB: “‘If you walk contrary to me, and won’t listen to me, then I will bring seven times more plagues on you according to your sins.
3_26_21.CSB17: “If you act with hostility toward me and are unwilling to obey me, I will multiply your plagues seven times for your sins.
3_26_21.AMP: 'If then, you act with hostility toward Me and are unwilling to obey Me, I will increase the plague on you seven times in accordance with your sins.
3_26_21.MSG: "If you defy me and refuse to listen, your punishment will be seven times more than your sins:
3_26_21.NIV: “‘If you remain hostile toward me and refuse to listen to me, I will multiply your afflictions seven times over, as your sins deserve.
3_26_21.NRSVCE: If you continue hostile to me, and will not obey me, I will continue to plague you sevenfold for your sins.

3_26_22.NKJV: I will also send wild beasts among you, which shall rob you of your children, destroy your livestock, and make you few in number; and your highways shall be desolate.
3_26_22.KJV: I will also send wild beasts among you, which shall rob you of your children, and destroy your cattle, and make you few in number; and your high ways shall be desolate.
3_26_22.ESV: And I will let loose the wild beasts against you, which shall bereave you of your children and destroy your livestock and make you few in number, so that your roads shall be deserted.
3_26_22.NASB: 'I will let loose among you the beasts of the field, which will bereave you of your children and destroy your cattle and reduce your number so that your roads lie deserted.
3_26_22.NLT: I will send wild animals that will rob you of your children and destroy your livestock. Your numbers will dwindle, and your roads will be deserted.
3_26_22.WEB: I will send the wild animals among you, which will rob you of your children, destroy your livestock, and make you few in number. Your roads will become desolate.
3_26_22.CSB17: I will send wild animals against you that will deprive you of your children, ravage your livestock, and reduce your numbers until your roads are deserted.
3_26_22.AMP: I will let loose the [wild] animals of the field among you, which will bereave you of your children and destroy your livestock and make you so few in number that your roads will lie deserted and desolate.
3_26_22.MSG: I'll set wild animals on you; they'll rob you of your children, kill your cattle, and decimate your numbers until you'll think you are living in a ghost town.
3_26_22.NIV: I will send wild animals against you, and they will rob you of your children, destroy your cattle and make you so few in number that your roads will be deserted.
3_26_22.NRSVCE: I will let loose wild animals against you, and they shall bereave you of your children and destroy your livestock; they shall make you few in number, and your roads shall be deserted.

3_26_23.NKJV: ‘And if by these things you are not reformed by Me, but walk contrary to Me,
3_26_23.KJV: And if ye will not be reformed by me by these things, but will walk contrary unto me;
3_26_23.ESV: “And if by this discipline you are not turned to me but walk contrary to me,
3_26_23.NASB: 'And if by these things you are not turned to Me, but act with hostility against Me,
3_26_23.NLT: “And if you fail to learn the lesson and continue your hostility toward me,
3_26_23.WEB: “‘If by these things you won’t be reformed to me, but will walk contrary to me;
3_26_23.CSB17: “If in spite of these things you do not accept my discipline, but act with hostility toward me,
3_26_23.AMP: 'And if by these things you are not turned to Me, but act with hostility against Me,
3_26_23.MSG: "And if even this doesn't work and you refuse my discipline and continue your defiance,
3_26_23.NIV: “‘If in spite of these things you do not accept my correction but continue to be hostile toward me,
3_26_23.NRSVCE: If in spite of these punishments you have not turned back to me, but continue hostile to me,

3_26_24.NKJV: then I also will walk contrary to you, and I will punish you yet seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.KJV: Then will I also walk contrary unto you, and will punish you yet seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.ESV: then I also will walk contrary to you, and I myself will strike you sevenfold for your sins.
3_26_24.NASB: then I will act with hostility against you; and I, even I, will strike you seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.NLT: then I myself will be hostile toward you. I will personally strike you with calamity seven times over for your sins.
3_26_24.WEB: then I will also walk contrary to you; and I will strike you, even I, seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.CSB17: then I will act with hostility toward you; I also will strike you seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.AMP: then I also will act with hostility against you, and I will strike you seven times for your sins.
3_26_24.MSG: then it will be my turn to defy you. I, yes I, will punish you for your sins seven times over:
3_26_24.NIV: I myself will be hostile toward you and will afflict you for your sins seven times over.
3_26_24.NRSVCE: then I too will continue hostile to you: I myself will strike you sevenfold for your sins.

3_26_25.NKJV: And I will bring a sword against you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant; when you are gathered together within your cities I will send pestilence among you; and you shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
3_26_25.KJV: And I will bring a sword upon you, that shall avenge the quarrel of my covenant: and when ye are gathered together within your cities, I will send the pestilence among you; and ye shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
3_26_25.ESV: And I will bring a sword upon you, that shall execute vengeance for the covenant. And if you gather within your cities, I will send pestilence among you, and you shall be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
3_26_25.NASB: 'I will also bring upon you a sword which will execute vengeance for the covenant; and when you gather together into your cities, I will send pestilence among you, so that you shall be delivered into enemy hands.
3_26_25.NLT: I will send armies against you to carry out the curse of the covenant you have broken. When you run to your towns for safety, I will send a plague to destroy you there, and you will be handed over to your enemies.
3_26_25.WEB: I will bring a sword upon you that will execute the vengeance of the covenant. You will be gathered together within your cities, and I will send the pestilence among you. You will be delivered into the hand of the enemy.
3_26_25.CSB17: I will bring a sword against you to execute the vengeance of the covenant. Though you withdraw into your cities, I will send a pestilence among you, and you will be delivered into enemy hands.
3_26_25.AMP: I will bring a sword on you that will execute vengeance for [breaking] the covenant; and when you gather together in your cities, I will send pestilence (virulent disease) among you, and you shall be handed over to the enemy.
3_26_25.MSG: I'll let war loose on you, avenging your breaking of the covenant; when you huddle in your cities for protection, I'll send a deadly epidemic on you and you'll be helpless before your enemies;
3_26_25.NIV: And I will bring the sword upon you to avenge the breaking of the covenant. When you withdraw into your cities, I will send a plague among you, and you will be given into enemy hands.
3_26_25.NRSVCE: I will bring the sword against you, executing vengeance for the covenant; and if you withdraw within your cities, I will send pestilence among you, and you shall be delivered into enemy hands.

3_26_26.NKJV: When I have cut off your supply of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall bring back your bread by weight, and you shall eat and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.KJV: And when I have broken the staff of your bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall deliver you your bread again by weight: and ye shall eat, and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.ESV: When I break your supply of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in a single oven and shall dole out your bread again by weight, and you shall eat and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.NASB: 'When I break your staff of bread, ten women will bake your bread in one oven, and they will bring back your bread in rationed amounts, so that you will eat and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.NLT: I will destroy your food supply, so that ten women will need only one oven to bake bread for their families. They will ration your food by weight, and though you have food to eat, you will not be satisfied.
3_26_26.WEB: When I break your staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in one oven, and they shall deliver your bread again by weight. You shall eat, and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.CSB17: When I cut off your supply of bread, ten women will bake your bread in a single oven and ration out your bread by weight, so that you will eat but not be satisfied.
3_26_26.AMP: When I break your staff of bread [that is, cut off your supply of food], ten women will bake your bread in one oven, and they will ration your bread; and you will eat and not be satisfied.
3_26_26.MSG: when I cut off your bread supply, ten women will bake bread in one oven and ration it out. You'll eat, but barely—no one will get enough.
3_26_26.NIV: When I cut off your supply of bread, ten women will be able to bake your bread in one oven, and they will dole out the bread by weight. You will eat, but you will not be satisfied.
3_26_26.NRSVCE: When I break your staff of bread, ten women shall bake your bread in a single oven, and they shall dole out your bread by weight; and though you eat, you shall not be satisfied.

3_26_27.NKJV: ‘And after all this, if you do not obey Me, but walk contrary to Me,
3_26_27.KJV: And if ye will not for all this hearken unto me, but walk contrary unto me;
3_26_27.ESV: “But if in spite of this you will not listen to me, but walk contrary to me,
3_26_27.NASB: 'Yet if in spite of this you do not obey Me, but act with hostility against Me,
3_26_27.NLT: “If in spite of all this you still refuse to listen and still remain hostile toward me,
3_26_27.WEB: “‘If you in spite of this won’t listen to me, but walk contrary to me;
3_26_27.CSB17: “And if in spite of this you do not obey me but act with hostility toward me,
3_26_27.AMP: 'Yet if in spite of this you will not [attentively] listen to Me but act with hostility against me,
3_26_27.MSG: "And if this—even this!—doesn't work and you still won't listen, still defy me,
3_26_27.NIV: “‘If in spite of this you still do not listen to me but continue to be hostile toward me,
3_26_27.NRSVCE: But if, despite this, you disobey me, and continue hostile to me,

3_26_28.NKJV: then I also will walk contrary to you in fury; and I, even I, will chastise you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.KJV: Then I will walk contrary unto you also in fury; and I, even I, will chastise you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.ESV: then I will walk contrary to you in fury, and I myself will discipline you sevenfold for your sins.
3_26_28.NASB: then I will act with wrathful hostility against you, and I, even I, will punish you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.NLT: then I will give full vent to my hostility. I myself will punish you seven times over for your sins.
3_26_28.WEB: then I will walk contrary to you in wrath. I will also chastise you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.CSB17: I will act with furious hostility toward you; I will also discipline you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.AMP: then I will act with hostility against you in wrath, and I also will punish you seven times for your sins.
3_26_28.MSG: I'll have had enough and in hot anger will defy you, punishing you for your sins seven times over:
3_26_28.NIV: then in my anger I will be hostile toward you, and I myself will punish you for your sins seven times over.
3_26_28.NRSVCE: I will continue hostile to you in fury; I in turn will punish you myself sevenfold for your sins.

3_26_29.NKJV: You shall eat the flesh of your sons, and you shall eat the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.KJV: And ye shall eat the flesh of your sons, and the flesh of your daughters shall ye eat.
3_26_29.ESV: You shall eat the flesh of your sons, and you shall eat the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.NASB: 'Further, you will eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters you will eat.
3_26_29.NLT: Then you will eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters.
3_26_29.WEB: You will eat the flesh of your sons, and you will eat the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.CSB17: You will eat the flesh of your sons; you will eat the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.AMP: You will eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.MSG: famine will be so severe that you'll end up cooking and eating your sons in stews and your daughters in barbecues;
3_26_29.NIV: You will eat the flesh of your sons and the flesh of your daughters.
3_26_29.NRSVCE: You shall eat the flesh of your sons, and you shall eat the flesh of your daughters.

3_26_30.NKJV: I will destroy your high places, cut down your incense altars, and cast your carcasses on the lifeless forms of your idols; and My soul shall abhor you.
3_26_30.KJV: And I will destroy your high places, and cut down your images, and cast your carcases upon the carcases of your idols, and my soul shall abhor you.
3_26_30.ESV: And I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars and cast your dead bodies upon the dead bodies of your idols, and my soul will abhor you.
3_26_30.NASB: 'I then will destroy your high places, and cut down your incense altars, and heap your remains on the remains of your idols, for My soul shall abhor you.
3_26_30.NLT: I will destroy your pagan shrines and knock down your places of worship. I will leave your lifeless corpses piled on top of your lifeless idols, and I will despise you.
3_26_30.WEB: I will destroy your high places, and cut down your incense altars, and cast your dead bodies upon the bodies of your idols; and my soul will abhor you.
3_26_30.CSB17: I will destroy your high places, cut down your shrines, and heap your lifeless bodies on the lifeless bodies of your idols; I will reject you.
3_26_30.AMP: I will destroy your high places [devoted to idolatrous worship], and cut down your incense altars, and heap your dead bodies upon the [crushed] bodies of your idols, and My soul will detest you [with deep and unutterable loathing].
3_26_30.MSG: I'll smash your sex-and-religion shrines and all the paraphernalia that goes with them, and then stack your corpses and the idol-corpses in the same piles—I'll abhor you;
3_26_30.NIV: I will destroy your high places, cut down your incense altars and pile your dead bodies on the lifeless forms of your idols, and I will abhor you.
3_26_30.NRSVCE: I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars; I will heap your carcasses on the carcasses of your idols. I will abhor you.

3_26_31.NKJV: I will lay your cities waste and bring your sanctuaries to desolation, and I will not smell the fragrance of your sweet aromas.
3_26_31.KJV: And I will make your cities waste, and bring your sanctuaries unto desolation, and I will not smell the savour of your sweet odours.
3_26_31.ESV: And I will lay your cities waste and will make your sanctuaries desolate, and I will not smell your pleasing aromas.
3_26_31.NASB: 'I will lay waste your cities as well and will make your sanctuaries desolate, and I will not smell your soothing aromas.
3_26_31.NLT: I will make your cities desolate and destroy your places of pagan worship. I will take no pleasure in your offerings that should be a pleasing aroma to me.
3_26_31.WEB: I will lay your cities waste, and will bring your sanctuaries to desolation. I will not take delight in the sweet fragrance of your offerings.
3_26_31.CSB17: I will reduce your cities to ruins and devastate your sanctuaries. I will not smell the pleasing aroma of your sacrifices.
3_26_31.AMP: I will lay waste your cities as well and will make your sanctuaries desolate, and I will not smell your sweet and soothing aromas [of offerings by fire].
3_26_31.MSG: I'll turn your cities into rubble; I'll clean out your sanctuaries; I'll hold my nose at the "pleasing aroma" of your sacrifices.
3_26_31.NIV: I will turn your cities into ruins and lay waste your sanctuaries, and I will take no delight in the pleasing aroma of your offerings.
3_26_31.NRSVCE: I will lay your cities waste, will make your sanctuaries desolate, and I will not smell your pleasing odors.

3_26_32.NKJV: I will bring the land to desolation, and your enemies who dwell in it shall be astonished at it.
3_26_32.KJV: And I will bring the land into desolation: and your enemies which dwell therein shall be astonished at it.
3_26_32.ESV: And I myself will devastate the land, so that your enemies who settle in it shall be appalled at it.
3_26_32.NASB: 'I will make the land desolate so that your enemies who settle in it will be appalled over it.
3_26_32.NLT: Yes, I myself will devastate your land, and your enemies who come to occupy it will be appalled at what they see.
3_26_32.WEB: I will bring the land into desolation; and your enemies that dwell therein will be astonished at it.
3_26_32.CSB17: I also will devastate the land, so that your enemies who come to live there will be appalled by it.
3_26_32.AMP: I will make the land desolate, and your enemies who settle in it will be appalled at it.
3_26_32.MSG: I'll turn your land into a lifeless moonscape—your enemies who come in to take over will be shocked at what they see.
3_26_32.NIV: I will lay waste the land, so that your enemies who live there will be appalled.
3_26_32.NRSVCE: I will devastate the land, so that your enemies who come to settle in it shall be appalled at it.

3_26_33.NKJV: I will scatter you among the nations and draw out a sword after you; your land shall be desolate and your cities waste.
3_26_33.KJV: And I will scatter you among the heathen, and will draw out a sword after you: and your land shall be desolate, and your cities waste.
3_26_33.ESV: And I will scatter you among the nations, and I will unsheathe the sword after you, and your land shall be a desolation, and your cities shall be a waste.
3_26_33.NASB: 'You, however, I will scatter among the nations and will draw out a sword after you, as your land becomes desolate and your cities become waste.
3_26_33.NLT: I will scatter you among the nations and bring out my sword against you. Your land will become desolate, and your cities will lie in ruins.
3_26_33.WEB: I will scatter you among the nations, and I will draw out the sword after you. Your land will be a desolation, and your cities shall be a waste.
3_26_33.CSB17: But I will scatter you among the nations, and I will draw a sword to chase after you. So your land will become desolate, and your cities will become ruins.
3_26_33.AMP: I will scatter you among the nations and draw out the sword [of your enemies] after you; your land will become desolate and your cities will become ruins.
3_26_33.MSG: I'll scatter you all over the world and keep after you with the point of my sword in your backs. There'll be nothing left in your land, nothing going on in your cities.
3_26_33.NIV: I will scatter you among the nations and will draw out my sword and pursue you. Your land will be laid waste, and your cities will lie in ruins.
3_26_33.NRSVCE: And you I will scatter among the nations, and I will unsheathe the sword against you; your land shall be a desolation, and your cities a waste.

3_26_34.NKJV: Then the land shall enjoy its sabbaths as long as it lies desolate and you are in your enemies’ land; then the land shall rest and enjoy its sabbaths.
3_26_34.KJV: Then shall the land enjoy her sabbaths, as long as it lieth desolate, and ye be in your enemies' land; even then shall the land rest, and enjoy her sabbaths.
3_26_34.ESV: “Then the land shall enjoy its Sabbaths as long as it lies desolate, while you are in your enemies' land; then the land shall rest, and enjoy its Sabbaths.
3_26_34.NASB: 'Then the land will enjoy its sabbaths all the days of the desolation, while you are in your enemies' land; then the land will rest and enjoy its sabbaths.
3_26_34.NLT: Then at last the land will enjoy its neglected Sabbath years as it lies desolate while you are in exile in the land of your enemies. Then the land will finally rest and enjoy the Sabbaths it missed.
3_26_34.WEB: Then the land will enjoy its Sabbaths as long as it lies desolate and you are in your enemies’ land. Even then the land will rest and enjoy its Sabbaths.
3_26_34.CSB17: “Then the land will make up for its Sabbath years during the time it lies desolate, while you are in the land of your enemies. At that time the land will rest and make up for its Sabbaths.
3_26_34.AMP: 'Then the land [of Israel] will enjoy its Sabbaths as long as it lies desolate, while you are in your enemies' land; then the land will rest and enjoy its Sabbaths.
3_26_34.MSG: With you gone and dispersed in the countries of your enemies, the land, empty of you, will finally get a break and enjoy its Sabbath years.
3_26_34.NIV: Then the land will enjoy its sabbath years all the time that it lies desolate and you are in the country of your enemies; then the land will rest and enjoy its sabbaths.
3_26_34.NRSVCE: Then the land shall enjoy its sabbath years as long as it lies desolate, while you are in the land of your enemies; then the land shall rest, and enjoy its sabbath years.

3_26_35.NKJV: As long as it lies desolate it shall rest— for the time it did not rest on your sabbaths when you dwelt in it.
3_26_35.KJV: As long as it lieth desolate it shall rest; because it did not rest in your sabbaths, when ye dwelt upon it.
3_26_35.ESV: As long as it lies desolate it shall have rest, the rest that it did not have on your Sabbaths when you were dwelling in it.
3_26_35.NASB: 'All the days of [its] desolation it will observe the rest which it did not observe on your sabbaths, while you were living on it.
3_26_35.NLT: As long as the land lies in ruins, it will enjoy the rest you never allowed it to take every seventh year while you lived in it.
3_26_35.WEB: As long as it lies desolate it shall have rest, even the rest which it didn’t have in your Sabbaths, when you lived on it.
3_26_35.CSB17: As long as it lies desolate, it will have the rest it did not have during your Sabbaths when you lived there.
3_26_35.AMP: As long as it lies desolate, it will have rest, the rest it did not have on your Sabbaths, while you were living on it.
3_26_35.MSG: All the time it's left there empty, the land will get rest, the Sabbaths it never got when you lived there.
3_26_35.NIV: All the time that it lies desolate, the land will have the rest it did not have during the sabbaths you lived in it.
3_26_35.NRSVCE: As long as it lies desolate, it shall have the rest it did not have on your sabbaths when you were living on it.

3_26_36.NKJV: ‘And as for those of you who are left, I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a shaken leaf shall cause them to flee; they shall flee as though fleeing from a sword, and they shall fall when no one pursues.
3_26_36.KJV: And upon them that are left alive of you I will send a faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; and the sound of a shaken leaf shall chase them; and they shall flee, as fleeing from a sword; and they shall fall when none pursueth. shaken: Heb. driven
3_26_36.ESV: And as for those of you who are left, I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies. The sound of a driven leaf shall put them to flight, and they shall flee as one flees from the sword, and they shall fall when none pursues.
3_26_36.NASB: 'As for those of you who may be left, I will also bring weakness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies. And the sound of a driven leaf will chase them, and even when no one is pursuing they will flee as though from the sword, and they will fall.
3_26_36.NLT: “And for those of you who survive, I will demoralize you in the land of your enemies. You will live in such fear that the sound of a leaf driven by the wind will send you fleeing. You will run as though fleeing from a sword, and you will fall even when no one pursues you.
3_26_36.WEB: “‘As for those of you who are left, I will send a faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies. The sound of a driven leaf will put them to flight; and they shall flee, as one flees from the sword. They will fall when no one pursues.
3_26_36.CSB17: “I will put anxiety in the hearts of those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies. The sound of a wind-driven leaf will put them to flight, and they will flee as one flees from a sword, and fall though no one is pursuing them.
3_26_36.AMP: As for those who are left of you, I will bring despair (lack of courage, weakness) into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a scattered leaf will put them to flight, and they will flee as if [running] from the sword, and will fall even when no one is chasing them.
3_26_36.MSG: "As for those among you still alive, I'll give them over to fearful timidity—even the rustle of a leaf will throw them into a panic. They'll run here and there, back and forth, as if running for their lives even though no one is after them,
3_26_36.NIV: “‘As for those of you who are left, I will make their hearts so fearful in the lands of their enemies that the sound of a windblown leaf will put them to flight. They will run as though fleeing from the sword, and they will fall, even though no one is pursuing them.
3_26_36.NRSVCE: And as for those of you who survive, I will send faintness into their hearts in the lands of their enemies; the sound of a driven leaf shall put them to flight, and they shall flee as one flees from the sword, and they shall fall though no one pursues.

3_26_37.NKJV: They shall stumble over one another, as it were before a sword, when no one pursues; and you shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.KJV: And they shall fall one upon another, as it were before a sword, when none pursueth: and ye shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.ESV: They shall stumble over one another, as if to escape a sword, though none pursues. And you shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.NASB: 'They will therefore stumble over each other as if [running] from the sword, although no one is pursuing; and you will have [no strength] to stand up before your enemies.
3_26_37.NLT: Though no one is chasing you, you will stumble over each other as though fleeing from a sword. You will have no power to stand up against your enemies.
3_26_37.WEB: They will stumble over one another, as it were before the sword, when no one pursues. You will have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.CSB17: They will stumble over one another as if fleeing from a sword though no one is pursuing them. You will not be able to stand against your enemies.
3_26_37.AMP: They shall stumble over one another as if to escape from a sword when no one is chasing them; and you will have no power to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.MSG: tripping and falling over one another in total confusion. You won't stand a chance against an enemy.
3_26_37.NIV: They will stumble over one another as though fleeing from the sword, even though no one is pursuing them. So you will not be able to stand before your enemies.
3_26_37.NRSVCE: They shall stumble over one another, as if to escape a sword, though no one pursues; and you shall have no power to stand against your enemies.

3_26_38.NKJV: You shall perish among the nations, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
3_26_38.KJV: And ye shall perish among the heathen, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
3_26_38.ESV: And you shall perish among the nations, and the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
3_26_38.NASB: 'But you will perish among the nations, and your enemies' land will consume you.

3_26_39.NKJV: And those of you who are left shall waste away in their iniquity in your enemies’ lands; also in their fathers’ iniquities, which are with them, they shall waste away.
3_26_39.KJV: And they that are left of you shall pine away in their iniquity in your enemies' lands; and also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away with them.

3_26_39.NASB: 'So those of you who may be left will rot away because of their iniquity in the lands of your enemies; and also because of the iniquities of their forefathers they will rot away with them.
3_26_39.NLT: Those of you who survive will waste away in your enemies’ lands because of their sins and the sins of their ancestors.

3_26_39.NRSVCE: And those of you who survive shall languish in the land of your enemies because of their iniquities; also they shall languish because of the iniquities of their ancestors.

3_26_43.NKJV: The land also shall be left empty by them, and will enjoy its sabbaths while it lies desolate without them; they will accept their guilt, because they despised My judgments and because their soul abhorred My statutes.
3_26_43.KJV: The land also shall be left of them, and shall enjoy her sabbaths, while she lieth desolate without them: and they shall accept of the punishment of their iniquity: because, even because they despised my judgments, and because their soul abhorred my statutes.
3_26_43.ESV: But the land shall be abandoned by them and enjoy its Sabbaths while it lies desolate without them, and they shall make amends for their iniquity, because they spurned my rules and their soul abhorred my statutes.
3_26_43.NASB: 'For the land will be abandoned by them, and will make up for its sabbaths while it is made desolate without them. They, meanwhile, will be making amends for their iniquity, because they rejected My ordinances and their soul abhorred My statutes.
3_26_43.NLT: For the land must be abandoned to enjoy its years of Sabbath rest as it lies deserted. At last the people will pay for their sins, for they have continually rejected my regulations and despised my decrees.
3_26_43.WEB: The land also will be left by them, and will enjoy its Sabbaths while it lies desolate without them: and they will accept the punishment of their iniquity; because, even because they rejected my ordinances, and their soul abhorred my statutes.
3_26_43.CSB17: For the land abandoned by them will make up for its Sabbaths by lying desolate without the people, while they make amends for their iniquity, because they rejected my ordinances and abhorred my statutes.
3_26_43.AMP: But the land will be abandoned by them and will enjoy its Sabbaths while it lies desolate without them; and they will accept the punishment for their wickedness and make amends because they rejected My ordinances and their soul rejected My statutes.
3_26_43.MSG: "The land will be empty of them and enjoy its Sabbaths while they're gone. They'll pay for their sins because they refused my laws and treated my decrees with contempt.
3_26_43.NIV: For the land will be deserted by them and will enjoy its sabbaths while it lies desolate without them. They will pay for their sins because they rejected my laws and abhorred my decrees.
3_26_43.NRSVCE: For the land shall be deserted by them, and enjoy its sabbath years by lying desolate without them, while they shall make amends for their iniquity, because they dared to spurn my ordinances, and they abhorred my statutes.

3_26_44.NKJV: Yet for all that, when they are in the land of their enemies, I will not cast them away, nor shall I abhor them, to utterly destroy them and break My covenant with them; for I am the LORD their God.
