3_27_8.MSG: If anyone is too poor to pay the stated amount, he is to present the person to the priest, who will then set the value for him according to what the person making the vow can afford.
3_27_8.NIV: If anyone making the vow is too poor to pay the specified amount, he is to present the person to the priest, who will set the value for him according to what the man making the vow can afford.
3_27_8.NRSVCE: If any cannot afford the equivalent, they shall be brought before the priest and the priest shall assess them; the priest shall assess them according to what each one making a vow can afford.

3_27_9.NKJV: ‘If it is an animal that men may bring as an offering to the LORD, all that anyone gives to the LORD shall be holy.
3_27_9.KJV: And if it be a beast, whereof men bring an offering unto the LORD, all that any man giveth of such unto the LORD shall be holy.
3_27_9.ESV: “If the vow is an animal that may be offered as an offering to the LORD, all of it that he gives to the LORD is holy.
3_27_9.NASB: 'Now if it is an animal of the kind which men can present as an offering to the LORD, any such that one gives to the LORD shall be holy.
3_27_9.NLT: “If your vow involves giving an animal that is acceptable as an offering to the LORD, any gift to the LORD will be considered holy.
3_27_9.WEB: “‘If it is an animal, of which men offer an offering to Yahweh, all that any man gives of such to Yahweh becomes holy.
3_27_9.CSB17: “If the vow involves one of the animals that may be brought as an offering to the LORD, any of these he gives to the LORD will be holy.
3_27_9.AMP: 'Now if it is an animal of the kind which men can present as an offering to the LORD, any such that one gives to the LORD shall be holy.
3_27_9.MSG: "If he vowed an animal that is acceptable as an offering to God, the animal is given to God and becomes the property of the Sanctuary.
3_27_9.NIV: “‘If what he vowed is an animal that is acceptable as an offering to the Lord, such an animal given to the Lord becomes holy.
3_27_9.NRSVCE: If it concerns an animal that may be brought as an offering to the LORD, any such that may be given to the LORD shall be holy.

3_27_10.NKJV: He shall not substitute it or exchange it, good for bad or bad for good; and if he at all exchanges animal for animal, then both it and the one exchanged for it shall be holy.
3_27_10.KJV: He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then it and the exchange thereof shall be holy.
3_27_10.ESV: He shall not exchange it or make a substitute for it, good for bad, or bad for good; and if he does in fact substitute one animal for another, then both it and the substitute shall be holy.
3_27_10.NASB: 'He shall not replace it or exchange it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good; or if he does exchange animal for animal, then both it and its substitute shall become holy.
3_27_10.NLT: You may not exchange or substitute it for another animal — neither a good animal for a bad one nor a bad animal for a good one. But if you do exchange one animal for another, then both the original animal and its substitute will be considered holy.
3_27_10.WEB: He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: and if he shall at all change animal for animal, then both it and that for which it is changed shall be holy.
3_27_10.CSB17: He may not replace it or make a substitution for it, either good for bad, or bad for good. But if he does substitute one animal for another, both that animal and its substitute will be holy.
3_27_10.AMP: He shall not replace it or exchange it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good; but if he does exchange an animal for an animal, then both the original offering and its substitute shall be holy.
3_27_10.MSG: He must not exchange or substitute a good one for a bad one, or a bad one for a good one; if he should dishonestly substitute one animal for another, both the original and the substitute become property of the Sanctuary.
3_27_10.NIV: He must not exchange it or substitute a good one for a bad one, or a bad one for a good one; if he should substitute one animal for another, both it and the substitute become holy.
3_27_10.NRSVCE: Another shall not be exchanged or substituted for it, either good for bad or bad for good; and if one animal is substituted for another, both that one and its substitute shall be holy.

3_27_11.NKJV: If it is an unclean animal which they do not offer as a sacrifice to the LORD, then he shall present the animal before the priest;
3_27_11.KJV: And if it be any unclean beast, of which they do not offer a sacrifice unto the LORD, then he shall present the beast before the priest:
3_27_11.ESV: And if it is any unclean animal that may not be offered as an offering to the LORD, then he shall stand the animal before the priest,
3_27_11.NASB: 'If, however, it is any unclean animal of the kind which men do not present as an offering to the LORD, then he shall place the animal before the priest.
3_27_11.NLT: If your vow involves an unclean animal — one that is not acceptable as an offering to the LORD — then you must bring the animal to the priest.
3_27_11.WEB: If it is any unclean animal, of which they do not offer as an offering to Yahweh, then he shall set the animal before the priest;
3_27_11.CSB17: “If the vow involves any of the unclean animals that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD, the animal must be presented before the priest.
3_27_11.AMP: If it is any unclean animal of the kind which men do not present as an offering to the LORD, then he shall bring the animal before the priest,
3_27_11.MSG: If what he vowed is a ritually unclean animal, one that is not acceptable as an offering to God, the animal must be shown to the priest,
3_27_11.NIV: If what he vowed is a ceremonially unclean animal—one that is not acceptable as an offering to the Lord—the animal must be presented to the priest,
3_27_11.NRSVCE: If it concerns any unclean animal that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD, the animal shall be presented before the priest.

3_27_12.NKJV: and the priest shall set a value for it, whether it is good or bad; as you, the priest, value it, so it shall be.
3_27_12.KJV: And the priest shall value it, whether it be good or bad: as thou valuest it, who art the priest, so shall it be. as: Heb. according to thy estimation, O priest, etc
3_27_12.ESV: and the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall be.
3_27_12.NASB: 'The priest shall value it as either good or bad; as you, the priest, value it, so it shall be.
3_27_12.NLT: He will assess its value, and his assessment will be final, whether high or low.
3_27_12.WEB: and the priest shall value it, whether it is good or bad. As you the priest values it, so shall it be.
3_27_12.CSB17: The priest will set its value, whether high or low; the price will be set as the priest makes the assessment for you.
3_27_12.AMP: and the priest shall value it as either good or bad; it shall be as you, the priest, value it.
3_27_12.MSG: who will set its value, either high or low. Whatever the priest sets will be its value.
3_27_12.NIV: who will judge its quality as good or bad. Whatever value the priest then sets, that is what it will be.
3_27_12.NRSVCE: The priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, according to the assessment of the priest, so it shall be.

3_27_13.NKJV: But if he wants at all to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth to your valuation.
3_27_13.KJV: But if he will at all redeem it, then he shall add a fifth part thereof unto thy estimation.
3_27_13.ESV: But if he wishes to redeem it, he shall add a fifth to the valuation.
3_27_13.NASB: 'But if he should ever [wish to] redeem it, then he shall add one-fifth of it to your valuation.
3_27_13.NLT: If you want to buy back the animal, you must pay the value set by the priest, plus 20 percent.
3_27_13.WEB: But if he will indeed redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of it to its valuation.
3_27_13.CSB17: If the one who brought it decides to redeem it, he must add a fifth to the assessed value.
3_27_13.AMP: But if he ever wishes to redeem it, then he shall add one-fifth of it to your valuation.
3_27_13.MSG: If the owner changes his mind and wants to redeem it, he must add twenty percent to its value.
3_27_13.NIV: If the owner wishes to redeem the animal, he must add a fifth to its value.
3_27_13.NRSVCE: But if it is to be redeemed, one-fifth must be added to the assessment.

3_27_14.NKJV: ‘And when a man dedicates his house to be holy to the LORD, then the priest shall set a value for it, whether it is good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall stand.
3_27_14.KJV: And when a man shall sanctify his house to be holy unto the LORD, then the priest shall estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.
3_27_14.ESV: “When a man dedicates his house as a holy gift to the LORD, the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall stand.
3_27_14.NASB: 'Now if a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD, then the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as the priest values it, so it shall stand.
3_27_14.NLT: “If someone dedicates a house to the LORD, the priest will come to assess its value. The priest’s assessment will be final, whether high or low.
3_27_14.WEB: “‘When a man dedicates his house to be holy to Yahweh, then the priest shall evaluate it, whether it is good or bad: as the priest shall evaluate it, so shall it stand.
3_27_14.CSB17: “When a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD, the priest will assess its value, whether high or low. The price will stand just as the priest assesses it.
3_27_14.AMP: 'If a man consecrates his house as sacred to the LORD, the priest shall appraise it as either good or bad; as the priest appraises it, so shall it stand.
3_27_14.MSG: "If a man dedicates his house to God, into the possession of the Sanctuary, the priest assesses its value, setting it either high or low. Whatever value the priest sets, that's what it is.
3_27_14.NIV: “‘If a man dedicates his house as something holy to the Lord, the priest will judge its quality as good or bad. Whatever value the priest then sets, so it will remain.
3_27_14.NRSVCE: If a person consecrates a house to the LORD, the priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, as the priest assesses it, so it shall stand.

3_27_15.NKJV: If he who dedicated it wants to redeem his house, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall be his.
3_27_15.KJV: And if he that sanctified it will redeem his house, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be his.
3_27_15.ESV: And if the donor wishes to redeem his house, he shall add a fifth to the valuation price, and it shall be his.
3_27_15.NASB: 'Yet if the one who consecrates it should [wish to] redeem his house, then he shall add one-fifth of your valuation price to it, so that it may be his.
3_27_15.NLT: If the person who dedicated the house wants to buy it back, he must pay the value set by the priest, plus 20 percent. Then the house will again be his.
3_27_15.WEB: If he who dedicates it will redeem his house, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall be his.
3_27_15.CSB17: But if the one who consecrated his house redeems it, he must add a fifth to the assessed value, and it will be his.
3_27_15.AMP: If the one who consecrates his house should wish to redeem it, then he shall add one-fifth of your valuation price to it, so that it may be his.
3_27_15.MSG: If the man wants to buy it back, he must add twenty percent to its price and then it's his again.
3_27_15.NIV: If the man who dedicates his house redeems it, he must add a fifth to its value, and the house will again become his.
3_27_15.NRSVCE: And if the one who consecrates the house wishes to redeem it, one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner.

3_27_16.NKJV: ‘If a man dedicates to the LORD part of a field of his possession, then your valuation shall be according to the seed for it. A homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_16.KJV: And if a man shall sanctify unto the LORD some part of a field of his possession, then thy estimation shall be according to the seed thereof: an homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver. an: or, the land of an homer, etc
3_27_16.ESV: “If a man dedicates to the LORD part of the land that is his possession, then the valuation shall be in proportion to its seed. A homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_16.NASB: 'Again, if a man consecrates to the LORD part of the fields of his own property, then your valuation shall be proportionate to the seed needed for it: a homer of barley seed at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_16.NLT: “If someone dedicates to the LORD a piece of his family property, its value will be assessed according to the amount of seed required to plant it — fifty shekels of silver for a field planted with five bushels of barley seed.
3_27_16.WEB: “‘If a man dedicates to Yahweh part of the field of his possession, then your valuation shall be according to the seed for it. The sowing of a homer of barley shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_16.CSB17: “If a man consecrates to the LORD any part of a field that he possesses, your assessment of value will be proportional to the seed needed to sow it, at the rate of fifty silver shekels for every five bushels of barley seed.
3_27_16.AMP: 'And if a man consecrates to the LORD part of a field of his own property, then your valuation shall be proportionate to the seed needed for it; a homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
3_27_16.MSG: "If a man dedicates to God part of his family land, its value is to be set according to the amount of seed that is needed for it at the rate of fifty shekels of silver to six bushels of barley seed.
3_27_16.NIV: “‘If a man dedicates to the Lord part of his family land, its value is to be set according to the amount of seed required for it—fifty shekels of silver to a homer of barley seed.
3_27_16.NRSVCE: If a person consecrates to the LORD any inherited landholding, its assessment shall be in accordance with its seed requirements: fifty shekels of silver to a homer of barley seed.

3_27_17.NKJV: If he dedicates his field from the Year of Jubilee, according to your valuation it shall stand.
3_27_17.KJV: If he sanctify his field from the year of jubile, according to thy estimation it shall stand.
3_27_17.ESV: If he dedicates his field from the year of jubilee, the valuation shall stand,
3_27_17.NASB: 'If he consecrates his field as of the year of jubilee, according to your valuation it shall stand.
3_27_17.NLT: If the field is dedicated to the LORD in the Year of Jubilee, then the entire assessment will apply.
3_27_17.WEB: If he dedicates his field from the Year of Jubilee, according to your valuation it shall stand.
3_27_17.CSB17: If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee, the price will stand according to your assessment.
3_27_17.AMP: If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee, it shall stand according to your valuation.
3_27_17.MSG: If he dedicates his field during the year of Jubilee, the set value stays.
3_27_17.NIV: If he dedicates his field during the Year of Jubilee, the value that has been set remains.
3_27_17.NRSVCE: If the person consecrates the field as of the year of jubilee, that assessment shall stand;

3_27_18.NKJV: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall reckon to him the money due according to the years that remain till the Year of Jubilee, and it shall be deducted from your valuation.
3_27_18.KJV: But if he sanctify his field after the jubile, then the priest shall reckon unto him the money according to the years that remain, even unto the year of the jubile, and it shall be abated from thy estimation.
3_27_18.ESV: but if he dedicates his field after the jubilee, then the priest shall calculate the price according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee, and a deduction shall be made from the valuation.
3_27_18.NASB: 'If he consecrates his field after the jubilee, however, then the priest shall calculate the price for him proportionate to the years that are left until the year of jubilee; and it shall be deducted from your valuation.
3_27_18.NLT: But if the field is dedicated after the Year of Jubilee, the priest will assess the land’s value in proportion to the number of years left until the next Year of Jubilee. Its assessed value is reduced each year.
3_27_18.WEB: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall reckon to him the money according to the years that remain to the Year of Jubilee; and an abatement shall be made from your valuation.
3_27_18.CSB17: But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee, the priest will calculate the price for him in proportion to the years left until the next Year of Jubilee, so that your assessment will be reduced.
3_27_18.AMP: But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee, then the priest shall calculate the price for him in proportion to the years that remain until the Year of Jubilee; and it shall be deducted from your valuation.
3_27_18.MSG: But if he dedicates it after the Jubilee, the priest will compute the value according to the years left until the next Jubilee, reducing the value proportionately.
3_27_18.NIV: But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee, the priest will determine the value according to the number of years that remain until the next Year of Jubilee, and its set value will be reduced.
3_27_18.NRSVCE: but if the field is consecrated after the jubilee, the priest shall compute the price for it according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee, and the assessment shall be reduced.

3_27_19.NKJV: And if he who dedicates the field ever wishes to redeem it, then he must add one-fifth of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall belong to him.
3_27_19.KJV: And if he that sanctified the field will in any wise redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be assured to him.
3_27_19.ESV: And if he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it, then he shall add a fifth to its valuation price, and it shall remain his.
3_27_19.NASB: 'If the one who consecrates it should ever wish to redeem the field, then he shall add one-fifth of your valuation price to it, so that it may pass to him.
3_27_19.NLT: If the person who dedicated the field wants to buy it back, he must pay the value set by the priest, plus 20 percent. Then the field will again be legally his.
3_27_19.WEB: If he who dedicated the field will indeed redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of your valuation to it, and it shall remain his.
3_27_19.CSB17: If the one who consecrated the field decides to redeem it, he must add a fifth to the assessed value, and the field will transfer back to him.
3_27_19.AMP: If the one who consecrates the field should ever wish to redeem it, then he shall add one-fifth of the appraisal price to it, so that it may return to him.
3_27_19.MSG: If the one dedicating it wants to buy it back, he must add twenty percent to its valuation, and then it's his again.
3_27_19.NIV: If the man who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it, he must add a fifth to its value, and the field will again become his.
3_27_19.NRSVCE: And if the one who consecrates the field wishes to redeem it, then one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner;

3_27_20.NKJV: But if he does not want to redeem the field, or if he has sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed anymore;
3_27_20.KJV: And if he will not redeem the field, or if he have sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more.
3_27_20.ESV: But if he does not wish to redeem the field, or if he has sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed anymore.
3_27_20.NASB: 'Yet if he will not redeem the field, but has sold the field to another man, it may no longer be redeemed;
3_27_20.NLT: But if he does not want to buy it back, and it is sold to someone else, the field can no longer be bought back.
3_27_20.WEB: If he will not redeem the field, or if he has sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more;
3_27_20.CSB17: But if he does not redeem the field or if he has sold it to another man, it is no longer redeemable.
3_27_20.AMP: If he does not redeem the field, but has sold it to another man, it may no longer be redeemed.
3_27_20.MSG: But if he doesn't redeem it or sells the field to someone else, it can never be bought back.
3_27_20.NIV: If, however, he does not redeem the field, or if he has sold it to someone else, it can never be redeemed.
3_27_20.NRSVCE: but if the field is not redeemed, or if it has been sold to someone else, it shall no longer be redeemable.

3_27_21.NKJV: but the field, when it is released in the Jubilee, shall be holy to the LORD, as a devoted field; it shall be the possession of the priest.
3_27_21.KJV: But the field, when it goeth out in the jubile, shall be holy unto the LORD, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest's.
3_27_21.ESV: But the field, when it is released in the jubilee, shall be a holy gift to the LORD, like a field that has been devoted. The priest shall be in possession of it.
3_27_21.NASB: and when it reverts in the jubilee, the field shall be holy to the LORD, like a field set apart; it shall be for the priest as his property.
3_27_21.NLT: When the field is released in the Year of Jubilee, it will be holy, a field specially set apart for the LORD. It will become the property of the priests.
3_27_21.WEB: but the field, when it goes out in the Jubilee, shall be holy to Yahweh, as a devoted field. It shall be owned by the priests.
3_27_21.CSB17: When the field is released in the Jubilee, it will be holy to the LORD like a field permanently set apart; it becomes the priest’s property.
3_27_21.AMP: When the field reverts in the Jubilee, the field shall be holy to the LORD, like a field set apart (devoted); the priest shall possess it as his property.
3_27_21.MSG: When the field is released in the Jubilee, it becomes holy to God, the possession of the Sanctuary, God's field. It goes into the hands of the priests.
3_27_21.NIV: When the field is released in the Jubilee, it will become holy, like a field devoted to the Lord; it will become the property of the priests.
3_27_21.NRSVCE: But when the field is released in the jubilee, it shall be holy to the LORD as a devoted field; it becomes the priest’s holding.

3_27_22.NKJV: ‘And if a man dedicates to the LORD a field which he has bought, which is not the field of his possession,
3_27_22.KJV: And if a man sanctify unto the LORD a field which he hath bought, which is not of the fields of his possession;
3_27_22.ESV: If he dedicates to the LORD a field that he has bought, which is not a part of his possession,
3_27_22.NASB: 'Or if he consecrates to the LORD a field which he has bought, which is not a part of the field of his own property,
3_27_22.NLT: “If someone dedicates to the LORD a field he has purchased but which is not part of his family property,
3_27_22.WEB: “‘If he dedicates a field to Yahweh which he has bought, which is not of the field of his possession,
3_27_22.CSB17: “If a person consecrates to the LORD a field he has purchased that is not part of his inherited landholding,
3_27_22.AMP: Or if a man consecrates to the LORD a field which he has bought, which is not part of the field of his [ancestral] property,
3_27_22.MSG: "If a man dedicates to God a field he has bought, a field which is not part of the family land,
3_27_22.NIV: “‘If a man dedicates to the Lord a field he has bought, which is not part of his family land,
3_27_22.NRSVCE: If someone consecrates to the LORD a field that has been purchased, which is not a part of the inherited landholding,

3_27_23.NKJV: then the priest shall reckon to him the worth of your valuation, up to the Year of Jubilee, and he shall give your valuation on that day as a holy offering to the LORD.
3_27_23.KJV: Then the priest shall reckon unto him the worth of thy estimation, even unto the year of the jubile: and he shall give thine estimation in that day, as a holy thing unto the LORD.
3_27_23.ESV: then the priest shall calculate the amount of the valuation for it up to the year of jubilee, and the man shall give the valuation on that day as a holy gift to the LORD.
3_27_23.NASB: then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the year of jubilee; and he shall on that day give your valuation as holy to the LORD.
3_27_23.NLT: the priest will assess its value based on the number of years left until the next Year of Jubilee. On that day he must give the assessed value of the land as a sacred donation to the LORD.
3_27_23.WEB: then the priest shall reckon to him the worth of your valuation up to the Year of Jubilee; and he shall give your valuation on that day, as a holy thing to Yahweh.
3_27_23.CSB17: then the priest will calculate for him the amount of the assessment up to the Year of Jubilee, and the person will pay the assessed value on that day as a holy offering to the LORD.
3_27_23.AMP: then the priest shall calculate for him the amount of your valuation up to the Year of Jubilee; and the man shall give that [amount] on that day as a holy thing to the LORD.
3_27_23.MSG: the priest will compute its proportionate value in relation to the next year of Jubilee. The man must pay its value on the spot as something that is now holy to God, belonging to the Sanctuary.
3_27_23.NIV: the priest will determine its value up to the Year of Jubilee, and the man must pay its value on that day as something holy to the Lord.
3_27_23.NRSVCE: the priest shall compute for it the proportionate assessment up to the year of jubilee, and the assessment shall be paid as of that day, a sacred donation to the LORD.

3_27_24.NKJV: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, to the one who owned the land as a possession.
3_27_24.KJV: In the year of the jubile the field shall return unto him of whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land did belong.
3_27_24.ESV: In the year of jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, to whom the land belongs as a possession.
3_27_24.NASB: 'In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom he bought it, to whom the possession of the land belongs.
3_27_24.NLT: In the Year of Jubilee the field must be returned to the person from whom he purchased it, the one who inherited it as family property.
3_27_24.WEB: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land belongs.
3_27_24.CSB17: In the Year of Jubilee the field will return to the one he bought it from, the original owner.
3_27_24.AMP: In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was purchased, to whom the land belonged [as his ancestral inheritance].
3_27_24.MSG: In the year of Jubilee it goes back to its original owner, the man from whom he bought it.
3_27_24.NIV: In the Year of Jubilee the field will revert to the person from whom he bought it, the one whose land it was.
3_27_24.NRSVCE: In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought, whose holding the land is.

3_27_25.NKJV: And all your valuations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_25.KJV: And all thy estimations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
3_27_25.ESV: Every valuation shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
3_27_25.NASB: 'Every valuation of yours, moreover, shall be after the shekel of the sanctuary. The shekel shall be twenty gerahs.
3_27_25.NLT: (All the payments must be measured by the weight of the sanctuary shekel, which equals twenty gerahs.)
3_27_25.WEB: All your valuations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_25.CSB17: All your assessed values will be measured by the standard sanctuary shekel, twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_25.AMP: Every valuation of yours shall be in accordance with the sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
3_27_25.MSG: The valuations will be reckoned by the Sanctuary shekel, at twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_25.NIV: Every value is to be set according to the sanctuary shekel, twenty gerahs to the shekel.
3_27_25.NRSVCE: All assessments shall be by the sanctuary shekel: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.

3_27_26.NKJV: ‘But the firstborn of the animals, which should be the LORD’s firstborn, no man shall dedicate; whether it is an ox or sheep, it is the LORD’s.
3_27_26.KJV: Only the firstling of the beasts, which should be the LORD'S firstling, no man shall sanctify it; whether it be ox, or sheep: it is the LORD'S. firstling of: Heb. firstborn, etc
3_27_26.ESV: “But a firstborn of animals, which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD, no man may dedicate; whether ox or sheep, it is the LORD's.
3_27_26.NASB: 'However, a firstborn among animals, which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD, no man may consecrate it; whether ox or sheep, it is the LORD'S.
3_27_26.NLT: “You may not dedicate a firstborn animal to the LORD, for the firstborn of your cattle, sheep, and goats already belong to him.
3_27_26.WEB: “‘Only the firstborn among animals, which is made a firstborn to Yahweh, no man may dedicate it; whether an ox or sheep. It is Yahweh’s.
3_27_26.CSB17: “But no one can consecrate a firstborn of the livestock, whether an animal from the herd or flock, to the LORD, because a firstborn already belongs to the LORD.
3_27_26.AMP: 'However, the firstborn among animals, which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD, no man may consecrate, whether an ox or a sheep. It is [already] the Lord's.
3_27_26.MSG: "No one is allowed to dedicate the firstborn of an animal; the firstborn, as firstborn, already belongs to God. No matter if it's cattle or sheep, it already belongs to God.
3_27_26.NIV: “‘No one, however, may dedicate the firstborn of an animal, since the firstborn already belongs to the Lord; whether an ox or a sheep, it is the Lord’s.
3_27_26.NRSVCE: A firstling of animals, however, which as a firstling belongs to the LORD, cannot be consecrated by anyone; whether ox or sheep, it is the LORD’s.

3_27_27.NKJV: And if it is an unclean animal, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation, and shall add one-fifth to it; or if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
3_27_27.KJV: And if it be of an unclean beast, then he shall redeem it according to thine estimation, and shall add a fifth part of it thereto: or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to thy estimation.
3_27_27.ESV: And if it is an unclean animal, then he shall buy it back at the valuation, and add a fifth to it; or, if it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at the valuation.
3_27_27.NASB: 'But if [it is] among the unclean animals, then he shall redeem it according to your valuation and add to it one-fifth of it; and if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
3_27_27.NLT: However, you may buy back the firstborn of a ceremonially unclean animal by paying the priest’s assessment of its worth, plus 20 percent. If you do not buy it back, the priest will sell it at its assessed value.
3_27_27.WEB: If it is an unclean animal, then he shall buy it back according to your valuation, and shall add to it the fifth part of it; or if it isn’t redeemed, then it shall be sold according to your valuation.
3_27_27.CSB17: If it is one of the unclean livestock, it can be ransomed according to your assessment by adding a fifth of its value to it. If it is not redeemed, it can be sold according to your assessment.
3_27_27.AMP: If it is among the unclean animals, the owner may redeem it in accordance with your valuation, and add one-fifth to it; or if it is not redeemed, then it shall be sold in accordance with your valuation.
3_27_27.MSG: If it's one of the ritually unclean animals, he can buy it back at its assessed value by adding twenty percent to it. If he doesn't redeem it, it is to be sold at its assessed value.
3_27_27.NIV: If it is one of the unclean animals, he may buy it back at its set value, adding a fifth of the value to it. If he does not redeem it, it is to be sold at its set value.
3_27_27.NRSVCE: If it is an unclean animal, it shall be ransomed at its assessment, with one-fifth added; if it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at its assessment.

3_27_28.NKJV: ‘Nevertheless no devoted offering that a man may devote to the LORD of all that he has, both man and beast, or the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted offering is most holy to the LORD.
3_27_28.KJV: Notwithstanding no devoted thing, that a man shall devote unto the LORD of all that he hath, both of man and beast, and of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: every devoted thing is most holy unto the LORD.
3_27_28.ESV: “But no devoted thing that a man devotes to the LORD, of anything that he has, whether man or beast, or of his inherited field, shall be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy to the LORD.
3_27_28.NASB: 'Nevertheless, anything which a man sets apart to the LORD out of all that he has, of man or animal or of the fields of his own property, shall not be sold or redeemed. Anything devoted to destruction is most holy to the LORD.
3_27_28.NLT: “However, anything specially set apart for the LORD — whether a person, an animal, or family property — must never be sold or bought back. Anything devoted in this way has been set apart as holy, and it belongs to the LORD.
3_27_28.WEB: “‘Notwithstanding, no devoted thing, that a man shall devote to Yahweh of all that he has, whether of man or animal, or of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: every devoted thing is most holy to Yahweh.
3_27_28.CSB17: “Nothing that a man permanently sets apart to the LORD from all he owns, whether a person, an animal, or his inherited landholding, can be sold or redeemed; everything set apart is especially holy to the LORD.
3_27_28.AMP: 'But nothing that a man sets apart [that is, devotes as an offering] to the LORD out of all that he has, of man or of animal or of the fields of his own property, shall be sold or redeemed. Anything devoted to destruction (banned, cursed) is most holy to the LORD.
3_27_28.MSG: "But nothing that a man irrevocably devotes to God from what belongs to him, whether human or animal or family land, may be either sold or bought back. Everything devoted is holy to the highest degree; it's God's inalienable property.
3_27_28.NIV: “‘But nothing that a man owns and devotes to the Lord—whether man or animal or family land—may be sold or redeemed; everything so devoted is most holy to the Lord.
3_27_28.NRSVCE: Nothing that a person owns that has been devoted to destruction for the LORD, be it human or animal, or inherited landholding, may be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy to the LORD.

3_27_29.NKJV: No person under the ban, who may become doomed to destruction among men, shall be redeemed, but shall surely be put to death.
3_27_29.KJV: None devoted, which shall be devoted of men, shall be redeemed; but shall surely be put to death.
3_27_29.ESV: No one devoted, who is to be devoted for destruction from mankind, shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
3_27_29.NASB: 'No one who may have been set apart among men shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
3_27_29.NLT: No person specially set apart for destruction may be bought back. Such a person must be put to death.
3_27_29.WEB: “‘No one devoted, who shall be devoted from among men, shall be ransomed; he shall surely be put to death.
3_27_29.CSB17: No person who has been set apart for destruction is to be ransomed; he must be put to death.
3_27_29.AMP: No one who may have been set apart among men shall be ransomed [from death], he shall most certainly be put to death.
3_27_29.MSG: "No human who has been devoted to destruction can be redeemed. He must be put to death.
3_27_29.NIV: “‘No person devoted to destruction may be ransomed; he must be put to death.
3_27_29.NRSVCE: No human beings who have been devoted to destruction can be ransomed; they shall be put to death.

3_27_30.NKJV: And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD’s. It is holy to the LORD.
3_27_30.KJV: And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD'S: it is holy unto the LORD.
3_27_30.ESV: “Every tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the trees, is the LORD's; it is holy to the LORD.
3_27_30.NASB: 'Thus all the tithe of the land, of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the tree, is the LORD'S; it is holy to the LORD.
3_27_30.NLT: “One-tenth of the produce of the land, whether grain from the fields or fruit from the trees, belongs to the LORD and must be set apart to him as holy.
3_27_30.WEB: “‘All the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land or of the fruit of the trees, is Yahweh’s. It is holy to Yahweh.
3_27_30.CSB17: “Every tenth of the land’s produce, grain from the soil or fruit from the trees, belongs to the LORD; it is holy to the LORD.
3_27_30.AMP: 'And all the tithe (tenth part) of the land, whether the seed of the land or the fruit of the tree, is the Lord's; it is holy to the LORD.
3_27_30.MSG: "A tenth of the land's produce, whether grain from the ground or fruit from the trees, is God's. It is holy to God.
3_27_30.NIV: “‘A tithe of everything from the land, whether grain from the soil or fruit from the trees, belongs to the Lord; it is holy to the Lord.
3_27_30.NRSVCE: All tithes from the land, whether the seed from the ground or the fruit from the tree, are the LORD’s; they are holy to the LORD.

3_27_31.NKJV: If a man wants at all to redeem any of his tithes, he shall add one-fifth to it.
3_27_31.KJV: And if a man will at all redeem ought of his tithes, he shall add thereto the fifth part thereof.
3_27_31.ESV: If a man wishes to redeem some of his tithe, he shall add a fifth to it.
3_27_31.NASB: 'If, therefore, a man wishes to redeem part of his tithe, he shall add to it one-fifth of it.
3_27_31.NLT: If you want to buy back the LORD’s tenth of the grain or fruit, you must pay its value, plus 20 percent.
3_27_31.WEB: If a man redeems anything of his tithe, he shall add a fifth part to it.
3_27_31.CSB17: If a man decides to redeem any part of this tenth, he must add a fifth to its value.
3_27_31.AMP: If a man wishes to redeem any part of his tithe, he shall add one-fifth to it.
3_27_31.MSG: If a man buys back any of the tenth he has given, he must add twenty percent to it.
3_27_31.NIV: If a man redeems any of his tithe, he must add a fifth of the value to it.
3_27_31.NRSVCE: If persons wish to redeem any of their tithes, they must add one-fifth to them.

3_27_32.NKJV: And concerning the tithe of the herd or the flock, of whatever passes under the rod, the tenth one shall be holy to the LORD.
3_27_32.KJV: And concerning the tithe of the herd, or of the flock, even of whatsoever passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the LORD.
3_27_32.ESV: And every tithe of herds and flocks, every tenth animal of all that pass under the herdsman's staff, shall be holy to the LORD.
3_27_32.NASB: 'For every tenth part of herd or flock, whatever passes under the rod, the tenth one shall be holy to the LORD.
3_27_32.NLT: Count off every tenth animal from your herds and flocks and set them apart for the LORD as holy.
3_27_32.WEB: All the tithe of the herds or the flocks, whatever passes under the rod, the tenth shall be holy to Yahweh.
3_27_32.CSB17: Every tenth animal from the herd or flock, which passes under the shepherd’s rod, will be holy to the LORD.
3_27_32.AMP: For every tithe of the herd or flock, whatever passes under the [shepherd's] staff, the tenth one shall be holy to the LORD.
3_27_32.MSG: A tenth of the entire herd and flock, every tenth animal that passes under the shepherd's rod, is holy to God.
3_27_32.NIV: The entire tithe of the herd and flock—every tenth animal that passes under the shepherd’s rod—will be holy to the Lord.
3_27_32.NRSVCE: All tithes of herd and flock, every tenth one that passes under the shepherd’s staff, shall be holy to the LORD.

3_27_33.NKJV: He shall not inquire whether it is good or bad, nor shall he exchange it; and if he exchanges it at all, then both it and the one exchanged for it shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.’ ”
3_27_33.KJV: He shall not search whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it: and if he change it at all, then both it and the change thereof shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
3_27_33.ESV: One shall not differentiate between good or bad, neither shall he make a substitute for it; and if he does substitute for it, then both it and the substitute shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.”
3_27_33.NASB: 'He is not to be concerned whether [it is] good or bad, nor shall he exchange it; or if he does exchange it, then both it and its substitute shall become holy. It shall not be redeemed.''
3_27_33.NLT: You may not pick and choose between good and bad animals, and you may not substitute one for another. But if you do exchange one animal for another, then both the original animal and its substitute will be considered holy and cannot be bought back.”
3_27_33.WEB: He shall not search whether it is good or bad, neither shall he change it. If he changes it at all, then both it and that for which it is changed shall be holy. It shall not be redeemed.’”
3_27_33.CSB17: He is not to inspect whether it is good or bad, and he is not to make a substitution for it. But if he does make a substitution, both the animal and its substitute will be holy; they cannot be redeemed.”
3_27_33.AMP: The man is not to be concerned whether the animal is good or bad, nor shall he exchange it. But if he does exchange it, then both it and its substitute shall become holy; it shall not be redeemed.'"
3_27_33.MSG: He is not permitted to pick out the good from the bad or make a substitution. If he dishonestly makes a substitution, both animals, the original and the substitute, become the possession of the Sanctuary and cannot be redeemed."
3_27_33.NIV: He must not pick out the good from the bad or make any substitution. If he does make a substitution, both the animal and its substitute become holy and cannot be redeemed.’”
3_27_33.NRSVCE: Let no one inquire whether it is good or bad, or make substitution for it; if one makes substitution for it, then both it and the substitute shall be holy and cannot be redeemed.

3_27_34.NKJV: These are the commandments which the LORD commanded Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.
3_27_34.KJV: These are the commandments, which the LORD commanded Moses for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.
3_27_34.ESV: These are the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
3_27_34.NASB: These are the commandments which the LORD commanded Moses for the sons of Israel at Mount Sinai.
3_27_34.NLT: These are the commands that the LORD gave through Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
3_27_34.WEB: These are the commandments which Yahweh commanded Moses for the children of Israel on Mount Sinai.
3_27_34.CSB17: These are the commands the LORD gave Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
3_27_34.AMP: These are the commandments which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai for the children of Israel.
3_27_34.MSG: These are the commandments that God gave to Moses on Mount Sinai for the People of Israel.
3_27_34.NIV: These are the commands the Lord gave Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
3_27_34.NRSVCE: These are the commandments that the LORD gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
